---

§2 Per-field reality check

§2.1 reports (Pipeline B output store)

12 columns (Q1.4, Q1.5). The interesting payload is report_data JSONB. Top-level keys in a current report (Q2.2): job_id, status, version, category, bilingual, created_at, is_partial, approved_at, last_edited, report_date, source_file, total_posts, delivery_log, category_name, source_method, topic_analyses, analysis_preset, topics_analyzed, version_history, category_name_ar, executive_summary, processing_metrics, executive_summary_ar, executive_summary_en, unique_posts_by_platform.

Per-topic keys inside report_data.topic_analyses.<id> (Q2.3): urls, stats, actors, enabled, analysis, emotions, hashtags, keywords, mentions, locations, narrative, sentiment, analysis_ar, analysis_en, actors_typed, admin_edited, example_posts, visualization, analysis_sample, sentiment_trend, source_analytics, actors_structured.

Field Verdict Backing
report_data envelope Reliable — 1,146/1,146 rows valid JSONB Q1.2, Q2.2
executive_summary_en / _ar Reliable — present on every recent report Q2.2
unique_posts_by_platform top-level Reliable — present on every recent report Q2.2
topic_analyses.*.sentiment (count map) Reliable with caveat — keys are overwhelmingly English (negative 8,853 / neutral 7,559 / positive 6,580 across the entire table; only 3 rows of urgent and no Arabic keys). Compare to the 2026-04-23 baseline that documented mixed AR/EN. Q2.6, Q2.6b
topic_analyses.*.actors_structured[*].type Partial — 100 % unknown across the latest 200 reports (11,126/11,126). Worse than the 81.57 % baseline. Q2.5
topic_analyses.*.sentiment_confidence Dead — 0/1,938 topics in latest 200 reports carry the key. Producer never emits it. Q2.7
topic_analyses.*.visualization.dominant_platform Unverified in current era — Q2.8 returned no non-NULL distribution in the latest 200 reports; the field exists as a per-topic key (Q2.3) but the writer path appears not to populate it on the recent slice. The Facebook-vs-facebook casing drift the 2026-04-23 baseline cited at 84 rows is not re-derived here — that figure is carried verbatim from docs/client-data-quality-response-2026-05.md line 49 and was not recomputed against current data. Q2.8
topic_analyses.*.visualization.trend_direction Unverified in current era (same caveat as dominant_platform). Q2.8
category (top-level column) Reliable with caveat — 9.95 % of rows still use legacy slugs (general_news, government, foreign_entities, israeli_media, financial, fact_checking); 114 of 1,146 rows. Down from 18.67 % at the 2026-04-23 baseline. Q2.4
status Reliable — 1,113 approved / 33 rejected Q2.1
§2.2 raw_posts

9 columns (Q1.5): id, row_data JSONB, source_file, source_type, imported_at, posted_at, batch_id, row_hash, file_date. Indexes include (row_data->>'platform'), (row_data->>'external_url'), (row_data->>'author_username') — supports the per-day unique-posts-by-platform path without table scans.

Field Verdict Backing
Span Reliable — file_date 2026-01-10 .. 2026-05-14; imported_at 2026-02-25 .. 2026-05-18; daily volume on recent days ~30–40 k rows. Q3.1, Q3.10
source_type mix Reliable — s3 3,174,051 / smtp 12,593 / smoke_test 70 / test 48 Q3.1
row_data->>'dashboard_section' (current era) Reliable with caveat — last 5,000 rows: 100 % registry slugs (PS_Media 2,904; PS_Gaza 779; INT_Media 502; IL_Media 362; …; 8 blanks). Newly-seen since the 2026-04-23 baseline: PS_Verification (was registry-listed but absent from the prior snapshot's sample). Q3.3
row_data->>'dashboard_section' = 'INT_Verificaiton' (typo) Resolved in current ingestion — 0/200,000 in latest sample. 942 historical rows total, all prior to 2026-04-28 (peaked Apr 13 at 34/day; full daily-history series in Q3.5c). The fuzzy-filename match (Task #145) still recovers the file to the correct category at ingestion. Q3.5, Q3.5b, Q3.5c
Legacy News / Government slugs Resolved in current ingestion — 0 in latest 200,000-row sample; 12,593 historical rows table-wide. Q3.5d (and Q3.3 confirms absence in current sample)
row_data->>'platform' (current era) Reliable with caveat — last 5,000 rows: telegram 4,029 / facebook 966 / one each of blank, youtube, instagram, tiktok, twitter. No Facebook capital-F in the current era. Q3.4
row_data->>'nlp_sentiment_label' = '{}' literal Resolved in current ingestion — 0/200,000 in latest sample. The upstream serialiser fault that produced the 2026-04-23 baseline's 1,345 rows is no longer reproducing. Q3.6
row_data->>'nlp_analysis.actors' (note: flat-dot key, not nlp_actors) Reliable, contract by design — on the latest 5,000-row sample, 4,992 have the key (absent only 8) and of those 4,884 are JSON-encoded strings while 108 are JSON null literals. Mirror of upstream posts.csv schema; the client either parses the string or we add a parsed-array companion field. Q3.7, Q3.7b
row_data->>'copy_or_original' Reliable with caveat — last 5,000 rows: original 3,369 (67.4 %) / blank 1,412 (28.2 %) / copy 219 (4.4 %). Empty bucket mirrors the upstream classifier coverage gap; defensive default 'original' lives at db.py:2979, processing.py:1635. Q3.8
Top-level keys breadth Reliable — 47 distinct keys observed across a 100 k-row sample; every row carries the same upstream schema. Q3.2
§2.3 unified_reports (Pipeline C run header)

53 columns (Q1.5). The 2026-04-23 baseline predates this table — every field below is new since then.

Field Verdict Backing
Date span Reliable — 2026-05-05 .. 2026-05-14, 92 runs Q4.1
status mix Partial — 72 succeeded / 18 failed / 2 stuck running. The two stuck rows are older than the 30-min STALE_RUNNING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS stale-heuristic ceiling and surface in the admin strip as failed (stale) without being rewritten. Q4.1
Embedding coverage distribution Partial — >=95 %: 68; <50 %: 3; NULL: 21. The 21 NULLs are the failed/stuck rows that never reached the coverage probe. Q4.1
executive_summary_en/ar Partial — 9 runs carry EN, 9 carry AR, and the same 9 runs carry both (~10 %). The remainder are runs from before the synthesis stage went live or runs that failed prior to synthesis. Q4.2
run_flags.forced_low_coverage Reliable — 10/92 runs were forced under the typed-confirmation modal; single distinct signer admin@socialstudio.me, accepted floor min 95 % / max 98 % vs configured 99 %. Q4.1 (forced), Q5.7b
total_topic_count vs reportable_topic_count Reliable — totals 1,167 vs 230 across all runs. The 80 % carry-out matches the configured cap behaviour. Q4.2
§2.4 unified_topics (Pipeline C per-cluster row)

53 columns (Q1.5). The single most important table for this snapshot — both the LLM-emitted narrative fields and the deterministic Stage 9 viewpoints live here.

Aggregates over all 1,568 rows (Q4.3, Q4.4):

Field Populated Verdict
viewpoints_en 1,343 / 1,568 (85.7 %) Reliable — Stage 9 deterministic derivation reaches the field on most rows
viewpoints_ar 1,343 / 1,568 (85.7 %) Reliable — same
key_voices_en 696 / 1,568 (44.4 %) Partial — striking AR/EN asymmetry
key_voices_ar 1,396 / 1,568 (89.0 %) Reliable with caveat — Arabic side substantially more populated than English
narrative_en 386 / 1,568 (24.6 %) Partial — only ~25 % of clusters have an English narrative
narrative_ar 870 / 1,568 (55.5 %) Partial — AR/EN asymmetry persists
is_reportable=true 631 / 1,568 (40.2 %) Reliable — soft top-N cap behaving as expected
factuality_pass 230 true / 1,338 NULL / 0 false Partial — only 15 % of rows ran the post-hoc factuality check; rest are NULL because the gate runs only on rows that produced full narrative + key_voices. The fact that the count of factuality_pass non-NULL (230) matches the count of every one of the six bilingual analysis-prose fields (actors_analysis_en/ar, sentiment_analysis_en/ar, …; all exactly 230) is the strongest evidence that those Stage 7 fields land together-or-not-at-all.
Six per-domain analysis fields, EN side 230 / 1,568 (14.7 %) each Partial — every field has the same denominator; the Stage 7 LLM call either succeeds for the whole language or persists none of them
domain_routing_method 1,565 llm_fallback / 3 centroid (anchor-only) Reliable with caveat — anchor-only gate is functionally never reached; near-universal LLM fallback. This may be intentional (the gate's delta + abs floor are deliberately tight per ADR 0007) or it may indicate the anchor centroids drifted off-corpus. Flagged in §6 / §7.
is_carried_forward 522 / 1,568 (33.3 %) Reliable — Phase 1 continuity contract reaching ~1/3 of clusters day-over-day
momentum emerging 1,284 / stable 144 / rising 97 / declining 43 Reliable
§2.5 Other Pipeline C tables
Table Inventory Backing
unified_topic_centroids 350 rows; single model (text-embedding-3-small v1); spans 2026-05-07..09 (gap vs unified_reports 2026-05-05..14 worth noting — see §7) Q4.7
unified_post_cluster_assignments 91,104 assignments / 66,660 distinct post URLs; spans 2026-05-10..14 (also narrower than unified_reports span — newer table, started later) Q4.8
unified_llm_calls 802 rows; 100 % status='succeeded' across all (topic_analysis/synthesis) × (en/ar) buckets. No failed/parse_error rows in the audit trail. Q4.9
unified_ingestion_snapshots 8 snapshots, one per file_date 2026-05-05..14 (missing 06, 07; both predate the snapshot-writer rollout). All currently live (superseded_at IS NULL). Q4.10
pipeline_c_prompt_versions 9 rows / 9 distinct keys — matches the 7 per-domain prompts + 2 synthesis prompts. Q4.14
post_embeddings 503,595 rows; single model (text-embedding-3-small v1, dim 1,536); daily production peaked 2026-05-20 at 139,008 vectors and was still climbing on 2026-05-21 (43,264 partial-day). Q4.11
§2.6 Operator / admin surfaces
Surface Verdict Backing
pipeline_control_state Reliable — three rows (pipeline_c, segmented, raw_posts), all enabled=true Q5.1
admin_audit_log Reliable — 639 events. Heaviest event: admin_seed_skipped (628; auth-startup noise). Next: pipeline_c_forced_low_coverage_trigger (10 — matches the 10 forced runs in Q5.7). Q5.2
phase5_window Partial — single closed window with date range 2027-04-01..07 and closing_summary literal range test cleanup; not a real production window. Q5.3
phase5_window_archive Reliable — 930 archived rows spanning versions 29..924; the rubric harness has been heavily exercised. Q5.3
phase5_measurements Reliable — 450 rows spanning report_dates 2031-05-07..20 (synthetic dates), 14 distinct dates, 34 distinct (section, metric, pipeline) tuples. Looks like a development harness, not production capture. Flagged in §7. Q5.4
webhook_deliveries Partial — 321 attempts, 42 successes (13 %), 279 failures (87 %). HTTP status mix on failures: 251 (without status; transport failure), 503 (16; service unavailable), 413 (12; payload too large). The 251s suggest the client endpoint is intermittently unreachable; the 413s suggest a payload budget breach (see docs/webhook-payload-budget.md). Q5.5
embedding_worker_telemetry Partial — most recent cycles exit with max_batches_reached (the per-cycle batch cap); two latest cycles exited with early_return_fetch_failed. Worker is loaded and occasionally hitting transient fetch failures, but is producing 102,400 embeddings per max-batch cycle as expected. Q4.13
category_prompts Reliable — 32 rows, 30 active Q5.6
admin_users Reliable — 2 rows, both active Q5.6

§3 Surfaces → tables mapping

Surface Reads from
Webhook payload (webhook.py:_build_payload) reports.report_data (segmented runs); unified_reports+unified_topics+ derived viewpoints_* / key_voices_* (unified runs)
GET /api/client/reports/daily-summary raw_posts only (via db.get_raw_posts_platform_counts_for_date)
Streamlit admin (per-topic review) reports.report_data
FastAPI admin dashboard unified_reports, unified_topics, unified_ingestion_snapshots, unified_llm_calls, pipeline_control_state, admin_audit_log, phase5_*, webhook_deliveries, embedding_worker_telemetry, s3_scan_history
Pipeline C orchestrator reads raw_posts (via S3, not table); reads/writes post_embeddings, unified_*, pipeline_c_prompt_versions
Pipeline B orchestrator reads/writes reports, topic_diagnostics, report_clustering_summary, post_embeddings

---

§4 Visualisations → fields mapping (segmented Pipeline B reports)

These remain unchanged from the per-topic key inventory (Q2.3); the mappings to dashboard cards live in app.py and were not the focus of this resurvey. The shape is intact, the population varies:

  • Engagement cards → topic.stats, topic.engagement_* (populated)
  • Bilingual summaries → topic.analysis_en / analysis_ar (populated)
  • Keywords / hashtags / mentions → topic.keywords, hashtags, mentions (populated)
  • Locations → topic.locations (populated, no quality probe this pass)
  • Actors → topic.actors, topic.actors_structured, topic.actors_typed (see §2.1 — actors_structured.type 100 % unknown)
  • Sentiment → topic.sentiment (count map; see §2.1 — English-only)
  • Source diversity → topic.source_analytics
  • Topic velocity → topic.sentiment_trend, topic.visualization.trend_direction (population unverified — see §2.1)

Carrying the 2026-04-23 dead-field list forward against current code:

Field 2026-04-23 status 2026-05-21 status
sentiment_label (per-topic) Dead (never produced) Dead (Q2.7 mirror; producer still never emits)
sentiment_confidence (per-topic) Dead Dead (Q2.7)
Older topic.dominant_platform top-level key Dead — replaced by visualization.dominant_platform Dead (Q2.6/Q2.8 — top-level path returned NULL for every topic in the 200-report sample)

No previously-dead field has been resurrected by new producer code in this period.

§6 Feature blockers (re-derivation of prior verdicts)

§6.1 Prior fourteen findings re-derived

Sourced from docs/client-data-quality-response-2026-05.md table at lines 41–57 (the 2026-04-23 baseline's Section 6 verdict matrix). Each row is tagged Confirmed / Refined / Resolved / Withdrawn with the backing query.

# Finding 2026-04-23 verdict 2026-05-21 status Backing
1.1 actors_structured.type 81.57 % unknown Confirmed; channel fixed, data missing Confirmed — worse (100 % unknown on 11,126/11,126 actor entries across 200 latest reports) Q2.5
1.2 normalizedNameEn empty when originalName populated Confirmed; upstream gap Confirmed (unchanged) — no transliteration step added; producer still passes through whatever upstream wrote static (no producer query)
1.3 actors_structured.type 15-value vocabulary Confirmed; passthrough Refined — collapsed to a single value (unknown) on the recent slice; the 15-value vocabulary surfaced in the 2026-04-23 snapshot is not reachable on current data because every value is unknown Q2.5
1.4 raw_posts.nlp_actors 100 % string Confirmed; this is the contract Confirmed (unchanged) — on the latest 5,000-row sample, 4,992 rows have the key present and 8 are absent (Q3.7b); of the 4,992 present, 4,884 carry a JSON-encoded string and 108 are JSON null literals (Q3.7). The flat-dot key is nlp_analysis.actors (the 2026-04-23 doc abbreviated it to nlp_actors). Q3.7, Q3.7b
2.1 sentiment_label mixed Arabic / English / casing Confirmed; pipeline drift Refined — English-only era — the entire reports table currently shows only English sentiment keys (negative 8,853 / neutral 7,559 / positive 6,580) plus 3 rows of urgent. No Arabic sentiment keys reproduce on the current slice. Q2.6, Q2.6b
2.2 trend_direction 4 variants (Emerging / stable / Stable / Declining) Confirmed; two writers + historical residue Unverified in current era — the per-topic visualization.trend_direction path returned no non-NULL values on 1,938 topics across the latest 200 reports. Cannot reconfirm the casing drift on current data; the field appears in the per-topic key list but is not populated on the recent slice. Q2.8
2.3 dominant_platform Facebook vs facebook (84 rows) Confirmed; per-topic path doesn't normalise Unverified in current era (same caveat as 2.2 — visualization.dominant_platform path empty on 1,938-topic sample) Q2.8
2.4 raw_posts.nlp_sentiment_label literal '{}' (1,345 rows) Confirmed; upstream serialiser fault Resolved in current ingestion — 0/200,000 in latest sample. Historical rows likely still on disk but not reproducing on the current ingestion. Q3.6
3.1 sentiment_label (per-topic, not the raw_posts variant) — never populated Confirmed never populated Confirmed (unchanged) — producer never emits static + Q2.7 mirror
3.2 99 %-populated narrative fields not read by the client Confirmed; producer-intentional, client decision Confirmed (unchanged) — narrative fields still emitted by producer; client read-side decision is outside this DB's surface n/a (client-side)
3.3 raw_posts.copy_or_original 61.6 % empty Confirmed; upstream classifier coverage gap Refined — 28.2 % empty on the latest 5,000-row sample (vs 61.6 % at baseline). Upstream classifier coverage has materially improved. Q3.8
4.1 Platform mix (Twitter absent, etc.) Refined — telegram + facebook present in latest snapshot, Twitter still absent Refined further — latest 5,000-row sample shows telegram 4,029, facebook 966 dominant, plus one row each of youtube, instagram, tiktok, twitter. Twitter is now technically present (1 row) but still essentially absent. Q3.4
4.3 dashboard_section taxonomy drift; INT_Verificaiton typo on 94 rows Mixed — current sample clean, historical residue in client mirror Resolved in current ingestion — 0/200,000 in latest sample; 942 historical rows table-wide (not 94 — the typo was more pervasive in earlier eras than the client snapshot revealed, all dated ≤ 2026-04-28). Legacy News/Government slugs also 0 in latest sample, 12,593 table-wide. Fuzzy-filename match (Task #145) still recovers the file to the correct category. Q3.5, Q3.5b, Q3.5c, Q3.5d
4.4 daily_reports.category vs topics.category mismatch (18.67 % / 21.65 % legacy) Confirmed; different writers Refined — 9.95 % on reports.category (114/1,146 rows on legacy slugs vs 18.67 % at baseline). The daily_reports and topics tables the client cited do not exist on this DB (Q1.3) — what the prior inventory called daily_reports is reports, and what it called topics is reports.report_data->'topic_analyses'. The per-topic legacy share lives inside JSONB and was not separately re-derived this pass. Q2.4, Q1.3

Answer to the two pinned questions (Task #512 brief, "Done looks like"):

1. social_media_posts liveness — are inserts still arriving? No. Table does not exist on this DB (Q1.3) and the producer code-grep confirms zero hits for social_media_posts in the Python tree (matches the 2026-04-23 baseline finding that we have never written to this table). Task #71 is Resolved from the producer side; whatever the client sees in their mirror of this table is not produced by us. Current count: 0 inserts (table absent). 2. topics.viewpoints population — is Stage 9 reaching the field? Yes. unified_topics.viewpoints_en and _ar are each populated on 1,343/1,568 rows (85.7 %). The deterministic Stage 9 viewpoints derivation (pipeline_c_viewpoints.py) is live and reaching the database. The 14.3 % unpopulated bucket correlates with topics whose source_analytics payload had no by_section key — flagged in §7.

§6.2 New findings (Pipeline C era)
# Finding Evidence Backing
C.1 unified_topic_centroids span lags unified_reports span Centroids span 2026-05-07..09; run header spans 2026-05-05..14. Six days of runs without centroid writes implies Stage 7 continuity inheritance (topic_stable_id carry) is operating off stale centroids on the most recent days. Q4.1, Q4.7
C.2 unified_post_cluster_assignments started 2026-05-10 Assignments span 2026-05-10..14, missing the first five days of unified_reports runs. The per-post drill-through (ADR 0013) is unavailable for those days. Q4.8
C.3 Six per-domain analysis fields land together-or-not-at-all Each of actors_analysis_en, sentiment_analysis_en, geographic_analysis_en, temporal_analysis_en, platform_analysis_en, source_analysis_en, sentiment_summary_en is populated on exactly 230 of 1,568 rows. Same denominator on every field means the Stage 7 LLM call either succeeds for the whole payload or persists none of them. Q4.4
C.4 Striking key_voices AR/EN asymmetry key_voices_ar: 1,396/1,568 (89.0 %). key_voices_en: 696/1,568 (44.4 %). 700 topics carry Arabic key voices but not English. Q4.3
C.5 Anchor-only routing essentially never fires 1,565/1,568 topics routed via llm_fallback; only 3 topics matched the anchor gate (centroid). Either intentional (tight ADR-0007 thresholds) or a calibration drift; worth a separate measurement pass. Q4.5
C.6 unified_llm_calls.status is 100 % succeeded across 802 calls All four buckets (topic_analysis×{en,ar}, synthesis×{en,ar}) are succeeded. Either the audit table is only updated on the success path (so failed/parse_error never lands), or the run window genuinely had zero LLM failures. The dispatcher's record_call in pipeline_c_dispatch.py:407–429 does pass status="parse_error" / "failed" on those branches; so the 100 % rate suggests the runs in this window simply did not exercise those paths. Worth confirming on a broader window. Q4.9
C.7 Webhook delivery success rate is 13 % 42/321 attempts succeeded. Failure status mix is 251 (no HTTP status — transport failure) / 503 / 413. The 13 % rate is the headline operational risk in this snapshot. Q5.5
C.8 Two unified_reports.status='running' rows are stale Older than the 30-min STALE_RUNNING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS; admin strip shows them as failed (stale) without rewriting the row. The orchestrator's "next successful run rewrites the latest state" contract leaves these rows on disk indefinitely. Q4.1
C.9 actors_structured.type regression vs the 2026-04-23 baseline 100 % unknown (11,126/11,126 entries across 200 latest reports), worse than the 81.57 % the client reported. Either the upstream NER stopped emitting type at all, or _aggregate_actors_typed lost the channel between snapshots. This is a quality regression and the highest-leverage Section 6 / §7 action item. Q2.5
C.10 phase5_window carries a single closed test window Range 2027-04-01..07 with closing_summary='range test cleanup'; not a real production window. The 930 phase5_window_archive rows tell the real history. Q5.3

---

§7 Open questions

Carrying forward 2026-04-23 questions that remain open, plus new ones this pass surfaced. No remediation tasks proposed here — per the Task #512 brief these are notes, not work items.

1. Why are the unified_topic_centroids writes lagging six days behind the run window? Are recent runs persisting centroids under a different model_id, or is the centroid upsert in Stage 7 silently skipping when the daily run completes degraded? (Q4.7 vs Q4.1) 2. Why is anchor-only routing essentially never reached (3/1,568 topics)? Are the ADR-0007 thresholds (LLM_FALLBACK_DELTA=0.05, LLM_FALLBACK_ABS_FLOOR=0.45) tighter than the corpus warrants, or have the anchor centroids drifted off-corpus? (Q4.5) 3. Why is key_voices_en populated on only 44 % of topics while _ar lands on 89 %? The single Stage 7 LLM call emits both languages from the same per-language dispatch; the asymmetry suggests either an English-side parse failure path that doesn't propagate to the AR side, or the _normalise_key_voices recovery path (pipeline_c_dispatch.py:137–238) is fail-softing more aggressively on English shapes. (Q4.3) 4. Why are the per-topic visualization.dominant_platform and visualization.trend_direction paths empty on the recent 200 reports? The 2026-04-23 baseline found 84 rows of Facebook casing drift on this exact path. Has the writer been retired, or has the population path moved? (Q2.8) 5. Is unified_llm_calls.status ever non-succeeded in practice? 100 % succeeded across 802 calls is suspicious given the per-language dispatcher explicitly distinguishes failed/parse_error statuses (pipeline_c_dispatch.py:407–429). Either the runs in this window genuinely all succeeded, or the audit writer is dropping the non-succeeded path. (Q4.9) 6. Is the 13 % webhook success rate a current outage or a baseline? The 251 transport failures suggest the client endpoint is the problem, but the 413 payload-too-large failures (12) are on our side. (Q5.5) 7. What writes the 230 factuality_pass=true rows but never factuality_pass=false? The deterministic post-hoc grounding check (pipeline_c_factuality) is supposed to set both true and false outcomes; zero false outcomes across 230 graded rows is either a pass-rate of 100 % or a writer that only persists the pass path. (Q4.3) 8. actors_structured.type regressed to 100 % unknown since the 2026-04-23 baseline. Investigation needed at the upstream NER boundary vs the _aggregate_actors_typed aggregation (processing.py:1789). (Q2.5; finding C.9) 9. phase5_measurements carries 2031-dated synthetic rows — confirm this is a test harness and either label it (env-tag the rows) or sweep it. (Q5.4) 10. Two unified_reports.status='running' rows are stale — confirm the operational contract is "leave them on disk, admin strip masks them" rather than "recovery sweep should rewrite to failed". (Q4.1; finding C.8)

Resolved from the 2026-04-23 question list: * ~~Is social_media_posts still being written?~~ — No; table absent and producer-grep zero. Resolved. * ~~Is Stage 9 viewpoints derivation reaching the persisted table?~~ — Yes; 85.7 % reach on unified_topics.viewpoints_{en,ar}. Resolved.

---

Appendix A — Field-level evidence tables

Aggregations consolidated above; the SQL log (.local/docs/data-inventory-queries.sql) carries every query in runnable form. To reproduce any number in this document: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -f .local/docs/data-inventory-queries.sql and locate the labeled block (Q#.#) referenced from the relevant table cell.

Changelog

2026-05-21 — full regeneration (Task #512)

* No on-disk predecessor. The prior 2026-04-23 docs/data-inventory.md and its query log are not present in git history; the baseline used for the §6 re-derivation is the fourteen-finding verdict table inside docs/client-data-quality-response-2026-05.md (the artifact that came out of the same 2026-04-23 audit Task #268 cycle). Sources consulted (read-only): db.py (table-creation SQL — schema source of truth for a project that has no shared/schema.ts) api.py (admin / client endpoint surface; STREAMLIT_ADMIN_URL substitution; FastAPI HTML dashboard) webhook.py (client payload contract) processing.py (Pipeline B writers; per-topic shape; LLM_CALL_REGISTRY) pipeline_c_processing.py (Pipeline C orchestrator; embedding-gate residual semantics; finalisation status rule) pipeline_c_dispatch.py (Stage 7 per-language LLM dispatch; _normalise_key_voices shape recovery) pipeline_c_viewpoints.py (Stage 9 deterministic viewpoints — confirms producer reach for question #2) pipeline_c_reportable.py (reportable-cap selector — confirms 631/1,568 reportable share) docs/client-data-quality-response-2026-05.md (baseline verdict table for §6) * docs/adr/0005..0016 (referenced in surface-mapping notes) Section-by-section diff vs the 2026-04-23 baseline (re-derived from the client-data-quality-response artifact): §1 — added Pipeline C tables (unified_*, pipeline_c_prompt_versions, pipeline_control_state, etc.); explicit Q1.3 confirms topics/daily_reports/weekly_reports/social_media_posts/custom_reports do not exist on this DB. Row counts updated. §2 — per-field reality check updated for every watchlist field; new subsections (§2.3 / §2.4 / §2.5 / §2.6) for Pipeline C tables and operator surfaces that did not exist in the baseline. §3 — surface→table mapping updated for the dual-admin architecture (Streamlit:5000 + FastAPI:8000) and Pipeline C reads. §4 — no behavioural change; populations updated to the current era. §5 — dead-field list unchanged from 2026-04-23; no fields resurrected, no live fields demoted. §6 — all fourteen prior findings tagged Confirmed / Refined / Resolved with backing queries. Ten new Pipeline-C-era findings (C.1–C.10) added. §7 — two prior questions resolved (social_media_posts and viewpoints reach); ten new open questions raised, most Pipeline-C-shaped.

17 / 28 · Data Inventory Source: data-inventory.md · Task #512 · 2026-05-21 regeneration · supersedes 2026-04-22/23 versions
18 / Data & Quality

LLM Prompts.

Single reference covering both analytical pipelines that ship in this codebase. Pipeline B is the legacy segmented per-category pipeline (§2). Pipeline C is the unified whole-day clustering pipeline (§3). The two pipelines are independent — Pipeline B reads its prompts from category_prompts rows and seed_presets.py; Pipeline C reads its prompts from pipeline_c_prompts.py. Both pass through the same call_llm wrapper in processing.py. v2.0 / 2026-05-21 — renamed from docs/llm_analysis_prompts.md (Pipeline-B-only, 2026-04-30) → docs/llm-prompts-reference.md (this document); supersedes the prior. Source: llm-prompts-reference.md.

Date written: 2026-05-21 · Source revision: working copy at time of writing · Status: Documentation only; no code was changed · Supersedes: docs/llm_analysis_prompts.md (Pipeline-B-only, 2026-04-30).

How to use this document. It is a single reference covering both analytical pipelines that ship in this codebase. Pipeline B is the legacy segmented per-category pipeline (§2). Pipeline C is the unified whole-day clustering pipeline (§3).

Status caveat — Pipeline C prompts are starter drafts

The eight Phase 2b domain prompts (§3.2) and the Phase 2c synthesis prompt (§3.3) reproduced verbatim below are Replit-authored starter drafts pending an analyst-revision pass (see §01 System Reference §3.2 for the standing rule). They are the production text Stage 7 and Stage 9 feed at runtime, but they have not been through the domain-analyst revision pass described in docs/prompt-authorship-guide.md. ADR 0008 §11 tracks consolidated-skeleton elements the drafts replicate vs. omit; pipeline_c_prompt_skeleton_check.py is the per-(prompt, language) pattern-match CLI surfacing the gap.

§1 — Unified call-site map

All LLM traffic in the project — Pipeline B and Pipeline C alike — routes through the single call_llm wrapper in processing.py. Every production call site registers a call_type in LLM_CALL_REGISTRY; the inventory below is sourced directly from that registry.

1.1 Pipeline B call sites (legacy segmented pipeline)

call_typeTriggerSource functionModelJSON mode
topic_analysisPrimary per-topic structured analysisgenerate_topic_analysis → generate_for_language; reanalyze_single_topic → generate_for_languagegpt-4.1-mini✅
topic_analysis_retryJSON-parse fallback OR language-mixing retrySame generate_for_language retry branchesgpt-4.1-mini✅
executive_summaryPer-language report executive summarygenerate_overall_summary → generate_summary_for_languagegpt-4.1❌ (plain text)

1.2 Pipeline C call sites (unified whole-day clustering pipeline)

call_typePhase / StageFunctionModelPurpose
domain_routing_fallbackPhase 2a (routing)pipeline_c_processing._classify_with_llm_fallbackgpt-4.1-miniPick one of seven domains for a cluster that failed the anchor-similarity gate
pipeline_c_topic_analysisPhase 2b · Stage 7pipeline_c_dispatch.run_topic_analysis_for_run; pipeline_c_prompt_eval._call_llm_for_topic (validation CLI)gpt-4.1-miniPer-topic structured analysis using the eight Phase 2b domain prompts, one call per (topic, language)
pipeline_c_daily_synthesisPhase 2c · Stage 9pipeline_c_synthesis.run_daily_synthesis_for_rungpt-4.1-miniDay-level executive synthesis from the Stage 7 briefs, one call per language
(deterministic — no LLM)Stage 9 viewpointspipeline_c_viewpoints.render_viewpoints—Per-topic viewpoints_{ar,en} rendered from per-section analytics

1.3 call_llm wrapper

Source: processing.py — call_llm (~line 1434, body ~60 lines).

All call sites in §1.1 and §1.2 pass through this single function. It selects the model by call_type from LLM_CALL_REGISTRY, applies response_format={'type':'json_object'} when use_json=True, fixes max_completion_tokens=8192, logs each call to the job logger (including any reasoning_tokens reported by the o-series / gpt-5 families), and retries on transient API errors up to 4 attempts via tenacity. Unknown call_type values fall through to _fallback_fast / _fallback_full whose models are derived at module load time from topic_analysis_retry / executive_summary (see §5).

§2 — Pipeline B prompt surface (legacy segmented)

Pipeline B is the legacy segmented pipeline: one analytical run per category CSV. Its prompts are assembled at runtime from preset rows, category rows, and hardcoded templates in processing.py. This section is preserved from the 2026-04-30 reference document and renumbered to nest under §2; the subsections below are drift-checked against the current source tree as of 2026-05-21. See §8 for the diff summary.

2.1 Assembly diagrams per API call

Each Pipeline B LLM call assembles its final prompt from up to five layers, applied in order: (1) system-level prompt header → (2) preset framework (if any) → (3) category-specific prompt → (4) hardcoded topic-prompt template (per-call shape) → (5) conditional overlays. The four per-call-type diagrams are reproduced verbatim below.

2.1.1 `topic_analysis` — primary call
system message └── build_preset_system_prompt(preset, lang) ├── preset['system_prompt'] (from DB row / seed_presets.py) └── + "\n\nيجب أن يكون كل الإخراج باللغة العربية فقط." [Arabic] or + "\n\nAll output MUST be in English only." [English] └── (falls back to call_llm default if no preset) user message └── category_context (§5, §7.6) ├── custom_prompt path: │ "\n\n=== CATEGORY-SPECIFIC INSTRUCTIONS ===\n" │ + custom_prompt │ + "\n=== END CATEGORY INSTRUCTIONS ===\n\n" └── DB default path: "\n\n" + get_category_prompt(category, lang) + "\n\n" └── _safe_format(prompts['topic_prompt'], **kwargs) (§6.1 / §6.2) ├── Arabic or English topic prompt skeleton (get_analysis_prompts) ├── {analysis_framework} slot filled by: │ ├── preset['analysis_framework_ar/en'] (§4, if non-empty) │ ├── or default framework block (§6.3) │ └── + coherence overlay (§7.1, if SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE) ├── {emotion_context} slot (§7.2, if ENABLE_ENRICHED_SIGNALS) └── {faction_context} slot (§7.3, if ENABLE_ENRICHED_SIGNALS)
2.1.2 `topic_analysis_retry` — JSON-parse fallback
system message (identical to 2.1) user message (identical to 2.1 — same prompt object re-sent unchanged) Difference from 2.1: call_type = "topic_analysis_retry" fast = True
2.1.3 `topic_analysis_retry` — language-mixing retry
system message (identical to 2.1) user message └── [everything from 2.1] └── + "\n\nIMPORTANT: Your previous response contained Arabic text. Please respond ONLY in English this time." (§7.4 — English side only)
2.1.4 `executive_summary`

---

system message └── build_preset_system_prompt(preset, lang) (same logic as 2.1) or call_llm default if no preset user message └── Arabic header: "أنت محلل إعلامي رفيع المستوى..." (§6.5) or English: "You are a senior media analyst..." (§6.6) └── "=== سياق التقرير ===" / "=== REPORT CONTEXT ===" + category_instruction (_load_category_context → context_ar/en, §5) + "=== نهاية السياق ===" / "=== END CONTEXT ===" └── topic list JSON (topics_summary, a list of {topic, title, brief, post_count, engagement}) └── total post count └── summary_framework block: ├── preset['executive_summary_prompt_ar/en'] (§4, if non-empty) └── or default_summary_framework (§6.4 Arabic / §6.5 English)

2.2 System-level prompts

2.2.1 build_preset_system_prompt behaviour

Source: processing.py:501–509

def build_preset_system_prompt(preset, lang):
    if not preset or not preset.get('system_prompt'):
        return None
    prompt = preset['system_prompt']
    if lang == 'arabic':
        prompt += "\n\nيجب أن يكون كل الإخراج باللغة العربية فقط."
    else:
        prompt += "\n\nAll output MUST be in English only."
    return prompt

Arabic language enforcement line (appended verbatim):

يجب أن يكون كل الإخراج باللغة العربية فقط.

English language enforcement line (appended verbatim):

All output MUST be in English only.
2.2.2 Fallback when no preset is supplied

When build_preset_system_prompt returns None, the caller omits the system_prompt kwarg and call_llm uses its default argument:

Source: processing.py:818

You are an expert analyst specializing in Palestinian social media trends and Middle Eastern affairs. Always respond in the same language as the content provided (Arabic or English).

This default is also used for the executive_summary call when no preset is provided.

> Cross-reference: See §8 for worked examples showing how the system prompt is assembled per call type.

---

2.3 Preset prompts (four named personas)

Source file: seed_presets.py, BUILTIN_PRESETS list starting at line 4.

All four presets are seeded once at startup via seed_presets. Admins may create additional presets via the UI (stored as DB rows); those are not quoted here — this section covers only the built-in seeded defaults.

> Cross-reference: See §8 for worked examples showing how preset fields combine with category prompts and templates.

---

2.3.1 Preset: "Factual Reporter"

Key / persona type: factual_reporter Default? ✅ Yes (is_default: True) Source: seed_presets.py:5–62

system_prompt (non-empty):

You are a factual news reporter. Your role is to report exactly what was said, by whom, and when — without adding interpretation, opinion, or editorial framing. Never speculate, editorialize, or draw conclusions beyond what the data shows. Attribute every claim to its source. Use neutral, objective language throughout.

analysis_framework_ar (non-empty):

=== إطار التحليل المطلوب ===

❌ لا تفعل:
- لا تضف أي تفسير أو رأي أو تحليل شخصي
- لا تستخدم لغة عاطفية أو تقييمية (مثل "مثير للقلق"، "ملفت"، "خطير")
- لا تحلل دوافع المصادر أو تتكهن بنواياهم
- لا تربط بين أحداث لم تربطها المصادر نفسها
- لا تقدم استنتاجات أو توصيات
- لا تصف ما شهده الموضوع من تفاعل

✅ افعل:
- أبلغ بدقة عمّا قاله كل مصدر بالاسم (مثال: "نشر حساب [الاسم] أن...")
- اقتبس مباشرة من المنشورات مع ذكر المصدر
- أبلغ عن الحقائق الموضوعية فقط: من قال ماذا، متى، وأين
- إذا وجدت ادعاءات متناقضة، اعرض الادعاءين بحيادية دون تفضيل أحدهما
- استخدم لغة محايدة وموضوعية تماماً
- اعرض المعلومات بترتيب زمني أو حسب الأهمية الموضوعية

analysis_framework_en (non-empty):

=== ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK ===

❌ DO NOT:
- Add any interpretation, opinion, or personal analysis
- Use emotional or evaluative language (e.g., "alarming", "notable", "dangerous", "significant")
- Analyze motivations of sources or speculate about their intentions
- Draw connections between events that the sources themselves did not make
- Provide conclusions or recommendations
- Describe levels of engagement or discussion intensity

✅ DO:
- Report accurately what each source said BY NAME (e.g., "The account [Name] posted that...")
- Quote directly from posts with source attribution
- Report only objective facts: who said what, when, and where
- If contradictory claims exist, present both sides neutrally without favoring either
- Use completely neutral and objective language
- Present information in chronological order or by topical relevance

executive_summary_prompt_ar (non-empty):

اكتب ملخصاً إخبارياً حيادياً من 4-5 فقرات:
- الفقرة 1: ما أبرز الأحداث التي تم الإبلاغ عنها اليوم؟ من أبلغ عنها؟
- الفقرة 2: ما الادعاءات والتصريحات الرئيسية ومن أصدرها؟
- الفقرة 3: هل وردت ادعاءات متناقضة؟ اعرضها بحيادية
- الفقرة 4: ما المعلومات الجغرافية والزمنية المرتبطة بالأحداث؟
- الفقرة 5: ملخص موجز للوقائع الأساسية

اكتب بأسلوب إخباري حيادي صرف. لا تضف تفسيراً أو تقييماً.

executive_summary_prompt_en (non-empty):

Write a neutral news-style summary of 4-5 paragraphs:
- Para 1: What are the main events reported today? Who reported them?
- Para 2: What are the key claims and statements, and who made them?
- Para 3: Were there contradictory claims? Present them neutrally
- Para 4: What geographic and temporal information is associated with the events?
- Para 5: Brief summary of core facts

Write in a strictly neutral news reporting style. Do not add interpretation or evaluation.

---

2.3.2 Preset: "Media Monitor"

Key / persona type: media_monitor Default? ❌ No Source: seed_presets.py:63–116

system_prompt (non-empty):

You are a professional media monitoring specialist. Your role is to track and report on media discourse patterns, trends, and information flows. Report what is being discussed, by whom, on which platforms, and how the conversation is evolving. Stay neutral and descriptive. Do not editorialize or inject personal analysis. Focus on observable patterns in the data.

analysis_framework_ar (non-empty):

=== إطار التحليل المطلوب ===

❌ لا تفعل:
- لا تقدم آراءك أو تقييماتك الشخصية
- لا تستخدم لغة تقييمية (مثل "مثير للقلق"، "إيجابي"، "سلبي") إلا عند نقل تحليل المشاعر من البيانات
- لا تقدم توصيات أو تتكهن بما سيحدث
- لا تكرر الإحصائيات بشكل سردي

✅ افعل:
- وثّق الروايات الرئيسية المنتشرة وحدد مصادرها بالاسم
- أبلغ عن أنماط الانتشار: أي المنصات، أي أنواع الحسابات، وأي فترات زمنية
- وثّق اقتباسات مباشرة من المصادر الرئيسية مع ذكر أسمائهم
- حدد الرسائل الأكثر تداولاً والمصادر الأكثر تأثيراً
- صِف كيف تطور النقاش بمرور الوقت بناءً على البيانات
- أبلغ عن توزيع المشاعر كما يظهر في البيانات

analysis_framework_en (non-empty):

=== ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK ===

❌ DO NOT:
- Offer your own opinions or personal assessments
- Use evaluative language (e.g., "alarming", "positive", "negative") except when relaying sentiment data
- Provide recommendations or speculate about future developments
- Repeat statistics in narrative form

✅ DO:
- Document the main narratives circulating and identify their sources BY NAME
- Report on distribution patterns: which platforms, which account types, which time periods
- Document direct quotes from key sources with their names
- Identify the most shared messages and the most influential sources
- Describe how the discussion evolved over time based on the data
- Report sentiment distribution as it appears in the data

executive_summary_prompt_ar (non-empty):

اكتب ملخص رصد إعلامي من 4-5 فقرات:
- الفقرة 1: ما أبرز القضايا التي شغلت الخطاب الإعلامي اليوم؟
- الفقرة 2: ما الروايات الرئيسية المنتشرة ومن هي مصادرها؟
- الفقرة 3: كيف توزع النقاش عبر المنصات وأنواع الحسابات؟
- الفقرة 4: ما أنماط الانتشار الملفتة؟ ما الهاشتاغات والرسائل الأكثر تداولاً؟
- الفقرة 5: كيف تطور النقاش خلال اليوم؟

اكتب بأسلوب رصد إعلامي مهني ومحايد.

executive_summary_prompt_en (non-empty):

Write a media monitoring summary of 4-5 paragraphs:
- Para 1: What were the main issues dominating media discourse today?
- Para 2: What are the main narratives circulating and who are their sources?
- Para 3: How was the discussion distributed across platforms and account types?
- Para 4: What are the notable distribution patterns? What hashtags and messages were most shared?
- Para 5: How did the discussion evolve throughout the day?

Write in a professional, neutral media monitoring style.

---

2.3.3 Preset: "Analyst"

Key / persona type: analyst Default? ❌ No Source: seed_presets.py:117–172

system_prompt (non-empty):

You are an expert analyst specializing in Palestinian social media trends and Middle Eastern affairs. Always respond in the same language as the content provided (Arabic or English).

analysis_framework_ar (non-empty):

=== إطار التحليل المطلوب ===

❌ لا تفعل:
- لا تكرر الإحصائيات أو تصف عدد المنشورات
- لا تستخدم عبارات مثل "شهد الموضوع تفاعلاً واسعاً" أو "حظي باهتمام كبير"
- لا تصف البيانات بل حللها
- لا تكتب ملخصات عامة فارغة من المضمون
- لا تقل "أحد المصادر" أو "بعض الحسابات" - سمِّ المصدر بالاسم

✅ افعل:
- حدد الروايات المتنافسة والادعاءات الرئيسية في كل رواية
- اذكر المصادر بأسمائها الفعلية عند عرض وجهات النظر المختلفة (مثال: "قال حساب وزارة الصحة..." أو "نشر الصحفي أحمد...")
- اقتبس مباشرة من المنشورات مع ذكر اسم المصدر ونوعه
- حدد نقاط التوتر والخلاف بين الأطراف المسماة
- اربط بين الأحداث والتصريحات وردود الفعل مع تحديد من قال ماذا
- حلل الفجوات: ما الذي لم يُذكر؟ من الغائب عن النقاش؟

analysis_framework_en (non-empty):

=== ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK ===

❌ DO NOT:
- Repeat statistics or describe post counts
- Use phrases like "the topic saw widespread discussion" or "garnered significant attention"
- Describe the data - analyze it
- Write generic summaries devoid of substance
- Say "one source" or "some accounts" - NAME the source explicitly

✅ DO:
- Identify competing narratives and the key claims within each
- NAME sources explicitly when presenting different perspectives (e.g., "The Ministry of Health account stated..." or "Journalist Ahmed posted...")
- Quote directly from posts WITH the source name and type
- Identify points of tension and disagreement between NAMED parties
- Connect events, statements, and reactions while specifying who said what
- Analyze gaps: What wasn't mentioned? Who is absent from the discussion?

executive_summary_prompt_ar (non-empty):

اكتب ملخصاً تنفيذياً تحليلياً من 4-5 فقرات:
- الفقرة 1: القصة الرئيسية - ما أهم حدث أو تطور اليوم وما أبعاده؟
- الفقرة 2: الروايات المتنافسة - من يقول ماذا ولماذا؟ ما نقاط الخلاف؟
- الفقرة 3: المشاعر والمخاوف - ما الذي يقلق الناس؟ ما مصادر الغضب أو الأمل؟
- الفقرة 4: الأنماط الملفتة - ما الجديد؟ ما الذي تغير؟ ما الغائب؟
- الفقرة 5: الخلاصة للقيادة - ما الذي يجب الانتباه له؟ ما المخاطر والفرص؟

اكتب بأسلوب تحليلي مباشر وليس وصفياً.

executive_summary_prompt_en (non-empty):

Write an analytical executive summary of 4-5 paragraphs:
- Para 1: The main story - What is the most important event or development today and what are its implications?
- Para 2: Competing narratives - Who says what and why? What are the points of contention?
- Para 3: Sentiments and concerns - What worries people? What are the sources of anger or hope?
- Para 4: Notable patterns - What's new? What changed? What's missing?
- Para 5: Takeaways for leadership - What should be watched? What are the risks and opportunities?

Write in direct analytical style, not descriptive.

---

2.3.4 Preset: "Political Analyst"

Key / persona type: political_analyst Default? ❌ No Source: seed_presets.py:173–230

system_prompt (non-empty):

You are a senior political analyst specializing in Palestinian politics, regional geopolitics, and strategic communications. Your analysis examines power dynamics, political positioning, factional interests, and diplomatic implications. Attribute all claims to named sources. Ground your analysis in the actual content provided.

analysis_framework_ar (non-empty):

=== إطار التحليل المطلوب ===

❌ لا تفعل:
- لا تكرر الإحصائيات أو تصف حجم التفاعل
- لا تكتب ملخصات عامة بدون مضمون سياسي
- لا تتجاهل السياق السياسي والجيوسياسي
- لا تقل "أحد المصادر" - سمِّ المصدر بالاسم

✅ افعل:
- حلل المواقف السياسية للأطراف المسماة والانتماءات الفصائلية
- حدد أنماط التواصل الاستراتيجي: من يوجه رسائل لمن ولماذا؟
- اربط الخطاب بالسياق السياسي الأوسع: انتخابات، مفاوضات، تحالفات
- حدد التحولات في المواقف السياسية بالأسماء والاقتباسات
- حلل ديناميكيات القوة: من يتحكم في الرواية ومن يتحداها؟
- اقتبس من المصادر بأسمائها الفعلية مع تحديد انتماءاتها

analysis_framework_en (non-empty):

=== ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK ===

❌ DO NOT:
- Repeat statistics or describe engagement levels
- Write generic summaries without political substance
- Ignore the political and geopolitical context
- Say "one source" - NAME the source explicitly

✅ DO:
- Analyze the political positions of NAMED parties and factional affiliations
- Identify strategic communication patterns: who is messaging whom and why?
- Connect discourse to the broader political context: elections, negotiations, alliances
- Identify shifts in political positions with names and quotes
- Analyze power dynamics: who controls the narrative and who challenges it?
- Quote sources by their actual names with their affiliations identified

executive_summary_prompt_ar (non-empty):

اكتب ملخصاً سياسياً استراتيجياً من 4-5 فقرات:
- الفقرة 1: الحدث السياسي الأبرز - ما القضية المركزية وما أبعادها السياسية؟
- الفقرة 2: خريطة المواقف - من يقف أين؟ ما التحالفات والخلافات الظاهرة؟
- الفقرة 3: الرسائل الاستراتيجية - من يوجه رسائل لمن؟ ما الأهداف السياسية الظاهرة؟
- الفقرة 4: التحولات السياسية - هل تغيرت مواقف أطراف معينة؟ ما الجديد في المشهد؟
- الفقرة 5: التداعيات الاستراتيجية - ما المسارات المحتملة؟ ما التأثير على ميزان القوى؟

اكتب بأسلوب تحليلي سياسي معمق.

executive_summary_prompt_en (non-empty):

Write a strategic political summary of 4-5 paragraphs:
- Para 1: The main political event - What is the central issue and what are its political dimensions?
- Para 2: Position mapping - Who stands where? What alliances and disagreements are visible?
- Para 3: Strategic messaging - Who is messaging whom? What are the apparent political objectives?
- Para 4: Political shifts - Have specific parties changed positions? What's new in the landscape?
- Para 5: Strategic implications - What are the possible trajectories? What is the impact on the balance of power?

Write in deep political analytical style.

---

2.4 Category prompts (twenty-three production + nine deactivated legacy)

Source file: seed_categories.py, BUILTIN_CATEGORIES list starting at line 13.

For every category, the table below shows which runtime functions consume which fields:

Field Runtime reader Used in Call type
analytical_prompt_ar / analytical_prompt_en get_category_prompt(category, language) (processing.py:2548) Topic prompt (category_context block) topic_analysis / topic_analysis_retry
default_prompt s3_automation.py:783 — read as analysis_prompt = cat_info.get('default_prompt', '') then passed as custom_prompt=analysis_prompt to process_csv_data() (s3_automation.py:815). This means it is wrapped with the === CATEGORY-SPECIFIC INSTRUCTIONS === block (§7.6) before being sent through the standard topic_analysis call path. NOT read by get_category_prompt() in processing.py. S3 batch pipeline (via custom_prompt code path) topic_analysis (S3-triggered)
context_ar / context_en _load_category_context(category) (processing.py:3280) Executive summary (category_instruction block) executive_summary

The analytical_prompt_* fields are wrapped (when using the DB-default path) as a bare string prepended to the topic prompt with a surrounding "\n\n" on each side. When using custom_prompt (admin override), the wrapper is === CATEGORY-SPECIFIC INSTRUCTIONS === … === END CATEGORY INSTRUCTIONS === (see §7.6).

> Cross-reference: See §8 for worked examples showing how category prompts assemble into real API calls.

> Note on user-edited DB rows: Admins can override any category's prompt fields via the UI. Such overrides shadow the seeded defaults at runtime. This document quotes only the seeded built-in values; the override mechanism exists but the custom text is not quoted here.

---

2.4.1 PS_Verification — Fact-Checking Sources

Source: seed_categories.py:15–74

default_prompt:

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with expertise in misinformation detection and information integrity in the Palestinian context. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian fact-checking and verification source posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Document each specific claim being fact-checked with the verdict and supporting evidence
- Categorize types of misinformation detected (fabrication, manipulation, out-of-context, etc.)
- Trace the origins and dissemination patterns of false information within Palestinian media
- Evaluate the strength and methodology of evidence used to debunk claims
- Assess the potential impact of each misinformation case on Palestinian public discourse

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
For each fact-checked story, present:
- The original claim and its source/spread
- The fact-checking verdict with confidence level
- The specific evidence that supports or refutes the claim
- The methodology used for verification
- Any remaining ambiguities or contested elements

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Quote the specific evidence and sources cited in fact-check posts
- Never add your own fact-checking beyond what is documented in the data
- Distinguish between conclusive debunks and ongoing investigations

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية لمصادر التحقق ===
- ما أبرز المعلومات المضللة المنتشرة؟
- من مصدر هذه المعلومات المضللة؟
- ما الأدلة المقدمة للتصحيح؟
- ما سرعة انتشار المعلومات المضللة مقارنة بالتصحيحات؟
- ما الأنماط المتكررة في المعلومات المضللة؟
- من الجهات الأكثر نشاطاً في التصحيح؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Fact-Checking & Verification Analytical Questions ===
- What prominent misinformation is spreading?
- Who is the source of this misinformation?
- What evidence is provided for corrections?
- How fast does misinformation spread compared to corrections?
- What recurring patterns exist in misinformation?
- Which entities are most active in corrections?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من مصادر التحقق من الحقائق الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما المعلومات المضللة الأكثر انتشاراً؟ من مصدرها؟
- ما الأدلة المقدمة للتصحيح؟
- كيف يتم تأطير التصحيحات؟
- ما القصص التي لم يتم التحقق منها رغم أهميتها؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian fact-checking and verification sources.
Required analytical questions:
- What misinformation is most prevalent? Who is the source?
- What evidence is provided for corrections?
- How are corrections framed?
- What stories haven't been fact-checked despite their importance?

---

2.4.2 PS_Financial — Financial & Economic Actors

Source: seed_categories.py:75–132

default_prompt:

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with expertise in financial communications and economic affairs in Palestine. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian financial and economic actor posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Examine bank marketing campaigns, product announcements, and promotional messaging
- Document welfare activist communications, demands, and advocacy framing
- Analyze financial news coverage of economic developments and market conditions
- Clearly distinguish between commercial/promotional content and activism/advocacy
- Identify citizen economic concerns, challenges, and public sentiment on financial matters

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the financial discourse landscape with precision:
- Separate institutional banking communications from grassroots economic activism
- Present welfare activist demands and grievances with their stated justifications
- Articulate economic news objectively, noting multiple interpretations when present
- Identify tensions between commercial interests and public welfare concerns
- Map the spectrum from official economic messaging to citizen-level economic discourse

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific institutions, campaigns, and statements from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية للمصادر المالية والاقتصادية ===
- ما الاتجاهات الاقتصادية الرئيسية؟
- ما تأثير الوضع السياسي على الاقتصاد؟
- من المستفيدون والمتضررون؟
- ما القطاعات الأكثر تأثراً؟
- ما التوقعات والتحذيرات الاقتصادية؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Financial & Economic Actors Analytical Questions ===
- What are the main economic trends?
- How does the political situation affect the economy?
- Who benefits and who is harmed?
- Which sectors are most affected?
- What economic forecasts and warnings exist?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من القطاع المالي والاقتصادي في فلسطين.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما التحديات الاقتصادية التي تُناقش؟
- كيف تُقدم المؤسسات المالية نفسها للجمهور؟
- ما الخدمات والمبادرات المروج لها؟
- هل هناك قلق عام حول الوضع المالي يظهر في النقاش؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian financial and economic actors.
Required analytical questions:
- What economic challenges are being discussed?
- How do financial institutions present themselves to the public?
- What services and initiatives are being promoted?
- Is there public concern about the financial situation visible in the discussion?

---

2.4.3 PS_Government — Government & Official Institutions

Source: seed_categories.py:133–190

default_prompt:

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in Palestinian affairs. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE official Palestinian Government and institutional social media posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Examine official government announcements, policy communications, and citizen-facing messaging
- Assess the credibility, tone, and strategic intent behind each official statement
- Identify key governmental priorities and recurring messaging themes
- Evaluate how different government entities may present complementary or divergent positions
- Analyze the potential impact and public reception of official communications

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
When multiple perspectives exist within the data, present each narrative clearly and fairly. Distinguish between:
- Official policy positions vs. ministerial commentary
- Formal announcements vs. reactive communications
- Unified government messaging vs. departmental variations

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific posts, quotes, and statistics from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية خاصة بالمصادر الحكومية ===
- ما هي الرسائل الرئيسية التي تحاول الجهات الرسمية إيصالها؟
- ما الفجوة بين الخطاب الرسمي والواقع المُعاش؟
- ما المواضيع التي تتجنب الجهات الرسمية الحديث عنها؟
- هل هناك تناقضات بين تصريحات مختلف المسؤولين؟
- كيف يتم تأطير الأزمات والمشاكل؟ من يُلام؟
- ما الوعود التي تُقدم؟ هل هي قابلة للتحقق؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Government & Official Institutions Analytical Questions ===
- What key messages are official sources trying to convey?
- What gaps exist between official discourse and lived reality?
- What topics do official sources avoid discussing?
- Are there contradictions between statements from different officials?
- How are crises and problems framed? Who is blamed?
- What promises are being made? Are they achievable?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من المصادر الحكومية والمؤسسات الرسمية الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما الرسائل الرئيسية التي تروج لها الحكومة؟ ما الذي تحاول إيصاله للمواطنين؟
- هل هناك فجوة بين الخطاب الرسمي والواقع كما يعكسه المحتوى؟
- ما القضايا التي تتجنب الحكومة الحديث عنها؟
- كيف ترد الحكومة على الانتقادات إن وجدت؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian government and official institutional sources.
Required analytical questions:
- What key messages is the government promoting? What is it trying to communicate to citizens?
- Is there a gap between official discourse and reality as reflected in the content?
- What issues is the government avoiding?
- How does the government respond to criticism, if at all?

---

2.4.4 PS_Media — Media Outlets

Source: seed_categories.py:191–247

default_prompt:

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in the Palestinian media landscape. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian media outlet posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Identify the most prominent and recurring topics across different media outlets
- Map the spectrum of viewpoints and narratives on each major story
- Compare and contrast how different outlets frame identical events or issues
- Detect editorial bias patterns, political alignments, and journalistic perspectives
- Highlight areas of media consensus versus points of significant disagreement

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present competing narratives with equal clarity and fairness. For each major topic:
- Articulate the dominant narrative and its proponents
- Present alternative or dissenting narratives with their supporting evidence
- Identify where narratives converge, diverge, or directly contradict
- Note which outlets champion which perspectives

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific posts, outlets, and quotes from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية لوسائل الإعلام ===
- ما القصة الأهم التي يجب على صانع القرار معرفتها؟
- ما الروايات المتنافسة حول الأحداث الرئيسية؟
- من المصادر الأكثر تأثيراً في تشكيل الخطاب؟
- ما التحولات في المشاعر العامة؟
- ما الموضوعات الناشئة التي تستحق المتابعة؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Media Outlets Analytical Questions ===
- What is the most important story a decision-maker needs to know?
- What competing narratives exist around key events?
- Which sources are most influential in shaping discourse?
- What shifts in public sentiment are occurring?
- What emerging topics deserve follow-up?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من وسائل الإعلام الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما القصص التي تحظى بأكبر تغطية؟ ولماذا؟
- كيف تختلف التغطية بين المنافذ الإعلامية المختلفة لنفس القصة؟
- ما وجهات النظر الممثلة وأيها غائبة؟
- هل هناك أجندات واضحة تظهر في التغطية؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian media outlets.
Required analytical questions:
- Which stories are getting the most coverage? Why?
- How does coverage differ between media outlets for the same story?
- What viewpoints are represented and which are absent?
- Are there visible agendas in the coverage?

---

2.4.5 PS_Culture — Culture, Heritage & Events

Source: seed_categories.py:248–304

default_prompt: (seed_categories.py:255–277)

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in Palestinian cultural affairs. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian culture, heritage, and events posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Document cultural events, exhibitions, performances, and heritage preservation initiatives
- Examine how cultural identity and national heritage are framed and promoted
- Identify key cultural actors, institutions, and their messaging strategies
- Analyze the intersection of culture with political expression and resistance narratives
- Track community engagement with cultural programming and heritage initiatives

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the cultural discourse landscape with nuance:
- Distinguish between institutional cultural programming and grassroots cultural expression
- Highlight how heritage and identity narratives serve different community purposes
- Note the role of culture in community resilience and identity preservation
- Identify tensions between preservation of tradition and contemporary cultural evolution

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific events, institutions, and cultural figures from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية للثقافة والتراث والفعاليات ===
- ما الفعاليات الثقافية الأبرز وما حجم التفاعل معها؟
- كيف يتم تأطير الهوية الثقافية والتراث الوطني؟
- ما المؤسسات الثقافية الأكثر نشاطاً وتأثيراً؟
- كيف يتقاطع الخطاب الثقافي مع الخطاب السياسي؟
- ما دور الثقافة في تعزيز صمود المجتمع؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Culture, Heritage & Events Analytical Questions ===
- What are the most prominent cultural events and what engagement do they generate?
- How are cultural identity and national heritage being framed?
- Which cultural institutions are most active and influential?
- How does cultural discourse intersect with political discourse?
- What role does culture play in community resilience?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من المؤسسات الثقافية والتراثية والفعاليات الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما الأنشطة الثقافية البارزة؟
- كيف يتم الحفاظ على التراث وتقديمه؟
- ما علاقة الخطاب الثقافي بالواقع السياسي؟
- من الجمهور المستهدف لهذه الفعاليات؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian cultural and heritage institutions and event organizers.
Required analytical questions:
- What cultural activities are prominent?
- How is heritage being preserved and presented?
- How does cultural discourse relate to the political reality?
- Who is the target audience for these events?

---

2.4.6 PS_Education — Education & Student Movements

Source: seed_categories.py:305–361

default_prompt: (seed_categories.py:312–334)

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in Palestinian education and student affairs. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian education and student movement posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Examine official university and school communications, announcements, and policies
- Document student movement activities, protests, campaigns, and advocacy
- Analyze the impact of political conditions on educational continuity and access
- Track student union elections, political alignments, and organizational dynamics
- Identify emerging concerns around education quality, access, and funding

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the education landscape comprehensively:
- Distinguish between institutional messaging and student activist voices
- Present student grievances and demands alongside institutional responses
- Note political affiliations within student movements without bias
- Highlight the intersection of education with broader societal challenges

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific institutions, student groups, and statements from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية للتعليم والحركات الطلابية ===
- ما القضايا التعليمية الأبرز المطروحة؟
- كيف تؤثر الظروف السياسية على العملية التعليمية؟
- ما مطالب الحركات الطلابية وما التفاعل معها؟
- ما التوجهات السياسية داخل الحراك الطلابي؟
- هل هناك تحديات تتعلق بجودة التعليم أو التمويل؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Education & Student Movements Analytical Questions ===
- What are the most prominent educational issues being raised?
- How do political conditions affect the educational process?
- What are student movement demands and what engagement do they receive?
- What political orientations exist within the student movement?
- Are there challenges related to education quality or funding?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من المؤسسات التعليمية والحركات الطلابية الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما التحديات التعليمية الرئيسية؟
- كيف يتفاعل الطلاب مع الواقع السياسي؟
- ما الأنشطة الأكاديمية والطلابية البارزة؟
- هل هناك قضايا تتعلق بحرية التعبير في الجامعات؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian educational institutions and student movements.
Required analytical questions:
- What are the main educational challenges?
- How do students engage with the political reality?
- What academic and student activities are prominent?
- Are there issues related to freedom of expression on campuses?

---

2.4.7 PS_Factions — Political Parties & Factions

Source: seed_categories.py:362–419

default_prompt: (seed_categories.py:369–392)

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in Palestinian political dynamics. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian political party and faction posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Examine official statements and positions from each political party or faction
- Track inter-factional dynamics including alliances, rivalries, and reconciliation efforts
- Analyze how each faction frames current events and positions itself to its constituency
- Identify shifts in political messaging, tone, or strategic direction
- Document responses to key events and how different factions diverge or align

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the factional landscape with precision and balance:
- Give each faction's position fair and accurate representation
- Highlight areas of agreement and contention between factions
- Note the intended audience for each faction's messaging (domestic vs. international)
- Identify implicit messaging and strategic communications patterns
- Track evolving positions on unity, governance, and resistance strategies

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific factions, leaders, and statements from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية للأحزاب والفصائل السياسية ===
- ما المواقف الرئيسية لكل فصيل من الأحداث الجارية؟
- هل هناك تقارب أو تباعد بين الفصائل؟
- كيف يتم تأطير قضايا المصالحة والانقسام؟
- ما الرسائل الموجهة للقاعدة الشعبية مقابل المجتمع الدولي؟
- هل هناك تحولات في الخطاب السياسي لأي فصيل؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Political Parties & Factions Analytical Questions ===
- What are each faction's main positions on current events?
- Is there convergence or divergence between factions?
- How are reconciliation and division issues framed?
- What messages target the domestic base vs. the international community?
- Are there shifts in political discourse for any faction?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من الأحزاب والفصائل السياسية الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما الديناميكيات بين الفصائل المختلفة؟
- كيف تتعامل الفصائل مع الأحداث الراهنة؟
- ما التنافس أو التعاون الظاهر بين الفصائل؟
- هل هناك خطاب موحد أو متناقض حول القضايا الكبرى؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian political parties and factions.
Required analytical questions:
- What are the dynamics between different factions?
- How do factions address current events?
- What competition or cooperation is visible between factions?
- Is there unified or contradictory discourse on major issues?

---

2.4.8 PS_Gaza — Humanitarian, Aid & Gaza-Focused

Source: seed_categories.py:420–479

default_prompt: (seed_categories.py:427–450)

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in humanitarian affairs and the Gaza context. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE humanitarian, aid, and Gaza-focused posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Document the humanitarian situation as described in the data: casualties, displacement, infrastructure damage, access issues
- Track aid delivery reports, obstacles, and organizational responses
- Examine appeals, funding requests, and donor communications
- Identify gaps between humanitarian needs described and aid being delivered
- Analyze how different organizations frame the crisis and their role in it

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the humanitarian landscape with sensitivity and precision:
- Lead with documented facts: numbers, locations, and specific incidents from the data
- Present organizational responses and their stated capacities
- Highlight access challenges and aid delivery obstacles
- Note the tone and urgency of communications and how they evolve
- Distinguish between situation reports, advocacy messaging, and fundraising appeals

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific organizations, reports, and statistics from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية للإنسانية والإغاثة وغزة ===
- ما الوضع الإنساني كما تصفه المنظمات العاملة؟
- ما العوائق أمام وصول المساعدات؟
- ما الاحتياجات الأكثر إلحاحاً المذكورة؟
- كيف تتفاعل المنظمات الإنسانية مع الأزمة؟
- هل هناك فجوة بين الاحتياجات والاستجابة؟
- ما النداءات والمناشدات الصادرة؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Humanitarian, Aid & Gaza-Focused Analytical Questions ===
- What is the humanitarian situation as described by operating organizations?
- What obstacles exist to aid delivery?
- What are the most urgent needs mentioned?
- How are humanitarian organizations responding to the crisis?
- Is there a gap between needs and response?
- What appeals and calls for action are being issued?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من المنظمات الإنسانية والإغاثية مع التركيز على غزة.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما حجم الأزمة الإنسانية كما تصفها المنظمات؟
- ما التحديات في إيصال المساعدات؟
- كيف يتم تأطير الأزمة في خطاب المنظمات؟
- ما الفجوة بين ما هو مطلوب وما يتم تقديمه؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from humanitarian and aid organizations focused on Gaza.
Required analytical questions:
- What is the scale of the humanitarian crisis as described by organizations?
- What challenges exist in delivering aid?
- How is the crisis framed in organizational communications?
- What is the gap between what is needed and what is being provided?

---

2.4.9 PS_Health — Health & Medical

Source: seed_categories.py:480–537

default_prompt: (seed_categories.py:487–510)

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in Palestinian health affairs. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian health and medical posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Document health system status reports, hospital capacity, and medical supply situations
- Track public health campaigns, vaccination drives, and disease outbreak communications
- Analyze the impact of political conditions on healthcare access and delivery
- Identify key health concerns, service disruptions, and emergency medical situations
- Examine the role of international medical aid and its coordination with local systems

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the health discourse with clinical precision:
- Lead with documented medical data: statistics, facility reports, and official health communications
- Distinguish between routine health messaging and crisis/emergency communications
- Present healthcare worker voices and their reported challenges
- Note the intersection of health with conflict, occupation, and humanitarian access
- Highlight emerging health threats and system vulnerabilities

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific health institutions, reports, and statistics from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية للصحة والطب ===
- ما الوضع الصحي العام كما تصفه المؤسسات الطبية؟
- ما التحديات الرئيسية في القطاع الصحي؟
- كيف تؤثر الظروف السياسية على الخدمات الصحية؟
- ما الحملات الصحية الجارية ومدى التفاعل معها؟
- هل هناك نقص في الأدوية أو المستلزمات الطبية؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== Health & Medical Analytical Questions ===
- What is the general health situation as described by medical institutions?
- What are the main challenges in the health sector?
- How do political conditions affect health services?
- What health campaigns are underway and what engagement do they receive?
- Are there shortages of medicines or medical supplies?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من المؤسسات الصحية والطبية الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما التحديات الصحية الأبرز؟
- كيف يتعامل النظام الصحي مع الضغوط الحالية؟
- ما مستوى الخدمات الصحية المتاحة؟
- هل هناك أزمات صحية طارئة؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian health and medical institutions.
Required analytical questions:
- What are the most prominent health challenges?
- How is the health system coping with current pressures?
- What level of health services is available?
- Are there emerging health emergencies?

---

2.4.10 PS_NGOs — NGOs & Civil Society

Source: seed_categories.py:538–595

default_prompt: (seed_categories.py:545–568)

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in Palestinian civil society. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian NGO and civil society posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Document organizational activities, programs, and advocacy campaigns
- Track human rights reporting, legal advocacy, and documentation efforts
- Analyze community development initiatives and their stated impact
- Identify key civil society concerns: governance, accountability, rights, and freedoms
- Examine relationships between civil society and government/political actors

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the civil society landscape comprehensively:
- Distinguish between service delivery, advocacy, and rights-based messaging
- Present organizational positions on governance and accountability issues
- Note the diversity of civil society voices and their areas of focus
- Highlight the operating environment challenges faced by NGOs
- Identify alignment or tension between civil society and political actors

GROUNDING REQUIREMENTS:
- Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data
- Cite specific organizations, campaigns, and statements from the dataset
- Never invent, assume, or extrapolate beyond what the data shows
- Acknowledge gaps or limitations in the data when relevant

analytical_prompt_ar:

=== أسئلة تحليلية للمنظمات غير الحكومية والمجتمع المدني ===
- ما القضايا الحقوقية والمدنية الأبرز المطروحة؟
- ما الأنشطة والبرامج التي تنفذها المنظمات؟
- كيف تتفاعل منظمات المجتمع المدني مع السلطات؟
- ما التحديات التي تواجه عمل المنظمات؟
- هل هناك حملات مناصرة بارزة؟

analytical_prompt_en:

=== NGOs & Civil Society Analytical Questions ===
- What are the most prominent rights and civic issues being raised?
- What activities and programs are organizations implementing?
- How do civil society organizations interact with authorities?
- What challenges do organizations face in their work?
- Are there prominent advocacy campaigns?

context_ar:

هذا التقرير يحلل منشورات من المنظمات غير الحكومية ومؤسسات المجتمع المدني الفلسطينية.
أسئلة تحليلية مطلوبة:
- ما أبرز الأنشطة والمبادرات المدنية؟
- كيف يتم تأطير قضايا حقوق الإنسان؟
- ما العلاقة بين المجتمع المدني والسلطات؟
- هل هناك ضغوط على عمل المنظمات؟

context_en:

This report analyzes posts from Palestinian NGOs and civil society organizations.
Required analytical questions:
- What are the most prominent civic activities and initiatives?
- How are human rights issues framed?
- What is the relationship between civil society and authorities?
- Are there pressures on organizational work?

---

2.4.11 PS_Officials — Elected Officials & Political Figures

Source: seed_categories.py:596–653

default_prompt: (seed_categories.py:603–626)

You are a senior media monitoring analyst and strategic communications specialist with deep expertise in Palestinian political affairs. Your analysis must be truthful, diligent, and rigorously accurate.

ANALYZE Palestinian elected officials and political figures' posts with the following approach:

ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK:
- Track individual officials' statements, positions, and public communications
- Analyze how officials position themselves on key issues and events
- Identify alignment or divergence between individual officials and their party lines
- Document constituency engagement, responsiveness, and accountability messaging
- Examine the personal vs. official nature of communications

NARRATIVE REPRESENTATION:
Present the political figures' discourse with precision:
- Attribute positions clearly to specific officials
