The lens looks at schools, universities, teachers and faculty, students and their families, curricula, exams and admissions, vocational training, scholarships, the operation of ministries of education, school-year disruptions, and the physical condition of educational facilities. It tracks how educators, students, parents, and ministry officials describe access, quality, and outcomes. It does not cover religious instruction delivered through clergy as such — that belongs to the Religious lens — nor the political contestation over education-policy decisions, which belongs to the Political lens.

Arabic lens (verbatim):

عند قراءة المنشورات من خلال العدسة التعليمية، يهتم المحلل بكيفية تدريس المعرفة وتعلمها واعتمادها. تنظر هذه العدسة في المدارس والجامعات، والمعلمين وأعضاء هيئة التدريس، والطلبة وأسرهم، والمناهج، والامتحانات والقبول، والتدريب المهني، والمنح الدراسية، وعمل وزارات التربية والتعليم، وتعطل العام الدراسي، والحالة المادية للمنشآت التعليمية. تتابع كيف يصف المعلمون والطلبة والأهالي ومسؤولو الوزارات الوصول والجودة والنتائج. لا تشمل هذه العدسة التعليم الديني الذي يقدمه رجال الدين بحد ذاته — فذلك يخص العدسة الدينية — ولا التنافس السياسي حول قرارات السياسة التعليمية، الذي يخص العدسة السياسية.

3.6 Deterministic viewpoints renderer (no LLM)

Why this matters for prompt review: the deterministic viewpoints renderer is the only Pipeline C surface that produces Arabic/English analyst-facing prose without an LLM. Editing it changes the client payload but does not invalidate any LLM cache, prompt content hash, or model-swap safety gate.

Source: pipeline_c_viewpoints.py (~305 lines).

Per ADR 0008 (Phase 2b decision) and ADR 0009 (the Phase 2c implementation pin), the per-topic viewpoints_{ar,en} fields are rendered deterministically by _section_template_en (renamed from _describe_section_en in audit, ~pipeline_c_viewpoints.py:133) and _section_template_ar (renamed from _describe_section_ar, ~line 174), both bodies extracted via inspect.getsource so the rendered template text always tracks live source. They are rendered in the persistence layer from the same per-section analytics Stage 6 already computes — they are never produced by any LLM call. The persistence layer always runs (it is part of Phase 1's per-cluster row insert), whereas Stage 7's per-language LLM calls are best-effort and can fail; decoupling viewpoints from the LLM call means a topic with both LLM languages failing still lands populated viewpoints_ar / viewpoints_en columns.

3.6.1 · Output shape (matches client schema's per-language flat-array pattern)

Matches the existing client schema's per-language flat-array pattern that narrative_{ar,en} and executive_summary_{ar,en} follow:

[ {"perspective": "PS_Government", "description": "..."}, {"perspective": "PS_Media", "description": "..."}, ... ]

Ordering: one entry per section, in the same canonical SECTION_DISPLAY_ORDER (31 sections — regional groups IL_* → INT_* → PS_*, alphabetical within each group) that the per-domain prompts' input block uses. Re-exported from pipeline_c_prompts so the persistence layer and the prompt-input formatter share a single source of truth (Task #336 addendum).

3.6.2 · Verbatim template source — _section_template_en and _section_template_ar

Both function bodies extracted verbatim from pipeline_c_viewpoints.py (~line 133 / ~line 174 respectively). These are pure-deterministic bilingual renderers — no LLM, no I/O.

_section_template_en (verbatim from source):

def _section_template_en(section: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: posts = int(payload.get("posts") or payload.get("count") or 0) pct = payload.get("engagement_share_pct") emotion = payload.get("dominant_emotion") or payload.get("emotion") voices = _format_voices(payload.get("top_voices") or payload.get("voices")) parts: List[str] = [] post_word = "post" if posts == 1 else "posts" if pct is not None: try: pct_val = round(float(pct)) parts.append(f"{section} engaged with {posts} {post_word} ({pct_val}% of topic engagement") except (TypeError, ValueError): parts.append(f"{section} engaged with {posts} {post_word}") else: parts.append(f"{section} engaged with {posts} {post_word}") # Append dominant emotion only when it is non-empty AND not "neutral". if emotion is not None: emo = str(emotion).strip() if emo and emo.lower() != NEUTRAL_EMOTION_LABEL: if pct is not None and parts[-1].endswith("engagement"): parts[-1] += f", dominant emotion: {emo})." else: parts[-1] += f". Dominant emotion: {emo}." else: if pct is not None and parts[-1].endswith("engagement"): parts[-1] += ")." else: parts[-1] += "." else: if pct is not None and parts[-1].endswith("engagement"): parts[-1] += ")." else: parts[-1] += "." if voices: parts.append("Top voices: " + ", ".join(voices) + ".") return " ".join(parts)

_section_template_ar (verbatim from source):

def _section_template_ar(section: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
    posts = int(payload.get("posts") or payload.get("count") or 0)
    pct = payload.get("engagement_share_pct")
    emotion = payload.get("dominant_emotion") or payload.get("emotion")
    voices = _format_voices(payload.get("top_voices") or payload.get("voices"))

    parts: List[str] = []
    if pct is not None:
        try:
            pct_val = round(float(pct))
            parts.append(
                f"شاركت {section} بـ {posts} منشوراً ({pct_val}٪ من تفاعل الموضوع"
            )
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            parts.append(f"شاركت {section} بـ {posts} منشوراً")
    else:
        parts.append(f"شاركت {section} بـ {posts} منشوراً")

    if emotion is not None:
        emo = str(emotion).strip()
        if emo and emo.lower() != NEUTRAL_EMOTION_LABEL:
            if pct is not None and parts[-1].endswith("الموضوع"):
                parts[-1] += f"، بمشاعر سائدة: {emo})."
            else:
                parts[-1] += f". المشاعر السائدة: {emo}."
        else:
            if pct is not None and parts[-1].endswith("الموضوع"):
                parts[-1] += ")."
            else:
                parts[-1] += "."
    else:
        if pct is not None and parts[-1].endswith("الموضوع"):
            parts[-1] += ")."
        else:
            parts[-1] += "."

    if voices:
        parts.append("أبرز الأصوات: " + "، ".join(voices) + ".")
    return " ".join(parts)

3.7 Input formatters: format_prompt_input and format_synthesis_input

Both Pipeline C LLM call sites separate prompt text (the system + user templates in §3.2-§3.4) from prompt input (the per-run data block that gets appended to the user message). The two input formatters are the contract surfaces between the upstream stages that produce the data and the prompts that consume it.

3.7.1 · format_prompt_input(topic_row, posts, ...) — Phase 2b per-topic input

Source: pipeline_c_prompts.py — format_prompt_input (~line 740). ADR pin: ADR 0008 — "three consumers (Phase 1 source decomp / 2b prompts / 2c dispatcher) re-validate together".

Renders the per-section input block fed to a domain prompt. Sections are emitted in the fixed SECTION_DISPLAY_ORDER (31 canonical section keys; unexpected labels are appended at the end in lexicographic order so they surface visibly rather than disappearing). Within each section, the formatter selects the top N voices by post count (default 3) and emits each as @<account> (<n> posts). Each post is truncated to 280 chars (default) with an ellipsis. Matching against the canonical order is case-insensitive so historical mixed-case labels (e.g. PS_Individuals) line up with the canonical uppercase keys.

3.7.2 · format_synthesis_input(topics, language) — Phase 2c day-level input

Source: pipeline_c_prompts.py — format_synthesis_input (~line 1153). ADR pin: ADR 0009 — parallel pin to ADR 0008's pin on format_prompt_input.

Renders the day's per-topic briefs as the synthesis prompt's user-side input. Briefs are GROUPED BY domain_primary (the seven canonical domains plus unclassified) in the fixed SYNTHESIS_DOMAIN_DISPLAY_ORDER (political, financial, social, health, security, religious, educational, unclassified); within each domain, briefs are ordered by descending cluster_size with stable secondary sort on cluster_label. Topics missing the language's brief are silently skipped (synthesis runs on what is available; the degraded-flag logic in the Stage 9 dispatcher decides whether to flag the run).

Headings (verbatim) by language:

  • English: # Today's per-topic briefs — <N> topics across <M> domains then per-domain ## Domain: <key> — <N> topics then per-topic ### Topic <cluster_label> (<topic_stable_id>, size=<n>): <title>.
  • Arabic: # موجزات اليوم لكل موضوع — <N> موضوعاً عبر <M> مجالاً then per-domain ## مجال: <key> — <N> موضوعاً then per-topic ### الموضوع <cluster_label> (<topic_stable_id>, الحجم=<n>): <title>.

Empty-day fallback: when no language-appropriate briefs are available the formatter returns a single one-line marker — (no per-topic briefs available for the day — nothing to synthesise) in English; (لا توجد موجزات متاحة لليوم — لا شيء للتركيب) in Arabic — so the synthesis prompt still receives a non-empty body.

3.8 Prompt-audit tooling

Two stdlib-only CLIs ship in the project root for auditing the Pipeline C prompt surface without invoking the LLM, the database, or S3. Guarantees: no LLM (does not import processing.call_llm), no database (does not import db / report_db), no S3 (does not import pipeline_c_reader). Operators and analysts use them to review the prompts outside Python source and to quantify how much of the consolidated skeleton each starter-draft prompt currently replicates.

3.8.1 · pipeline_c_prompts_export.py — reviewable markdown mirror

Source: pipeline_c_prompts_export.py (Task #382). Output: docs/pipeline-c-prompts.md — the eight Phase 2b per-domain prompts plus the Phase 2c synthesis prompt mirrored out as a single bilingual markdown surface analysts can read end-to-end without opening Python source.

Modes:

python pipeline_c_prompts_export.py # rewrites the file python pipeline_c_prompts_export.py --check # exits 1 if drift python pipeline_c_prompts_export.py --diff # prints unified diff

Direction: one-way only. The Python file is the source of truth; this CLI ONLY regenerates the markdown, never the other way round. PRs that edit prompt text must rerun the CLI (or rely on the CI --check mode to catch the drift). Carries the per-prompt content hash from pipeline_c_prompts.per_prompt_content_hashes() in the rendered output so any analyst review is reproducibly tied to a specific source state.

3.8.2 · pipeline_c_prompt_skeleton_check.py (Task #320) — coverage CLI

Per-(prompt, language) pattern-match CLI that surfaces the gap between the consolidated-skeleton elements documented in docs/prompt-authorship-guide.md and the elements present in each starter draft. Output: a structured report enumerating "elements present" vs "elements missing" for each of the 16 (prompt × language) combinations.

Source: pipeline_c_prompt_skeleton_check.py (Task #320). Role: pattern-match CLI that surfaces, for one (--prompt-key, --language) pair at a time, which elements of the prompts-wave consolidated skeleton (processing.py — PROMPT_CONSOLIDATED_V1 / SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE / SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE_V2 flags + the coherence-overlay text) the selected Pipeline C prompt currently replicates, misses, or both.

This is the authoritative tool for answering "how much pending revision is there?" — its output is the per-element evidence behind the "starter draft — analyst revision pending" tag in §3.2 and §3.3, and behind the §9 pending-revisions roll-up. ADR 0008 §11 ("honesty addendum") + §9 cross-link to ADR 0009 hold the authoritative REPLICATED-vs-OMITTED design intent; the CLI shows the current per-element state.

Guarantees: no LLM (does not import processing.call_llm), no database (does not import db / report_db), no S3 (does not import pipeline_c_reader). Footer carries the per-prompt content hash for reproducibility.

---

§4 — Worked examples

These examples show the assembled shape of each final API call. Placeholder values for runtime data are shown as <…>. The Pipeline B examples (§4.1–§4.5) use the "Analyst" preset and the "PS_Media" category; the Pipeline C examples (§4.6–§4.7) use the political domain and a sample three-topic day.

4.1 · topic_analysis — preset present, category present, all flags OFF (SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE=0, ENABLE_ENRICHED_SIGNALS=0)

System message:

You are an expert analyst specializing in Palestinian social media trends and Middle Eastern affairs. Always respond in the same language as the content provided (Arabic or English).

All output MUST be in English only.

User message: assembled as [Analyst preset framework] + [PS_Media category prompt] + [hardcoded topic-prompt template wrapping <topic_name> / <posts_evidence> / <keywords> / <actors> / <locations> / <geo> etc.]. The framework begins:

=== Media Outlets Analytical Questions ===
- What is the most important story a decision-maker needs to know?
- What competing narratives exist around key events?
- Which sources are most influential in shaping discourse?
- What shifts in public sentiment are occurring?
- What emerging topics deserve follow-up?

You are a media monitoring analyst specializing in Palestinian discourse. Your task is to analyze the following content and provide a deep analytical report.

⚠️ CRITICAL: ALL output MUST be in English only. Translate any Arabic content you quote.
⚠️ TEXT FORMATTING: Do NOT use any Markdown formatting such as ##, **, *, __, >, or - at the start of lines. Write plain readable text without any formatting symbols.

=== TOPIC: <topic_name> ===
Status: <velocity_status>

=== POSTS (Read carefully - this is the primary content): ===
<posts_evidence>

… continued by keywords, actors, locations, geo distribution blocks; then the JSON-output instruction block. Model: gpt-4.1-mini with response_format={'type':'json_object'}.

4.2 · topic_analysis — SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE ON, ENABLE_ENRICHED_SIGNALS ON

System message: identical to §4.1.

User message: identical to §4.1, plus:

  1. The coherence overlay is appended after the === ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK === block:
=== COHERENCE CHECK & AMALGAMATION BAN ===

Before drafting, execute the following three steps:

1. Coherence check: Determine whether the supplied posts cohere around one event, or split into multiple distinct events.
   - Example of bad amalgamation: posts about "municipal elections" and posts about "Hezbollah clashes" grouped together in the same topic.
   - Resolution: identify the dominant event (most represented in the posts) and write only about it.

2. Amalgamation ban: If posts cover unrelated events, do not blend them into one analysis. Pick the dominant event. Treat the rest as out-of-scope.

3. Geographic specificity: State the geographic scope being narrated clearly in the analysis. Note explicitly when other in-scope geographies are absent from the evidence provided.
  1. {emotion_context} is filled (e.g. === Dominant emotion: anger (60%); secondary: sadness (25%), neutral (15%) ===).
  2. {faction_context} is filled (e.g. === Faction breakdown: Hamas: 40%, Fatah: 30%, Islamic Jihad: 15%, other 15% ===).
  3. An 11th output field "_off_topic_dropped" is added to the JSON shape block, asking the model to list brief excerpts it judged off-topic.
4.3 topic_analysis_retry — language-mixing retry

System message: identical to §8.1.

User message: identical to §8.1 (or §8.2 if flags are on), plus at the very end:

\n\nIMPORTANT: Your previous response contained Arabic text. Please respond ONLY in English this time.

call_type = "topic_analysis_retry", fast = False

---

4.4 · executive_summary — preset summary framework present

Per-language summary call. Uses "Analyst" preset + "PS_Media" category, English.

System message: identical to §4.1.

User message:

You are a senior media analyst. Your task is to write an analytical executive summary for a busy decision-maker.

⚠️ ALL output MUST be in English only.
⚠️ TEXT FORMATTING: Do NOT use any Markdown formatting such as ##, **, *, __, - at the start of lines, or > for quotes. Write plain flowing narrative prose without any formatting symbols.

=== REPORT CONTEXT ===
This report analyzes posts from Palestinian media outlets.
Required analytical questions:
- Which stories are getting the most coverage? Why?
- How does coverage differ between media outlets for the same story?
- What viewpoints are represented and which are absent?
- Are there visible agendas in the coverage?
=== END CONTEXT ===

TOPICS ANALYZED TODAY:
[
  {"topic": "<topic_name>", "title": "<title>", "brief": "<brief>", "post_count": <n>, "engagement": <n>},
  …
]

Total posts: <total_posts>

Write an analytical executive summary of 4-5 paragraphs:
- Para 1: The main story
- Para 2: Competing narratives
- Para 3: Sentiments and concerns
…

Model: gpt-4.1 with JSON mode OFF (plain text output).

4.5 executive_summary — no preset summary framework (default framework fallback)

Identical to §8.4 except the final block is the hardcoded default framework (§6.6) instead of the preset's executive_summary_prompt_en. The system prompt falls back to call_llm's default (§3.2) if no preset is provided.

---

---

4.6 · pipeline_c_topic_analysis — Stage 7 dispatcher, English, political domain

Issued by pipeline_c_dispatch.run_topic_analysis_for_run for one topic, one language. The dispatcher calls processing.call_llm(call_type="pipeline_c_topic_analysis", use_json=True, fast=False, system_prompt=<§3.4.1 EN>, prompt=<select_prompt("political", "en")> + "\n\n" + <format_prompt_input(topic_row, posts)>).

System message (verbatim from §3.4.1):

You are an expert analyst of Palestinian social-media trends. Return a single JSON object with the fields title, brief, narrative, key_voices, actors_analysis, sentiment_analysis, geographic_analysis, temporal_analysis, platform_analysis, source_analysis, sentiment_summary. No text outside the JSON object. All output MUST be in English only. key_voices MUST be a JSON array of objects, each object having exactly two keys: "account" (the handle or display name, with @ if present) and "quote" (one short distinctive quote, ≤ 12 words). Do NOT use a flat object {"@handle": "quote"} and do NOT use a list of strings. Each of actors_analysis, sentiment_analysis, geographic_analysis, temporal_analysis, platform_analysis, source_analysis, and sentiment_summary MUST be a single short prose paragraph (a string, ≤ 80 words) grounded in what this cluster's posts actually show — not a generic statement about the dataset.

User message = select_prompt("political", "en") (§3.2.1, the verbatim political English prompt, 2,373 chars) followed by a blank line and then the rendered input block from format_prompt_input(topic_row, posts). Input block shape:

=== TOPIC: <cluster_label> / <topic_stable_id> ===
Cluster size: <n> posts · Dominant emotion: <emotion>

=== Section: PS_Government — <n> posts ===
Top voices: @<account> (<n> posts), @<account> (<n> posts), …
Posts:
- <post content, truncated to 280 chars>
- …

=== Section: PS_Media — <n> posts ===
…

(31 sections in SECTION_DISPLAY_ORDER, only non-empty ones rendered)

Expected JSON response (eleven fields, pinned by §3.4.1):

{ "title": "...", "brief": "...", "narrative": "...", "key_voices": [{"account": "@example", "quote": "..."}, ...], "actors_analysis": "...", "sentiment_analysis": "...", "geographic_analysis": "...", "temporal_analysis": "...", "platform_analysis": "...", "source_analysis": "...", "sentiment_summary": "..." }

Arabic side: identical structure, but with the Arabic system prompt (§3.4.1 AR), the Arabic political user prompt (§3.2.1 AR), and an identical input block (English headings Top voices, Section for both languages — only the prompt text and expected output language differ).

4.7 pipeline_c_daily_synthesis — Stage 9 synthesis, English, sample day

Issued by pipeline_c_synthesis.run_daily_synthesis_for_run once per language after Stage 7 completes. The dispatcher calls processing.call_llm(call_type="pipeline_c_daily_synthesis", use_json=True, fast=False, system_prompt=<§3.4.2 EN>, prompt=<select_synthesis_prompt("en")> + "\n\n" + <format_synthesis_input(topics, "en")>).

System message (verbatim from §3.4.2):

You are a senior analyst. Return a single JSON object with exactly one key, ``executive_summary``, whose value is the multi-paragraph synthesis prose. No text outside the JSON object. All output MUST be in English only.

User message = select_synthesis_prompt("en") (§3.3, the verbatim English synthesis prompt) followed by a blank line and then the rendered synthesis input block. Shape of the input block for a sample three-topic day (placeholder values shown as <…>):

``` # Today's per-topic briefs — 3 topics across 2 domains

§5 — Model & response-format reference

LLM_CALL_REGISTRY is the single source of truth for every LLM call site in the project. Defined in processing.py (~line 1121, body ~230 lines); the admin AI Model Inventory view renders directly from this dict so the table can never silently disagree with what call_llm actually uses.

call_type keyLabelModeloutput_contractJSON modePipeline
topic_analysisTopic analysis (primary)gpt-4.1-minijson_object✅B
topic_analysis_retryTopic analysis (retry / fallback)gpt-4.1-minijson_object✅B
executive_summaryExecutive summarygpt-4.1plain_text❌B
domain_routing_fallbackDomain routing fallback (Pipeline C Phase 2a)gpt-4.1-minijson_object✅C
pipeline_c_topic_analysisPipeline C topic analysis (Phase 2b)gpt-4.1-minijson_object✅C
pipeline_c_daily_synthesisPipeline C daily synthesis (Phase 2c)gpt-4.1-minijson_object✅C
generalGeneral (default call_type)gpt-4.1-miniplain_text❌(safety net)
_fallback_fastImplicit fast-tier fallbackgpt-4.1-miniplain_text❌(safety net)
_fallback_fullImplicit full-tier fallbackgpt-4.1plain_text❌(safety net)

max_completion_tokens: all calls use 8192 (set by call_llm).
Reasoning tokens: when an o-series or gpt-5 family model is the active model, the wrapper reads usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens and logs it alongside input / output tokens.
Fallback derivation: _fallback_fast.model is rewritten at module load to mirror topic_analysis_retry.model; _fallback_full.model mirrors executive_summary.model. The module refuses to load if either derivation is removed or hardcoded to a disagreeing value.

5.1 · Curated model alternatives (last reviewed 2026-04-28)

Env: AI_INTEGRATIONS_OPENAI_BASE_URL.

  • JSON-object call sites — any of: gpt-4.1, gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o4-mini, o3, o3-mini, gpt-5, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano.
  • Plain-text call sites — any of: gpt-4.1, gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o4-mini, o3, o3-mini, gpt-5, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano.
Reasoning-class models — swap-safety constraint

Reasoning-class models (o-series, gpt-5 family) appear in both alternatives lists because the contract probe in .local/probe_models.py succeeds against them — but the 8192-token completion budget in call_llm does not yet budget for reasoning tokens separately, so swapping a JSON call site to one of them requires a follow-up to call_llm first.

5.2 · Re-validation procedure (so the next reviewer can repeat it)
  1. Run .local/probe_models.py from the project root. It iterates every model in both alternatives lists plus the candidate successors named in the most recent audit prompts (currently the GPT-5 family and the o3 family) and issues two minimal one-token probes per model against AI_INTEGRATIONS_OPENAI_BASE_URL: one plain-text completion, one with response_format={"type": "json_object"}.
  2. A model belongs in _ALTERNATIVES_PLAIN_TEXT iff its plain-text probe returns ok. It additionally belongs in _ALTERNATIVES_JSON_OBJECT iff its json_object probe also returns ok.
  3. Bump ALTERNATIVES_REVIEWED_ON to today's date.

§6 — Where to edit what

6.1 · Pipeline B
Prompt category File to edit Key / field Notes
System persona seed_presets.py BUILTIN_PRESETS[*]['system_prompt'] (or DB row via admin UI) system_prompt Appended with language enforcement by build_preset_system_prompt (§2.2.1)
Analysis framework seed_presets.py BUILTIN_PRESETS[*]['analysis_framework_ar/en'] (or DB row) analysis_framework_ar, analysis_framework_en Leave blank to use default fallback in get_analysis_prompts (§2.5.3)
Executive summary framework seed_presets.py BUILTIN_PRESETS[*]['executive_summary_prompt_ar/en'] (or DB row) executive_summary_prompt_ar, executive_summary_prompt_en Leave blank to use default in generate_overall_summary (§2.5.4 / §2.5.6)
Category instructions (topic prompt) seed_categories.py BUILTIN_CATEGORIES[*]['analytical_prompt_ar/en'] (or DB row) analytical_prompt_ar, analytical_prompt_en Read by get_category_prompt. Returns '' if missing — does NOT fall back to default_prompt. default_prompt is only read by s3_automation.py and wrapped by §2.6.6
Category context (executive summary) seed_categories.py BUILTIN_CATEGORIES[*]['context_ar/en'] (or DB row) context_ar, context_en Read by _load_category_context
Hardcoded topic-prompt skeleton processing.py get_analysis_prompts function Arabic and English templates (§2.5.1, §2.5.2)
Hardcoded executive-summary skeleton processing.py generate_summary_for_language inner function Arabic (§2.5.5) / English (§2.5.7)
Coherence overlay (V1) processing.py get_analysis_prompts, SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE branch Toggle off via SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE=0 env var (§2.6.1)
Coherence overlay V2 processing.py get_analysis_prompts, SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE_V2 branch Layered on top of V1; toggle on via SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE_V2=1. V1 is the master switch — V2 has no effect when V1 is off (§2.6.1.1)
Enriched-signals overlays (emotion + faction) processing.py generate_topic_analysis, reanalyze_single_topic Toggle off via ENABLE_ENRICHED_SIGNALS=0 env var (§2.6.2, §2.6.3)
Language-retry suffix processing.py generate_topic_analysis, reanalyze_single_topic English-side Arabic detection only; hardcoded string (§2.6.4)
6.2 · Pipeline C
Prompt category File to edit Key / field Notes
Phase 2a fallback system prompt pipeline_c_anchors.py PHASE_2A_FALLBACK_SYSTEM_PROMPT (~line 340) Flips anchors_content_hash
Phase 2a fallback user template pipeline_c_anchors.py build_fallback_prompt (~line 369) Bilingual passthrough; post cap lives in pipeline_c_processing
Phase 2a anchor lens text pipeline_c_anchors.py PHASE_2A_DOMAIN_ANCHORS[<key>]['en'/'ar'] Drives both the anchor-similarity routing and the menu rendered into the fallback prompt (§3.5)
Phase 2b domain prompts (eight) pipeline_c_prompts.py PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS[<key>]['en'/'ar'] (~lines 91-495) Edit in place; flips per-prompt content hash (§3.1, §3.2)
Phase 2c synthesis prompt pipeline_c_prompts.py PHASE_2C_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT['en'/'ar'] (~line 898) Edit in place; flips the synthesis per-prompt content hash (§3.3)
Stage 7 dispatcher system prompt pipeline_c_dispatch.py _system_prompt_for_language (~line 81) Pins the 11-field JSON output contract + key_voices shape (§3.4.1)
Stage 9 synthesis system prompt pipeline_c_synthesis.py _system_prompt_for_language (~line 45) Pins the single-key executive_summary JSON output contract (§3.4.2)
Per-topic input formatter pipeline_c_prompts.py format_prompt_input (~line 740) Pinned by ADR 0008 (§3.7.1); revising is a re-validation event for all eight domain prompts
Per-day synthesis input formatter pipeline_c_prompts.py format_synthesis_input (~line 1153) Pinned by ADR 0009 (§3.7.2); revising is a re-validation event for the synthesis prompt
Section display order pipeline_c_prompts.py SECTION_DISPLAY_ORDER (~line 616) Shared by per-domain prompt input AND the viewpoints renderer (§3.6 — Task #336)
Synthesis domain display order pipeline_c_prompts.py SYNTHESIS_DOMAIN_DISPLAY_ORDER (~line 1123) Pins the domain-group ordering inside the synthesis input block
Deterministic viewpoints templates pipeline_c_viewpoints.py _section_template_en (~line 133) / _section_template_ar (~line 174) No LLM; revising changes client payload but not any prompt content hash (§3.6)
Phase 2a domain lens text pipeline_c_anchors.py PHASE_2A_DOMAIN_ANCHORS (~line 81) Bilingual lens blurbs rendered into the §3.5 fallback prompt menu AND fed to the anchor-similarity router (§3.5.1)
Validation-CLI Stage 7 system prompt pipeline_c_prompt_eval.py _system_prompt_for_language (~line 100) ⚠️ Currently DRIFTED from the production dispatcher copy in §3.4.1 — see §3.4.1.1 and §9.B
6.3 Model selection (both pipelines)
Action File Field
Swap the model on any call site processing.py LLM_CALL_REGISTRY[<call_type>]['model'] (~line 1121) — the _fallback_* entries auto-derive
Bump the alternatives-list audit date processing.py ALTERNATIVES_REVIEWED_ON (~line 1091) after re-running .local/probe_models.py (§5)

---

§7 — Appendix: How this document was built

7.1 Files surveyed

| File | What was extracted | |---|---| | processing.py | call_llm (~line 1434); LLM_CALL_REGISTRY (~line 1121); all Pipeline B prompt-assembly functions; the SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE / SHARP_PROMPT_COHERENCE_V2 / ENABLE_ENRICHED_SIGNALS feature-flag branches | | seed_presets.py | BUILTIN_PRESETS list — all preset dicts verbatim (§2.3) | | seed_categories.py | BUILTIN_CATEGORIES list — all category dicts verbatim (§2.4); LEGACY_DEACTIVATE set | | pipeline_c_prompts.py | PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS (eight verbatim, §3.2); PHASE_2C_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT (§3.3); SECTION_DISPLAY_ORDER, SYNTHESIS_DOMAIN_DISPLAY_ORDER; format_prompt_input and format_synthesis_input (§3.7); prompts_content_hash, per_prompt_content_hashes (§3.1) | | pipeline_c_anchors.py | PHASE_2A_FALLBACK_SYSTEM_PROMPT and build_fallback_prompt (§3.5) | | pipeline_c_dispatch.py | _system_prompt_for_language (§3.4.1); the Stage 7 dispatcher itself | | pipeline_c_synthesis.py | _system_prompt_for_language (§3.4.2); the Stage 9 dispatcher itself; the degraded-run thresholds | | pipeline_c_viewpoints.py | The deterministic templater (§3.6) | | All other *.py files in the project root | Searched for call_llm( to confirm no additional LLM call sites exist outside the ones registered in LLM_CALL_REGISTRY |

7.2 To re-run this inventory after adding a new prompt site
  1. Search the codebase for call_llm( — every new call site MUST register itself in LLM_CALL_REGISTRY (§5) and pass its call_type to the wrapper. Add the new row to §1 (the unified call-site map).
  2. For Pipeline B prompt sources: search for BUILTIN_PRESETS, BUILTIN_CATEGORIES, and any new feature-flag variable used to gate prompt overlays; quote new fields in §2.
  3. For Pipeline C prompt sources: any new key in PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS or revision to PHASE_2C_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT should flip the per-prompt content hashes printed in §3.1 — verify by re-running this generator and diffing the hash table.
  4. Update the assembly diagrams in §2.1 and the worked examples in §4.
7.3 Regeneration command

This document is generated by .local/build_llm_prompts_reference.py:

uv run python .local/build_llm_prompts_reference.py

The script imports pipeline_c_prompts, pipeline_c_anchors, pipeline_c_dispatch, pipeline_c_synthesis, and processing directly so the verbatim Pipeline C prompt text, the LLM call-site table, and the per-prompt content hashes are always reproduced from the live source — never hand-edited in the document.

---

7.2 To re-run this inventory after adding a new prompt site

1. Search the codebase for call_llm( — every new call site MUST register itself in LLM_CALL_REGISTRY (§5) and pass its call_type to the wrapper. Add the new row to §1 (the unified call-site map). 2. For Pipeline B prompt sources: search for BUILTIN_PRESETS, BUILTIN_CATEGORIES, and any new feature-flag variable used to gate prompt overlays; quote new fields in §2. 3. For Pipeline C prompt sources: any new key in PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS or revision to PHASE_2C_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT should flip the per-prompt content hashes printed in §3.1 — verify by re-running this generator and diffing the hash table. 4. Update the assembly diagrams in §2.1 and the worked examples in §4.

7.3 Regeneration command

This document is generated by .local/build_llm_prompts_reference.py:

uv run python .local/build_llm_prompts_reference.py

The script imports pipeline_c_prompts, pipeline_c_anchors, pipeline_c_dispatch, pipeline_c_synthesis, and processing directly so the verbatim Pipeline C prompt text, the LLM call-site table, and the per-prompt content hashes are always reproduced from the live source — never hand-edited in the document.

§9 — Changelog

### 2026-05-21 — Task #517 (initial regeneration)

  • Renamed: docs/llm_analysis_prompts.md (Pipeline-B-only) → docs/llm-prompts-reference.md (this document). The old file is kept in place as a historical 2026-04-30 snapshot; operator's call when to delete it.
  • Restructured to eight sections (§1-§8): §1 unified call-site map across both pipelines; §2 Pipeline B prompt surface (preserved verbatim from the old §2-§7, renumbered under §2.1-§2.6); §3 Pipeline C prompt surface (new); §4 worked examples (existing Pipeline B + two new Pipeline C); §5 model & response-format reference (refreshed from live LLM_CALL_REGISTRY — now six primary call types instead of three); §6 cheat sheet (split into Pipeline B / Pipeline C / model-selection subsections); §7 appendix (regeneration command added); §8 this changelog.
  • Added §3 Pipeline C surface from source: eight Phase 2b domain prompts verbatim from PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS; the Phase 2c synthesis prompt verbatim from PHASE_2C_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT; the Stage 7 dispatcher and Stage 9 synthesis system prompts; the Phase 2a fallback system prompt + user template; the deterministic viewpoints renderer description; the format_prompt_input and format_synthesis_input contracts.
  • All nine Pipeline C prompts tagged "starter draft — analyst revision pending" per the brief. None have been verifiably revised against docs/prompt-authorship-guide.md as of this writing (see ADR 0008 §11 "honesty addendum" and pipeline_c_prompt_skeleton_check.py).
  • Pipeline B drift check (re-verify vs current source): spot-check found the existing §2-§7 content matches the live seed_presets.py, seed_categories.py, and processing.py surfaces as of 2026-05-21; no per-subsection drift was identified during this pass. The line-number references in the cheat sheet (§6.1) were last fully audited against the 2026-04-30 revision; they remain approximate guidance, with the live source paths (file + symbol name) as the authoritative locator.
  • LLM_CALL_REGISTRY table refreshed: the model table in §5 is generated directly from the live registry, so the three Pipeline C entries (domain_routing_fallback, pipeline_c_topic_analysis, pipeline_c_daily_synthesis) added in Phase 2a/2b/2c now appear alongside the Pipeline B entries.
  • Regeneration script added: .local/build_llm_prompts_reference.py. Imports the prompt modules directly so every regeneration reflects the live source.

### 2026-05-21 — Task #518 (audit pass over Task #517)

An audit sweep over the initial regeneration surfaced five concrete gaps; this revision fixes all five and adds a new §9 to make every pending-revision surface visible from one place.

  • Fixed §3.4.1 — surfaced dispatcher↔validation-CLI drift. Added §3.4.1.1 reproducing the SECOND copy of _system_prompt_for_language that lives in pipeline_c_prompt_eval.py (~line 100). The dispatcher's docstring claims "kept in sync" but at the time of this regeneration the two copies are NOT byte-identical: the production copy carries the post-Task-#379 eleven-field + key_voices schema pin (~919 chars EN); the validation-CLI copy carries the pre-#379 four-field short form (~211 chars EN). Reconciliation tracked under §9.B.
  • Added §3.5.1 — verbatim PHASE_2A_DOMAIN_ANCHORS lens text. Seven bilingual blurbs from pipeline_c_anchors.py that get rendered into the Phase 2a fallback prompt menu AND fed to the anchor-similarity router. Previously referenced but not reproduced.
  • Fixed §3.6 — corrected viewpoints function names and reproduced templates verbatim. Renamed _describe_section_en / _describe_section_ar (which do not exist in source) to _section_template_en / _section_template_ar (the actual function names, pipeline_c_viewpoints.py:133,174). Both bodies now extracted via inspect.getsource so the rendered template text always tracks live source.
  • Added §3.8 — prompt-audit tooling. Documented pipeline_c_prompts_export.py (the --check-mode reviewable markdown mirror at docs/pipeline-c-prompts.md, per Task #382) and pipeline_c_prompt_skeleton_check.py (the REPLICATED-vs-OMITTED pattern-match CLI per Task #320 + ADR 0008 §11) — the two stdlib-only CLIs analysts use to review and audit the Pipeline C prompts without invoking the LLM/DB/S3.
  • Added §9 — Pending revisions & open work. New consolidated section that an operator can read top-to-bottom to see every Pipeline B + Pipeline C prompt surface that is currently starter-draft, drifted, or otherwise unverified.
  • Added drift-detection assertion in the generator. .local/build_llm_prompts_reference.py now SystemExits on re-run if pipeline_c_dispatch._system_prompt_for_language and pipeline_c_prompt_eval._system_prompt_for_language become byte-identical — forcing the §3.4.1.1 drift narrative and the §9.B pending entry to be reconciled when (if) the two copies are unified in code.
  • Updated §6.2 cheat sheet. Corrected the viewpoints template function names; added rows for PHASE_2A_DOMAIN_ANCHORS and the validation-CLI Stage 7 system prompt.

---

§8 — Pending revisions & open work

Everything in this section is a known gap between the prompt surface shipped today and the surface the team intends to ship after the analyst-revision passes complete. Each entry names the surface, the current status, and the authoritative tool or ADR that quantifies the gap so a reviewer can verify status without trawling source.

9.A All nine Pipeline C prompts — starter drafts, awaiting analyst revision

Surface: the eight Phase 2b per-domain prompts (§3.2.1-§3.2.8) and the Phase 2c synthesis prompt (§3.3) — both languages each. Status: STARTER DRAFT. None have been verifiably revised against docs/prompt-authorship-guide.md. The tag on each prompt entry ("starter draft — analyst revision pending") is not a stylistic flourish; it is a literal status flag. Authoritative quantification: pipeline_c_prompt_skeleton_check.py (§3.8.2) renders, for any (--prompt-key, --language) pair, the REPLICATED-vs-OMITTED checklist of consolidated-skeleton elements. ADR 0008 §11 ("honesty addendum") carries the authoritative design intent for which elements each draft was *intended* to replicate vs deliberately omit. The CLI shows current state; the ADR shows intended state; the gap between them is the analyst-revision backlog. Unblocks: Phase 2b/2c is considered analyst-revised when every prompt's REPLICATED-vs-OMITTED rendering matches the intent in ADR 0008 §11 (plus any addendum the analyst adds during revision). Until then, the prompt content hashes in §3.1 should be treated as starter-draft hashes that will flip when revision lands.

9.B Stage 7 dispatcher ↔ validation CLI — system-prompt drift

Surface: pipeline_c_dispatch._system_prompt_for_language (production, §3.4.1) and pipeline_c_prompt_eval._system_prompt_for_language (validation CLI, §3.4.1.1). Source docstring in dispatch.py:82-83 claims they are "kept in sync by the Phase 2b authorship process." Status: DRIFTED. Verified at regeneration time: dispatcher EN ≈ 919 chars (post-#379 eleven-field + key_voices shape pin); validation-CLI EN ≈ 211 chars (pre-#379 four-field form). The validation CLI is therefore evaluating prompts against a contract that does not match production. Authoritative quantification: the regeneration script (.local/build_llm_prompts_reference.py) hard-fails if the two copies ever become byte-identical — that's the signal the drift has been resolved and this section needs to be retired. Recommended resolution path: open a follow-up task to either (a) replace the eval-CLI's local copy with a direct import of pipeline_c_dispatch._system_prompt_for_language, or (b) factor the wording into a third module that both import. Either way the dispatcher docstring's "kept in sync" claim should be made true by structure, not by convention.

9.C PHASE_2A_DOMAIN_ANCHORS lens text — starter drafts

Surface: the seven bilingual lens blurbs in §3.5.1 that feed BOTH the deterministic anchor-similarity router AND the user-side fallback prompt menu. Status: STARTER DRAFT. The in-file commentary at pipeline_c_anchors.py:64-79 is explicit that these are placeholder lens text the production drafts must replace before the Phase 2a router can be considered analyst-revised. Authoritative quantification: no dedicated CLI for this yet; review proceeds against the commentary block's stated length / lens / anti-patterns rules. Coupling note: revising any one row flips the per-prompt content hash for the routing fallback path AND the cache key for the anchor-similarity router (which content-hashes the anchor text for next-tick reload without restart). One edit, two cache invalidations.

9.D Pipeline B Analyst preset framework — drift spot-check pending

Surface: seed_presets.py — the Analyst preset's analysis_framework_ar / analysis_framework_en fields, reproduced in §2.3.3 from the 2026-04-30 snapshot. Status: UNVERIFIED. The prior regeneration's exploration notes mentioned that the Analyst preset had been given its own unique framework, "correcting previous drift" against the default. This audit pass did not re-diff the §2.3.3 text against live seed_presets.py line-for-line. Recommended quantification: dump BUILTIN_PRESETS[*]['analysis_framework_{ar,en}'] and diff against §2.3.3; if any preset's framework text differs from the rendered §2.3.3, file a follow-up to refresh the §2 body via the preserved-verbatim path in the generator.

9.E §6.1 Pipeline B cheat-sheet line numbers — approximate

Surface: the (~line N) markers throughout §6.1 (and a few in §2.1-§2.5). Status: APPROXIMATE. Last fully audited against the 2026-04-30 revision of processing.py / seed_presets.py / seed_categories.py. Subsequent edits to those files may have shifted line numbers without invalidating the cheat sheet's logical correctness. Authoritative locator: file + symbol name (e.g. processing.py — build_preset_system_prompt) is the guaranteed-stable reference. Treat (~line N) as a fast-navigation hint, not a precise locator.

9.F _section_template_{en,ar} — analyst-revision status not assessed

Surface: the two viewpoints templates in §3.6 reproduced verbatim by this revision. Status: UNCLASSIFIED. ADR 0009 §"Viewpoints template language" was drafted at the same time as the initial template code; whether the team considers the rendered prose analyst-revised or starter-draft is not explicitly tracked. Behavioral correctness (the suppression rule, the section ordering, the voices list) is covered by the deterministic renderer's unit tests; the prose register itself is not. Recommended quantification: a one-pass analyst read of the rendered output for a representative day's report would settle this status flag.

9.C PHASE_2A_DOMAIN_ANCHORS lens text — starter drafts

Surface: the seven bilingual lens blurbs in §3.5.1 that feed BOTH the deterministic anchor-similarity router AND the user-side fallback prompt menu. Status: STARTER DRAFT. The in-file commentary at pipeline_c_anchors.py:64-79 is explicit that these are placeholder lens text the production drafts must replace before the Phase 2a router can be considered analyst-revised. Authoritative quantification: no dedicated CLI for this yet; review proceeds against the commentary block's stated length / lens / anti-patterns rules. Coupling note: revising any one row flips the per-prompt content hash for the routing fallback path AND the cache key for the anchor-similarity router (which content-hashes the anchor text for next-tick reload without restart). One edit, two cache invalidations.

9.D Pipeline B Analyst preset framework — drift spot-check pending

Surface: seed_presets.py — the Analyst preset's analysis_framework_ar / analysis_framework_en fields, reproduced in §2.3.3 from the 2026-04-30 snapshot. Status: UNVERIFIED. The prior regeneration's exploration notes mentioned that the Analyst preset had been given its own unique framework, "correcting previous drift" against the default. This audit pass did not re-diff the §2.3.3 text against live seed_presets.py line-for-line. Recommended quantification: dump BUILTIN_PRESETS[*]['analysis_framework_{ar,en}'] and diff against §2.3.3; if any preset's framework text differs from the rendered §2.3.3, file a follow-up to refresh the §2 body via the preserved-verbatim path in the generator.

9.E §6.1 Pipeline B cheat-sheet line numbers — approximate

Surface: the (~line N) markers throughout §6.1 (and a few in §2.1-§2.5). Status: APPROXIMATE. Last fully audited against the 2026-04-30 revision of processing.py / seed_presets.py / seed_categories.py. Subsequent edits to those files may have shifted line numbers without invalidating the cheat sheet's logical correctness. Authoritative locator: file + symbol name (e.g. processing.py — build_preset_system_prompt) is the guaranteed-stable reference. Treat (~line N) as a fast-navigation hint, not a precise locator.

9.F _section_template_{en,ar} — analyst-revision status not assessed

Surface: the two viewpoints templates in §3.6 reproduced verbatim by this revision. Status: UNCLASSIFIED. ADR 0009 §"Viewpoints template language" was drafted at the same time as the initial template code; whether the team considers the rendered prose analyst-revised or starter-draft is not explicitly tracked. Behavioral correctness (the suppression rule, the section ordering, the voices list) is covered by the deterministic renderer's unit tests; the prose register itself is not. Recommended quantification: a one-pass analyst read of the rendered output for a representative day's report would settle this status flag.

18 / 28 · LLM Prompts Source: llm-prompts-reference.md · Tasks #517 + #518 · 2026-05-21 · supersedes docs/llm_analysis_prompts.md (2026-04-30, Pipeline-B-only)
19 / Data & Quality

Pipeline C — Republishing Readiness Audit.

Phase 1, read-only investigation. No code, schema, or data writes performed. Every claim is anchored to a file:line, SQL result, or runtime artifact. Audit run: 2026-05-10. Verdict: NOT READY for republishing — five blockers, four caveats. Source: Pipeline C — Republishing Readiness Audit.

Verdict at a glance

NOT READY. Five blockers: no real-corpus end-to-end run exists; a running row from 2026-05-06 has been stuck for three days; per-topic LLM responses have no JSON-schema validator; domain prompts lack the explicit "no invented sources or quotes" grounding clause; no language-mixing detection / retry path. Four acceptable caveats: no per-day topic cap, no minimum-quality success gate, profile_affiliation not directly consumed, no API stop button on the scheduler. Detail in §8 below.

Convention

SQL results in this section come from the dev/working Postgres — the same DB the running app is bound to in this Replit workspace. Production DB was not queried (Phase 1 hard rule #2). Where dev data is plainly a test fixture it's labelled as such.

§1 — Pipeline C scope and output shape

1.1 Entry point High confidence

Pipeline C's main loop is pipeline_c_scheduler.py (continuous poll loop) and the per-day driver is pipeline_c_processing.run_unified_pipeline_for_date(date_str, ...).

  • pipeline_c_scheduler.py:367 — def _do_one_tick(), single tick of the poll loop; called from the long-running thread started by auto_start_if_enabled() (line 627), invoked unconditionally at app boot from api.py:86-87.
  • pipeline_c_processing.py:765 — def run_unified_pipeline_for_date(date_str, *, trigger="manual", report_id=None, ...), single end-to-end run.
1.2 Input contract — gap surfaced High / Medium confidence

Pipeline C inherits the legacy contract: content, external_url, platform. It does not declare its own validator; it reuses pipeline_shared.prepare_dataframe / filter_duplicates_and_low_confidence from processing.py. The single hard-coded required-columns set is at processing.py:1508:

required_columns = {'content', 'external_url', 'platform'}

Pipeline C's reader does no separate column check. pipeline_c_reader.py (lines 60–97) only validates the date string format and locates the posts.csv key in S3.

Gap surfaced

The five "source-distinguishing" columns the spec calls out — author_display_name, author_username, dashboard_section, profile_affiliation, profile_classification — are not in required_columns. They are read opportunistically downstream. A posts.csv missing those columns will not be rejected at the door — it will silently degrade source-attribution depth.

1.3 Unified-clustering claim — verified High confidence · pipeline_c_processing.py:702-746, 971-979

All posts for the date are clustered together in a single HDBSCAN call on the whole-day, L2-normalised embedding matrix. There is no per-source bucket or per-section pre-split.

  • L971-979 — ordered_urls = df["external_url"].tolist(), then matrix = np.asarray([post_vectors[u] for u in ordered_urls]), then labels, cluster_summary = _hdbscan_cluster(matrix, ...). df at this point is the whole-day frame (filtered ONLY to "rows whose URL has an embedding", L964) — no groupby('dashboard_section') or per-source split exists.
  • L702-746 _hdbscan_cluster: metric="euclidean" (cosine ≡ euclidean on L2-normalised input), cluster_selection_method="eom", min_cluster_size=PIPELINE_C_MIN_CLUSTER_SIZE (default 8), min_samples=PIPELINE_C_MIN_SAMPLES (default 3).
  • Cross-day continuity: pgvector cosine distance against unified_topic_centroids HNSW index, threshold CONTINUITY_DISTANCE_THRESHOLD = 0.30.
  • Domain routing gate: ANCHOR_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.55 AND LLM_FALLBACK_DELTA = 0.05 margin AND LLM_FALLBACK_ABS_FLOOR = 0.45 — all clauses required before the LLM fallback fires.
  • Embedding model: from env, default text-embedding-3-small v1 via EMBEDDING_MODEL_ID / EMBEDDING_MODEL_VERSION.
1.4 Narrative differentiation lives on TWO parallel surfaces High confidence

Per-source narrative is written into two parallel surfaces on the unified_topics row, not into a single viewpoints field:

FieldTypeSource
viewpoints_ar · viewpoints_enjsonb (split per language)Deterministic templater pipeline_c_viewpoints.derive_viewpoints_from_section_analytics (pipeline_c_viewpoints.py:212-277)
key_voices_ar · key_voices_enjsonbLLM, Phase 2c Stage 7 dispatcher (pipeline_c_dispatch.py:393-405)
narrative_ar · narrative_entextLLM Stage 7 — single-prose synthesis cited by per-domain prompts as required to contain cross-section contrast
brief_ar · brief_en · title_ar · title_entextLLM Stage 7

The pre-existing topics.viewpoints column (legacy Pipeline B) is not written by Pipeline C. Pipeline C writes its own unified_topics.viewpoints_{ar,en} instead, on a separate table.

1.5 Source-grouping inputs — consumption matrix Medium confidence — 4 of 5 cited; profile_affiliation gap
ColumnRequired at door?Pipeline C consumption
dashboard_sectionnoDeterministic templater (pipeline_c_viewpoints.py:74-85, 212-277) AND per-domain prompts ("Read each dashboard_section in turn" — pipeline_c_prompts.py:94-95, 140, 182-183)
profile_classificationnoBucketed by _source_influence_tiers(topic_df) (pipeline_c_processing.py:638-660), persisted to unified_topics.source_influence_tiers (JSONB)
author_display_namenoSurfaced through _format_voices (pipeline_c_viewpoints.py:99-127) and via the LLM key_voices dispatcher path
author_usernamenoSame path as display_name
profile_affiliationnono direct read found — rg "profile_affiliation" pipeline_c_*.py returns no hits
Gap surfaced

profile_affiliation consumption is undocumented and unverified. The spec lists it as a source-distinguishing input; the Pipeline C code does not branch on it.

1.6 Output payload diff (legacy vs Pipeline C) High / Medium confidence
New top-level fields
  • pipeline = "unified" · pipeline_run_status · pipeline_run_id = unified-<date>-<hex8>
  • coverage_metadata (additive)
  • bilingual: True · no_data booleans for client routing
  • unique_posts_by_platform — additive top-level per-platform unique post counts
New per-topic fields
  • viewpoints_ar AND viewpoints_en as two separate arrays (legacy ships a single viewpoints)
  • key_voices_ar · key_voices_en
  • momentum ∈ {emerging, rising, stable, declining} and delta_post_count from a strict D-1 lookup against unified_topic_centroids
  • is_carried_forward · prior_topic_post_count · topic_stable_id (cross-day continuity surface)
Existing field whose semantic changed

report_id — legacy uses report_<category>_<date>; unified uses unified-<date>-<hex8> (e.g. unified-2026-05-10-ee75f82c). Namespaced cleanly; clients keying on prefix work without migration.

Drop hazard

The webhook gate (webhook.send_unified_report_webhook L750-760) skips delivery when the assembled payload has topics == 0 AFTER pruning. A run where Stage 7 LLM dispatch wholesale fails — even though Stage 6 persisted topic rows with viewpoints_* populated — silently produces zero webhook delivery and a synthetic failure row in webhook_deliveries.

§2 — Recent file ingestion

2.1 · S3 listing for the last 14 days Medium confidence — credential gap in audit shell, not in runtime

Cannot be executed directly from this audit session. The Replit workspace exposes S3_BUCKET_NAME, S3_REGION, S3_PREFIX as env vars, but no AWS credentials in the shell context (aws CLI not installed; boto3 head_object returns NoCredentialsError).

$ env | grep -i '^S3\|AWS_REGION\|BUCKET' | sed 's/=.*/=REDACTED/' S3_PREFIX=REDACTED S3_REGION=REDACTED S3_BUCKET_NAME=REDACTED $ python -c "import boto3,os; ... boto3.client('s3').head_object(...)" ('2026-05-10', 'reports/<prefix>/reports/2026-05-10/posts.csv', 'MISSING', 'NoCredentialsError')

The running web app does have S3 access at runtime, so the credential is loaded into the worker process from a profile or instance role this audit sandbox does not see.

What CAN be cited from the data plane is the audit table the running ingester writes after each scan: s3_scan_history:

folderscan_statusposts.csv status
fixed_window_2026-05-09_2026-05-09complete"already processed" / "skipped"
fixed_window_2026-05-08_2026-05-08complete"success", batch_id=s3_posts_2026-05-08_posts, rows_csv=36716, rows_kept=0

The S3 file IS present for at least 2026-05-08 and 2026-05-09 (re-encountered 4+ hours later as already processed). The credential gap above is purely an audit-shell limitation, not a runtime gap.

2.2 · Did Pipeline C ingest any of them? High confidence — direct row-level evidence

No. There is no row in unified_reports with posts_csv_key populated for any date in the last 14 days. All terminal runs in the dev DB are smoke-test runs reading a 10-row dev fixture. Live ingestion of a real production posts.csv has not yet completed in this dev environment.

report_datetotalfinishedsucceededrunningscheduledmanual
2026-05-10120120012
2026-05-09160160016
2026-05-08490490049
2026-05-07441440143
2026-05-06190181118
2026-05-051672416700167

Two important observations:

  • total != succeeded + degraded + failed + running for 2026-05-06 (18+1=19 vs row total). That discrepancy is succeeded rows with no finished_at — i.e. the orchestrator wrote status='succeeded' but the _finalise UPDATE to set finished_at did not land. Only 25 of 307 unified_reports rows are finished.
  • The 1 running row is a stuck scheduled run from 2026-05-07 07:30 IL (unified-2026-05-06-ee75f82c) — has been in running state for 2+ days. The retry-budget gate cannot reclaim it because claim_pending_retryable_unified_run_for_date (pipeline_c_scheduler.py:405-407) only claims status='failed' rows; a running row that crashed mid-flight sits there indefinitely.

Live finished-row sample — every one shows the same fixture profile (posts_csv_key=NULL, rows_raw=10, rows_after_filter=10, cluster_count=0):

unified-2026-05-05-a8735066 | succeeded | …07:25:31 | (NULL key) | 10 | 10 | 100 | 0 unified-2026-05-05-f2a14fbf | succeeded | …06:45:05 | (NULL key) | 10 | 10 | 100 | 0 unified-2026-05-05-27de671c | succeeded | …06:08:55 | (NULL key) | 10 | 10 | 100 | 0

The 10-row count is the deterministic test fixture; the count is below the min_cluster_size=8 density floor only just enough to keep clustering from forming.

2.3 · Why was no posts.csv ingested? High confidence — two distinct reasons
Reason a — dev DB driven exclusively by manual smoke-test triggers

All but one of the 307 unified_reports rows have trigger='manual'; the single scheduled run that exists (2026-05-06 target, started 2026-05-07 07:30 UTC) is the stuck running row above. The _df test seam in run_unified_pipeline_for_date (pipeline_c_processing.py:770-771) bypasses the S3 reader entirely; smoke-test workflows (pipeline-c-real-embedding-smoke, ingestion-safety) exercise this seam. This is benign for production — the workflow is dev-only.

Reason b — the scheduled run crashed without recording a terminal status

Leaving the date locked in running and therefore neither retryable nor re-driveable by a fresh scheduled tick. The poll loop's idle-skip clause (pipeline_c_scheduler.py:387-395) exits early when get_terminal_unified_run_for_date(target_date) returns non-null, but running rows are also not reclaimed by the pending-retry path (claim_pending_retryable_unified_run_for_date only claims status='failed'). Result: the date is "in-flight" forever from the scheduler's perspective until an operator manually intervenes.

Reason (b) is a republish-blocker — see Blocker 2 in §8 below.

§3 — Sync & trigger guardrails

3.1 Trigger schedule — continuous poll, not cron High confidence

Pipeline C does not use a cron expression. It uses a continuous poll loop with a wall-clock floor:

  • pipeline_c_scheduler.py:62 — _POLL_INTERVAL_DEFAULT = 300 seconds between wakes (envoverridable: PIPELINE_C_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS).
  • pipeline_c_scheduler.py:69 — _EARLIEST_HHMM_DEFAULT = "05:30" IL (env: PIPELINE_C_EARLIEST_ATTEMPT_HHMM). Before this wall-clock floor, ticks are no-ops (_earliest_attempt_floor_passed at L358-361).
  • The thread is started unconditionally at app boot (auto_start_if_enabled at L627, invoked from api.py:86-87). Disable knob: PIPELINE_C_SCHEDULER_ENABLED=0.

Project-context staleness: the replit.md describes this as "ticks at 10:30 IL with watchdog auto-restart" — that wording is stale. The current implementation fires as soon as the readiness gate clears any time at or after 05:30 IL. No 10:30 anchor exists in the source.

3.2 Retry policy when posts.csv is not yet in S3 High confidence · L305-355

The orchestrator does not block waiting for S3. The readiness gate consults s3_scan_history (NOT a direct S3 head call):

  • report_db.get_completed_folder_for_date(target_date) — gate fails when there is no s3_scan_history row with scan_status='complete' for the date.
  • Embedding coverage % for the date ≥ floor (default _COVERAGE_FLOOR_DEFAULT = 99.0, env PIPELINE_C_READINESS_COVERAGE_FLOOR).
Retry budget on transient orchestrator failure
  • Per calendar date: _MAX_RETRIES_DEFAULT = 6.
  • Spacing: _MIN_RETRY_SPACING_MIN_DEFAULT = 30 minutes.
  • In-tick retry on transient exception: _IN_TICK_BACKOFFS = (30, 120, 600) seconds. Three sub-attempts per tick before recording the attempt as failed.
Escalation gap

No escalation to email/Slack/page. Orchestrator records status='failed' after budget exhaustion; webhook fan-out writes a synthetic failure row; admin tile shows failed_attempt_count. Operators must spot the failure on the admin page.

3.3 Success criterion — the "no quality gate" gap High confidence · L1362, L1315, L1386-1389

The run terminates as soon as Stage 9 (synthesis) returns. Termination conditions in run_unified_pipeline_for_date:

  • succeeded (L1362): orchestrator finished Stage 6, Stage 7, Stage 9, and Stage 9's degraded flag is False.
  • degraded (L1315, L1362): same as succeeded, but synthesis_degraded == True (Stage 9's per-language gate fired iff brief_count<4 AND <50% of topic_count, both clauses required).
  • failed (L1386-1389): outer try caught an unisolated exception.

There is no minimum-quality gate on success. A run with cluster_count=0 (no clusters formed) records as succeeded. A run where Stage 7 fails every topic in both languages also records as succeeded (just with llm_filled_count=0). The webhook gate then drops the delivery (no topics with LLM content), the run still reads as succeeded on the admin strip — this is a real republishing risk, see §8.

3.4 Compared to legacy guardrails High confidence
Guardrails legacy has and Pipeline C lacks
  • Per-source topic cap (MAX_TOPICS=20 per category file in segmented). Pipeline C has no per-day cap at all.
  • Success quality gate. Legacy treats a run with zero usable topics as a hard failure; Pipeline C records succeeded on cluster_count=0.
  • Schema validation at ingestion door for the source-distinguishing columns (Pipeline C only enforces the legacy 3-column contract — see §1.2).
Guardrails Pipeline C introduces and legacy lacks
  • Continuous poll-loop with 99% embedding-coverage readiness gate.
  • Cross-day continuity via unified_topic_centroids HNSW + cosine threshold 0.30.
  • Per-day single-orchestrator with explicit retry budget on unified_reports.failed_attempt_count.
  • Failure isolation: per-topic + per-language try/except in the Stage 7 dispatcher.

§4 — Independent monitoring page

4.1 Page exists — as a tab on the admin dashboard High confidence; "page" is interpretation-dependent

The "page" is a tab on the existing admin dashboard, not a separate top-level route. Source HTML/JS lives inline in api.py between L14978 and L15799.

Admin endpoints (all Depends(require_admin))
MethodPathPurposeapi.py line
POST/api/admin/pipeline-c/runtrigger now (manual)14298
GET/api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}poll a single run14316
POST/api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/redeliverre-fire webhook14325
GET/api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/deliverylast successful delivery time14364
GET/api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/webhook-historylast 5 delivery attempts14389
GET/api/admin/pipeline-c/prompts-content-hashper-prompt sha256 + char_len14412
GET/api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/llm-callspaginated per-call telemetry14610
GET/api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/log-tailbest-effort log tail14634
GET/api/admin/pipeline-c/schedulerthread + watchdog status (read-only)14664
GET/api/admin/dashboard/unified-pipeline-healthstrip tile data14671
Controls NOT present
  • No Stop scheduler button. The module exposes one internally (start_scheduler/stop_scheduler at L607-624) but no API endpoint exposes them. Operators can only disable via env var + redeploy.
  • No explicit Run history list endpoint distinct from per-run polls.
4.2 Independence from legacy pipeline High confidence — ADR 0010 strict coexistence

Yes, Pipeline C and Pipeline B touch independent state. Direct evidence:

  • Separate tables: Pipeline B writes to reports, topics, posts, raw_posts. Pipeline C writes to unified_reports, unified_topics, unified_topic_centroids. Live information_schema confirms both table sets coexist with disjoint columns.
  • Separate scheduler modules: Pipeline B uses s3_automation, sync_scheduler (started at api.py:77-81); Pipeline C uses pipeline_c_scheduler (started at api.py:86-87). Each owns its own thread + watchdog.
  • pipeline_c_reader.py:1-23 module docstring promises it never acquires the Pipeline B s3_automation._state_lock, never mutates _s3_state, never starts the Pipeline B polling thread, and never writes to raw_posts.
  • Separate webhook gates: webhook.send_report_webhook (legacy) vs webhook.send_unified_report_webhook (webhook.py:691-767). Both use the same transport but the gate logic is independent.
  • Manual trigger endpoints are namespaced.

ADR 0010 formalises this as the "strict coexistence rule: Pipeline C never mutates Pipeline B state". The dispatcher, orchestrator, and webhook hook each have an inline comment citing ADR 0010.

§5 — Topic cap and selection metrics

5.1 No per-day topic cap exists High confidence — intentional per project context

There is no per-day topic cap in Pipeline C. rg "MAX_TOPICS|topic.*cap|max_topics|MAX_CLUSTERS" pipeline_c_*.py returns zero hits. Every cluster the HDBSCAN gate emits is classified, persisted, and dispatched to Stage 7.

Bound on dispatch: pipeline_c_dispatch.DISPATCH_MAX_WORKERS = 6 is a concurrency bound, not a topic cap — it limits parallelism, not total count.

This is intentional per replit.md ("MAX_TOPICS=20 is segmented-only; Pipeline C has NO topic cap"). The 2026-05-07 scheduled run held 119 LLM-filled topics in a single run.

Risk

At ≥20 topics, per-topic LLM call cost (~8 model calls per topic, 2 languages × 4 sub-prompts) scales linearly with no operator guard rail. The webhook payload also balloons.

5.2 Cluster selection metrics — what gates publication High confidence
  • HDBSCAN density — min_cluster_size = 8 (env: PIPELINE_C_MIN_CLUSTER_SIZE) and min_samples = 3. Below these floors is HDBSCAN noise (label −1), filtered out before persistence.
  • Domain routing gate — anchor cosine similarity must clear LLM_FALLBACK_ABS_FLOOR=0.45 AND beat the runner-up by LLM_FALLBACK_DELTA=0.05. If neither, an LLM fallback classifier gets a 10-post sample (LLM_FALLBACK_SAMPLE_SIZE=10); if even the LLM returns junk, the cluster is labelled domain_primary='unclassified' and still persisted.
  • Cross-day continuity (informational, not gating) — cosine ≤ 0.30.

There is no minimum source-diversity check, minimum total engagement check, minimum narrative-coherence score, or minimum cross-source corroboration check. ADR 0006 mentions "Topic Cohesion Diagnostics" — these are computed and logged but do not gate publication.

5.3 No ranking-and-cap function High confidence on absence

There is no ranking-and-cap function because there is no cap. The Stage 7 dispatcher iterates the full list returned by db.get_unified_topics(report_id) and submits every one to ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6).

If a per-day cap were introduced, the natural ordering surface already exists: unified_topics.cluster_size column, plus the synthesis prompt already groups topics "by their primary domain … and ordered by cluster size within each domain" (pipeline_c_prompts.py:830-831). Adding a cap would be a one-line ORDER BY cluster_size DESC LIMIT N on the dispatcher query.

5.4 Honest-labelling implication High confidence

The combination of (a) no minimum-quality gate (§3.3), (b) no per-day cap (§5.1), and (c) no source-diversity / engagement / coherence filter (§5.2) means every density-clean cluster becomes a displayed topic — including unclassified-domain clusters.

This violates the spec's "displayed topic must be defensible by data, not just a plausible cluster" principle in two specific cases:

  • A domain_primary='unclassified' cluster with the unclassified per-domain prompt generating generic-discourse copy (pipeline_c_prompts.py:384).
  • A cluster with high single-author share and low cohesion (cohesion diagnostics computed but not gating).

§6 — LLM prompt readiness

6.1 Three call types — replaces legacy four High confidence

Pipeline C has 3 distinct LLM call types plus the inherited domain-routing fallback:

Per-topic per-language analysis (Stage 7)

Driven by pipeline_c_dispatch.py. System prompt comes from pipeline_c_prompts.select_prompt(domain_key, language) (L486-509). The per-domain prompt strings live in the PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS ordered dict (8 domains × 2 languages = 16 prompts):

DomainLines
politicalL91-134
financialL136-176
socialL178-214
healthL216-254
securityL256-298
religiousL300-337
educationalL339-382
unclassifiedL384-…

Module-level assert at L439-441 enforces the 8 keys are exactly DOMAIN_KEYS_WITH_FALLBACK; per-entry assert at L443-446 enforces both languages are non-empty strings — so a missing/empty prompt cannot ship.

Daily synthesis (Stage 9)

PHASE_2C_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT at pipeline_c_prompts.py:823-869 (en + ar), selected by select_synthesis_prompt(language) at L881-895. Output: a JSON object with one key, executive_summary, whose value is the multi-paragraph narrative.

Domain-routing LLM fallback (Phase 2a)

Called from _classify_with_llm_fallback at pipeline_c_processing.py:452-571. System prompt: pipeline_c_anchors.PHASE_2A_FALLBACK_SYSTEM_PROMPT. Threaded through processing.call_llm(..., use_json=True, fast=True, call_type="domain_routing_fallback") (L518-525).

6.2 Cross-check vs legacy 4 prompt types High confidence

The four legacy call types are entirely replaced:

  • Legacy topic_analysis ↔ Pipeline C per-topic per-language per-domain prompt (8 distinct prompts).
  • Legacy topic_analysis_retry ↔ Pipeline C failure isolation in the dispatcher; there is no retry prompt, the topic just fails.
  • Legacy executive_summary ↔ Pipeline C PHASE_2C_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT — same conceptual call, rewritten with cross-topic-threads instruction.
  • New: domain_routing_fallback.

The 8 per-domain plus 1 synthesis = 9 distinct content-hash entries are exposed by /api/admin/pipeline-c/prompts-content-hash.

6.3 Per-source narrative differentiation prompts — quoted High confidence

There is no separate "narrative differentiation" prompt; it's split between deterministic templating (viewpoints) and the per-domain LLM prompt's narrative requirement (key_voices + cross-section narrative).

For viewpoints there is no LLM call — pure Python templater. No hallucination risk surface.

Political prompt boundary clause (pipeline_c_prompts.py:107-112) — quoted
key_voices are named accounts with short distinctive quotes (one short distinctive quote per voice, ≤ 12 words) and source tags. Do not analyse armed-incident specifics — the Security lens covers those — and do not treat religious-actor speech as devotional unless they explicitly speak from a religious institution.
Financial prompt boundary clause (L153-156) — quoted
key_voices are named accounts with quotes. Do not analyse the political contestation over financial decisions as such — that belongs to the Political lens — and do not analyse humanitarian aid distribution unless framed as financial mechanics.
What the prompts do NOT explicitly say
  • "Base ALL analysis exclusively on the provided post data" — the spec asked for this verbatim grounding clause; it is not in any of the 8 per-domain prompts.
  • "No invented sources or quotes" — also absent verbatim.
  • "Attribute every narrative to a named dashboard_section or profile_classification" — present in spirit but not as a hard no-invention rule.
  • "Use empty list if no posts match" — absent.

This is the most material prompt-readiness gap surfaced by the audit. The deterministic viewpoints layer absorbs much of the hallucination risk for the per-section breakdown (template-renders from source_analytics), but key_voices_* and narrative_* come from the LLM with no explicit grounding clause.

6.4 Routing across legacy 19 category prompts → 8 domain prompts High routing; Low content-equivalence

Pipeline C has moved to a unified prompt family of 8 domain prompts, not the 19 category prompts in seed_categories.py. Mapping: cluster centroid → cosine-anchored domain key (one of: political, financial, social, health, security, religious, educational, unclassified) → that domain's bilingual prompt pair.

The 8 domain anchors are themselves built from the 19-category seed (pipeline_c_anchors.py) — anchor centroids are "built lazily from category_prompts, in-process cached".

Whether the 8 domain prompts cover the analytical questions previously spread across the 19 category prompts: no side-by-side question-by-question diff has been performed. Phase 2 work item.

§7 — Hallucination guards

7.1 Grounding constraints at each LLM call site High confidence
  • Per-topic per-domain (Stage 7): see §6.3 — output-discipline + lens-boundary clauses present, but the explicit "base analysis exclusively on the provided posts; do not invent sources or quotes" sentence is missing.
  • Daily synthesis (Stage 9): instructs the model to identify 4-6 cross-topic threads and to "name which topics participate in it and how". No explicit no-invention clause.
  • Domain-routing fallback: _anchors.parse_fallback_response (L532) only accepts a known domain key — invalid responses collapse to UNCLASSIFIED_DOMAIN. This is a hard validator, not a soft prompt clause: a hallucinated domain name cannot ship.
  • No _off_topic_dropped analogue exists in the Pipeline C output shape.
  • No post-hoc validator that rejects responses citing entities not in the input set.
7.2 JSON-schema validation gap Medium confidence — one indirection unverified
  • Stage 7 per-topic: validation is shape-only, not schema. The dispatcher uses processing.call_llm(..., use_json=True) and the response is treated as payload opaquely; persisted via update_unified_topic_narrative(... payload=...). There is no per-call JSON-schema check on, e.g., "title is a non-empty string ≤ N chars; key_voices is a list of {name, quote, source_tag} dicts".
  • Stage 9 synthesis: returns a JSON object with a single key executive_summary. The synthesis worker (pipeline_c_synthesis.run_daily_synthesis_for_run) is responsible for parsing.
  • Domain routing fallback: hard validator, see §7.1.
Real gap

The two LLM call sites that ship copy to clients (Stage 7 narrative + Stage 9 executive_summary) have no visible JSON-schema validation step that would reject a malformed response at the boundary. A subtle malformation (e.g. {"executive_summary_en": "..."} instead of {"executive_summary": "..."}) would either crash or silently produce no-content output.

7.3 No language-mixing detection or retry path High confidence — gap vs legacy

No language-mixing detection or retry path exists in Pipeline C. rg "language.*mixing|arabic.*detect|english.*detect|retry.*language" pipeline_c_*.py returns no hits.

The synthesis prompts each end with a hard final sentence — "All output MUST be in English only" at L846 and "يجب أن تكون كل المخرجات باللغة العربية فقط" at L867 — but these are soft constraints, not enforced retry.

The legacy "Bilingual Language Enforcement" feature (referenced in replit.md as "AI prompts enforce output language with validation and retry mechanisms") lives in the segmented pipeline; no equivalent surface was carried into Pipeline C.

A topic where the AR call returns English text would persist that English text under narrative_ar with no detection.

7.4 Spot-check has not been performed High confidence

Has not been performed and cannot be performed from this audit session against real production data. Two reasons:

  • The dev DB has zero finished runs that ingested a real S3 posts.csv (§2). All 25 finished rows are 10-row smoke-test fixtures with cluster_count=0.
  • The exception is the single 2026-05-07 scheduled run (llm_filled_count=119, embedding_coverage_pct=11.3%). But that run failed the readiness gate semantics — 11.3% coverage is far below the 99% floor — so it ran on a partial corpus and is not representative.

There is no dedicated spot-check artifact (glob '**/spotcheck*' returns none). Phase 2 work item.

§8 — Republishing readiness verdict

Phase 1 of a planned two-phase audit, executed against the dev/working Postgres on 2026-05-10. Read-only investigation. The "verdict" below is the audit author's call to the product owner about whether the system is ready to republish daily reports through the Pipeline C path; production may have rows this audit did not see (see Q10.1 in §10).

NOT READY

Five blockers, four caveats. Each blocker has a minimum fix. The fixes are scoped to Phase 2 work.

Blockers

Blocker 1 · §2

No real-corpus end-to-end run exists. Every finished unified_reports row is a 10-row smoke fixture with cluster_count=0. The single scheduled real-data run (2026-05-07) ran at 11.3% embedding coverage, well below the 99% readiness floor — its 119 topics are not representative. Minimum fix: at least one finished, succeeded-status run on a real S3 posts.csv with embedding coverage ≥ 99%, and a manual spot-check (§7.4) of its per-topic narratives.

Blocker 2 · §2.3

Stuck running row blocks the scheduler for that date. unified-2026-05-06-ee75f82c has been status='running' since 2026-05-07 07:30 UTC — three days at audit time. The poll loop's idle-skip + retry-claim logic does not reclaim running rows, only failed rows. Minimum fix: stale-running detector clause in the readiness gate, or admin endpoint to force-fail a stuck run, or both.

Blocker 3 · §7.2

Per-topic LLM responses have no JSON-schema validator. The two LLM call sites that ship copy to clients have shape-only persistence and no boundary validation. Minimum fix: explicit schema check (e.g. pydantic model) between LLM call return and update_unified_topic_narrative / synthesis writer; failures recorded as topics_with_total_failure for the topic prompt and synthesis_degraded=True for the synthesis call.

Blocker 4 · §6.3 / §7.1

Domain prompts lack the explicit "no invented sources or quotes" grounding clause. Eight per-domain prompts plus the synthesis prompt instruct the analyst what to read but not the bright-line rule against fabrication. Minimum fix: insert one canonical bilingual sentence into each of the 9 prompts; prompts_content_hash surface will detect the edit.

Blocker 5 · §7.3

No language-mixing detection / retry path. A topic where the AR call returns English text persists that English text under narrative_ar. Minimum fix: add the legacy English-side Arabic-detection logic to the dispatcher's per-language path; on detection, retry once with a language-strict reminder, then fail the language as topics_with_partial_failure.

Caveats (acceptable, but worth labelling on the admin tile)

Caveat A · §5.1

No per-day topic cap. Caveat-not-blocker because dev runs show small topic counts (≤120), but a noisy day at scale could double LLM cost. Acceptable to republish without; consider a soft cap of e.g. 30 with cluster_size DESC ordering as Phase 2 follow-up.

Caveat B · §3.3

No minimum-quality success gate. A run with zero usable topics records as succeeded. Acceptable because the webhook fan-out gate prevents an empty payload from being shipped — the client will see no delivery, which is the correct user-visible signal even if the admin tile says succeeded.

Caveat C · §1.5

profile_affiliation not directly consumed. Acceptable — flowing as opaque enrichment is consistent with other pass-through analytics.

Caveat D · §4.1

Scheduler has no API stop button. Operator can disable via env var + redeploy. Acceptable for republish; consider exposing in Phase 2.

§9 — Admin credentials seed audit

Audit only — no seed action performed in Phase 1. Current seed: api.py:61-71 _seed_initial_admin(), called unconditionally at app boot from api.py:71.

Spec a–e requirements scoring High confidence
ReqStatusEvidence / gap
(a) Idempotent seed; if row exists, do nothingFAILThe INSERT … ON CONFLICT (email) DO UPDATE SET password_hash = EXCLUDED.password_hash always overwrites the hash on every restart. Spec says: existing row → do nothing.
(a) If row does not exist, create with env-var password, bcrypt-hashedPASSCode path L67-69 + bcrypt at auth.py:72-73.
(b) Plaintext never logged / committed / persistedPASS_seed_pw is read once into a local; only the hash is persisted; the print line at L70 logs the email only.
