(b) Read once at seed timePARTIALauth.py:24 also reads ADMIN_PASSWORD at module import time, used by verify_password as a legacy fallback. Plaintext lives in process memory for the lifetime of the process.
(c) Role assignment is superadmin (or equivalent)UNDETERMINABLEThe admin_users table has no role column — schema is flat. All authenticated users with @socialstudio.me domain and a row in admin_users are equally privileged.
(d) Admin audit-log row noting the seed eventFAIL_seed_initial_admin does not write any audit row. The print line is stdout only. rg "system_seed" . returns no hits.
(e) Seed survives redeployPASS-with-caveatCalled unconditionally at module-import time. Caveat: because of the (a) failure, "survives" actually means "overwrites on every redeploy" — which masks the survival check.
Env-var name mismatch

Spec's env-var name is SUPERADMIN_INITIAL_PASSWORD. The code reads ADMIN_PASSWORD. Phase 2 seed work needs to either rename the env-var read, or accept both with SUPERADMIN_INITIAL_PASSWORD taking precedence.

Phase 2 seed fix list
  • Switch the ON CONFLICT to DO NOTHING (or guard with a read-then-create pattern).
  • Add a role column to admin_users with superadmin as a value.
  • Read SUPERADMIN_INITIAL_PASSWORD (with ADMIN_PASSWORD as fallback).
  • Write an admin_audit_log row on each real seed action with actor='system_seed'.
  • Drop the auth.py:108-112 plaintext-fallback branch once every admin has a hash row.

Phase 1 hard rule honoured: NONE of the above were executed. No admin_users row was inserted, updated, or deleted. No SUPERADMIN_INITIAL_PASSWORD was read or set.

§10 — Open questions

Items needing input before Phase 2 begins.

  1. Q10.1 — Which DB is "production"? This audit ran against dev/working Postgres. Production may already have completed real runs. If so, the readiness verdict needs to be re-scored against the production unified_reports.
  2. Q10.2 — Independent monitoring "page" interpretation. Pipeline C controls live as a tab on the existing admin dashboard, not a separate top-level route. Acceptable, or do you want a standalone /admin/pipeline-c route?
  3. Q10.3 — Scoping of the spot-check. Once a real-corpus run finishes, what's the sample size per topic — 5 rows? 10? — and what's the pass criterion (no invented entities, no out-of-input quotes, attribution to a named dashboard_section)?
  4. Q10.4 — profile_affiliation consumption. Should the per-source narrative layer distinguish on this column, or is pass-through acceptable?
  5. Q10.5 — Admin role model. Should Phase 2 add a role column with superadmin / admin / viewer distinctions, or keep the current flat model?
  6. Q10.6 — Stale running row reclaim. Phase 2 minimum-fix proposes either a stale-detector or an operator force-fail endpoint. Preference?
  7. Q10.7 — Topic cap policy. Hard cap (e.g. top 30 by cluster_size per day) wired in for Phase 2, soft warning on admin tile only, or no action?
19 / 28 · Pipeline C Audit Source: Pipeline C — Republishing Readiness Audit (2026-05-10) — full doc faithfully covered
20 / Data & Quality

Pipeline C — Canonical Architecture & Webhook Reference.

The operational pipeline doc. Stages, contracts, persistence shape, operator surfaces, webhook contract. Last regenerated 2026-05-21 (Task #511). Every claim carries a file:line citation. Source: pipeline-c-architecture.md. Companion to §01 System Reference (narrative), §16 Data Inventory (field-level), and §20 Client Spec (wire contract).

Status: canonical reference, first issued 2026-05-21 for Task #511. Audience: an operator, engineer, or analyst who needs a single code-grounded reference for the unified-corpus analysis pipeline that runs parallel to the legacy segmented Pipeline B. Every concrete claim below carries a file:line evidence anchor; values sourced from env vars or the DB quote the read site. Items not verifiable from code alone are tagged "unverified — needs runtime inspection" or "unverified — needs data-app team confirmation".

This is the canonical doc. The operator README (docs/unified-pipeline.md) and the cross-stage flow sheet (docs/pipeline-c-flow.md) remain useful for narrative and at-a-glance walks, and the ADR series (docs/adr/0005–0016) remains the authoritative decision record. Where any of those drift from the behaviour cited here, this doc reflects current code as of the date above and they are to be reconciled to it.

---

§1 — Purpose, scope, audience

Purpose. Pipeline C is the unified-corpus analysis pipeline: it takes the whole day's social-media posts (rather than per-category slices the way Pipeline B does), clusters them, classifies each cluster's domain, enriches each cluster with bilingual LLM analysis, runs a day-level synthesis, derives a deterministic per-section viewpoints array, runs a deterministic post-hoc factuality check, and ships the result to the external client app via the existing webhook contract.

In scope of this doc.

- The Pipeline C orchestrator entry point and every trigger surface that invokes it (cron, admin endpoints, recovery sweep, manual re-trigger). - Stages 1–9, by entry-point file:line, inputs/outputs, side-effects, and tunable parameters with current values + definition sites. - The persistence model: every table Pipeline C writes to or reads from, with write site and read site cited. - The webhook contract, reconciled side-by-side with the segmented Pipeline B contract for the four known shape differences. - The admin diagnostic surface inventory. - Known limitations / open items / pending plans.

Out of scope.

- Pipeline B internals (referenced only at §6.3 where reconciliation matters). - Client-side rendering of the webhook payload. - ADR-level decision rationale (links provided). - Prompt text — lives in pipeline_c_prompts.py and the Prompt Authorship Guide; we name prompts here but do not reproduce them. - Any code, schema, or data changes — this is a read-only doc.

Audience. Operators running the system day-to-day, engineers modifying any Pipeline C stage, and the data-app team integrating the webhook payload.

---

§2 — System context

Pipeline C is one of two analysis pipelines maintained in parallel.

Property Pipeline B (segmented, legacy) Pipeline C (unified)
Input substrate Labelled per-category CSVs uploaded via Streamlit admin The day's reports/{date}/posts.csv on S3
Clustering scope Within a single category at a time Whole-day corpus, all categories
Clustering model Embedding + composite consolidation (embedding_clustering.py) HDBSCAN over text-embedding-3-small vectors (pipeline_c_processing._hdbscan_cluster at pipeline_c_processing.py:886)
Domain assignment Folder/category-driven (1 file = 1 domain) Anchor-centroid cosine + LLM fallback (pipeline_c_processing._classify_with_llm_fallback)
Run record reports table unified_reports table (db.py:6778+)
Per-topic table topic_analyses JSONB inside reports unified_topics table (db.py:7020+)
Webhook target CLIENT_APP_URL/api/receive-report via webhook.send_report_webhook (webhook.py:514) Same endpoint, payload built by webhook.build_unified_report_dict (webhook.py:907) and sent through send_report_webhook
Operator surface Streamlit admin (port 5000) for upload + per-topic review FastAPI HTML dashboard (port 8000) for automation, kill-switches, readiness, Pipeline C report tab, Analyzed Clusters tab

Both pipelines share the auth layer (auth.py, JWT, @socialstudio.me domain), the embedding worker (embedding_worker.py) which writes the shared post_embeddings vector store, the webhook_deliveries audit table, and the legacy NLP helpers in processing.py (helper reuse audit recorded in ADR 0006 and docs/unified-pipeline.md §"Helper reuse audit").

The two operator portals and how to navigate them are documented in docs/admin-portals.md (canonical "where do I go to do X?").

---

§3 — Trigger surface

§3.1 Orchestrator entry point

pipeline_c_processing.run_unified_pipeline_for_date at pipeline_c_processing.py:971. Signature:

def run_unified_pipeline_for_date( date_str: str, *, trigger: str = "manual", report_id: Optional[str] = None, _df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None, _post_vectors: Optional[Mapping[str, Sequence[float]]] = None, _anchor_centroids: Optional[Mapping[str, Sequence[float]]] = None, _db_module=None, _now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None, deadline_seconds: Optional[float] = None, _llm_fallback_callable: Optional[Callable[[str, str], str]] = None, forced_low_coverage: Optional[bool] = None, ) -> Dict[str, Any]:

Returns the final unified_reports row as a dict (status, counters, thresholds). The underscore-prefixed kwargs are pure-function injection points used by the test suite — none are wired in production callers.

§3.2 S3 / posts.csv source contract

Pipeline C reads directly from S3 and never consults raw_posts for its corpus.

- Source read: pipeline_c_processing.py:1143-1144 — from pipeline_c_reader import read_posts_csv_for_date then read_out = read_posts_csv_for_date(date_str). - Reader implementation: pipeline_c_reader.read_posts_csv_for_date at pipeline_c_reader.py:308. Locates the dated S3 key via find_posts_csv_for_date, downloads to a tempfile, probes encoding via _read_with_encoding_fallback, applies shared prep + low-conf filter via pipeline_shared.prepare_dataframe and pipeline_shared.filter_duplicates_and_low_confidence. - The reader is read-only with respect to raw_posts, Pipeline B ingest cursors, and S3 folder-state rows. The raw_posts ingestion path (pipeline_c_ingestion.py + s3_automation.py) is an entirely separate code path with its own kill-switch (see §3.5).

Reader knobs:

Env var Default Definition site
PIPELINE_C_NATIVE_SETTLE_MINUTES 30 pipeline_c_reader.py:50
PIPELINE_C_NATIVE_MIN_SIZE_BYTES 1024 pipeline_c_reader.py:51
PIPELINE_C_NATIVE_MIN_ROWS 0 pipeline_c_reader.py:52
§3.3 Scheduler (cron-equivalent)

Continuous poll loop, single daemon thread plus a watchdog that restarts it within 60 s if it crashes (mirrors the email-automation watchdog pattern). All in pipeline_c_scheduler.py.

- Master enable: PIPELINE_C_SCHEDULER_ENABLED read at pipeline_c_scheduler.py:152, default "1". Setting to "0" no-ops the loop (logged at pipeline_c_scheduler.py:1289). - Earliest attempt floor: PIPELINE_C_EARLIEST_ATTEMPT_HHMM read at pipeline_c_scheduler.py:167, default "05:30" Jerusalem-local. - Watchdog loop entry: pipeline_c_scheduler.py:1273 (_watchdog_loop). - Per-tick orchestration: _do_one_tick (within pipeline_c_scheduler.py) calls sweep_missing_days opportunistically before dispatching the day's run, so the same daemon does both the forward run and missed-day recovery.

There is intentionally no fixed-tick PIPELINE_C_SCHEDULE_HHMM knob any more — references in older ADRs (ADR 0006 §8 in particular) are historical (see docs/unified-pipeline.md §"Historical note" for the Task #332 swap from fixed-tick to continuous-poll).

§3.4 Embedding gate

Synchronous, deadline-bounded mop-up of any residual post_embeddings rows for the day's filtered corpus. Runs at Stage 2 (see §4.2) but its trigger-surface knobs belong here.

- Deadline env var: PIPELINE_C_EMBEDDING_DEADLINE_SECONDS read at pipeline_c_processing.py:147, default 300.0 (5 min). - Residual-only floor: PIPELINE_C_EMBEDDING_GATE_MIN_BACKGROUND_COVERAGE read at pipeline_c_processing.py:183, default 95.0 (%). When the async worker's background coverage is below this floor the synchronous gate refuses to run; the orchestrator continues with whatever the worker has and the finalisation rule (Task #403, _decide_finalisation_status at pipeline_c_processing.py:191) may downgrade the run to failed. - Fail-on-low-coverage floor: PIPELINE_C_FAIL_ON_LOW_COVERAGE_PCT read at pipeline_c_processing.py:186, default 80.0 (%).

forced_low_coverage bypass: kwarg on the orchestrator (pipeline_c_processing.py:971, default None). When truthy, inspected at pipeline_c_processing.py:1693 to skip the readiness gate; the _decide_finalisation_status function also returns the base status unchanged when forced_low_coverage is true so a forced run cannot be silently downgraded to failed (pipeline_c_processing.py:221-222). Operators sign a typed-confirmation modal to take ownership of the outcome — see ADR 0012.

§3.5 Admin re-trigger / recovery endpoints

All in api.py:

Endpoint File:line Purpose
POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/run api.py:15057 Manual run (default = yesterday); supports forced_low_coverage query param
GET /api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id} api.py:15267 Poll a run's status
POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/redeliver api.py:15276 Re-fire the webhook for a previously-shipped run
POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/sweep-missing api.py:16069 Manually invoke the missed-day recovery sweep
POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/reap-stale-running api.py:16038 Mark stuck running rows as failed (stale)

Three independent pipeline-control kill-switches (pipeline_c, segmented, raw_posts) are exposed at api.py:16001+ (pipeline_control_status and the start/stop endpoints), backed by an admin_audit_log write per state change (admin_audit_log schema at db.py:6298+). The audit row tagged pipeline_c_promote_topic is also written by the per-cluster promote endpoint at api.py:15991+ and its correctness contract is the Task #459 hardening documented in docs/admin-portals.md § "Pipeline C 'Unified Report' tab — carried-cluster actions".

§3.6 Recovery sweep (auto + manual)

pipeline_c_scheduler.sweep_missing_days at pipeline_c_scheduler.py:672:

def sweep_missing_days( window_days: Optional[int] = None, max_recoveries_per_call: Optional[int] = None, ) -> Dict[str, Any]:

Walks the last N days, finds dates with no terminal Pipeline C run (no succeeded / degraded row), and dispatches a recovery run for each. Called both opportunistically from the scheduler poll loop and directly from the POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/sweep-missing admin endpoint at api.py:16069. The retry-counter cap that excludes abandoned folders is the Task #363 post-mortem behaviour summarised in replit.md ("Stuck S3 folder post-mortem & admin closure").

---

§4 — Stage-by-stage walk

The orchestrator's own comment markers (-- Stage N: …) drive the numbering; "5b" / "5c" / "7'" / "8" / "8b" reflect stages added after Phase 1 that were intentionally not renumbered to preserve commit history readability. See docs/pipeline-c-flow.md for the same ordering rendered as a single sheet.

§4.1 Stage 1 — Corpus assembly

Entry point. pipeline_c_reader.read_posts_csv_for_date at pipeline_c_reader.py:308, invoked by the orchestrator at pipeline_c_processing.py:1144.

Inputs. date_str (YYYY-MM-DD); optional chunksize; apply_segmented_prep (default True).

Outputs. Dict carrying the in-memory dataframe plus rows_raw, rows_read, encoding, and S3 metadata (posts_csv_key, posts_csv_folder).

Side effects. Read-only against S3. Writes a local tempfile and unlinks it. No DB writes at this stage; the run header has already been inserted at pipeline_c_processing.py:1067 (insert_unified_report) and Stage 1's counters update it via update_unified_report at run-finalisation time.

Tunables. See §3.2.

Helper reuse note. pipeline_shared.prepare_dataframe and pipeline_shared.filter_duplicates_and_low_confidence are the same helpers Pipeline B uses, so the input shape is byte-for-byte the same in both pipelines — symmetry is the basis for the Phase 5 parallel-run rubric (docs/unified-pipeline-phase-5-rubric.md).

§4.2 Stage 2 — Embedding gate

Entry point. pipeline_shared.run_embedding_gate at pipeline_shared.py:37, invoked by the orchestrator near pipeline_c_processing.py:1220-1283.

Model. text-embedding-3-small, 1536-d float32. Identifier persisted on the run header as unified_reports.embedding_model_id / embedding_model_version (see §5.1).

Storage. post_embeddings table at db.py:4723-4735: post_url TEXT, model_id TEXT, model_version TEXT, embedding vector(1536), dim INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ; unique on (post_url, model_id, model_version). Write site: embedding_worker.py:515 (upsert_post_embeddings_batch). Read site (the gate's re-fetch): pipeline_c_processing.py:1220 / :1283 via fetch_post_embeddings. The continuity centroid index (unified_topic_centroids HNSW) is documented at §5.3 — that table is not the post-vector store; post_embeddings is.

Operating mode. Residual-only since Task #403: the synchronous gate is allowed to run only when async background coverage is ≥ 95 % (_should_run_gate_residual_mode at pipeline_c_processing.py:257). The async worker (embedding_worker.py) does the bulk write.

Tunables. See §3.4 plus the worker-side knobs documented in docs/unified-pipeline.md § "Embedding worker throughput (Task #378)" — EMBEDDING_WORKER_BATCH_SIZE (256), EMBEDDING_WORKER_MAX_BATCHES_PER_CYCLE (40), EMBEDDING_WORKER_POLL_SECONDS (10), EMBEDDING_CLUSTERING (master flag), EMBEDDING_WORKER_AUTOSTART, EMBEDDING_MODEL_ID, EMBEDDING_MODEL_VERSION.

Outputs. Per-run counters: embedding_requested, embedded_synchronously, already_present, skipped_due_to_deadline, embedding_failed, embedding_coverage_pct (all on unified_reports).

§4.3 Stage 3 — HDBSCAN clustering

Entry point. pipeline_c_processing._hdbscan_cluster at pipeline_c_processing.py:886.

UMAP? No. L2-normalised raw 1536-d vectors are fed directly to hdbscan.HDBSCAN. UMAP is present only in the offline evaluation harness at scripts/eval_pipeline_c_umap.py and is not wired into the production orchestrator. The A/B record lives in docs/pipeline-c-umap-eval.md.

Algorithm. metric='euclidean' over L2-normed vectors (equivalent to cosine), cluster_selection_method='eom'.

Tunables (all read at import time, all env-overridable).

Knob Code-default value Definition site
PIPELINE_C_MIN_CLUSTER_SIZE 35 pipeline_c_processing.py:98-100
PIPELINE_C_MIN_SAMPLES 10 pipeline_c_processing.py:101-103
PIPELINE_C_CLUSTER_SELECTION_EPSILON 0.0 pipeline_c_processing.py:140-142; parser at :116-137

Task #425 flipped both the code defaults and the production / dev env vars to 35 / 10 / 0.0 (the editorial target shape). The final epsilon ≈ 0.10 A/B is still deferred to a ≥99 %-coverage healthy day. See docs/unified-pipeline.md § "HDBSCAN floor raised — Task #380 → Task #425" for the full history.

Output. A numpy.ndarray of int64 labels (length = post count, -1 for noise) plus a summary dict {n_posts, n_clusters, noise_count, noise_pct}. Persisted to unified_reports.cluster_count, noise_post_count, avg_cluster_size at run-finalisation.

§4.4 Stage 4 safeguards audit — explicit answers

The task brief requires explicit yes/no answers grounded in code for three safeguards. Do not import Pipeline B behaviour.

(a) Pre-clustering near-dup detection. Absent in the Pipeline C orchestrator. The only deduplication that runs before HDBSCAN is the basic dup / low-confidence filter inherited from pipeline_shared.filter_duplicates_and_low_confidence (called via the reader, §4.1). There is no cross-corpus embedding-cosine near-dup detection ahead of HDBSCAN.

(b) Post-clustering keyword Jaccard merge. Present as Stage 5b (see §4.6). Threshold default 0.55 (pipeline_c_merge.DEFAULT_THRESHOLD at pipeline_c_merge.py:65); env override PIPELINE_C_JACCARD_MERGE_THRESHOLD (pipeline_c_merge.py:66). Top-K keywords for the Jaccard calculation defaults to 15 (extract_cluster_keyword_sets in pipeline_c_merge.py:276). Deterministic single-pass union-find sorted by (-cluster_size, cluster_label asc).

(c) Centroid-level agglomerative merging. Absent. The pipeline relies on HDBSCAN's EOM selection plus the Stage 5b Jaccard merge. Stage 7's centroid nearest-neighbour lookup (find_nearest_unified_centroid) is a continuity / stable-id inheritance check, NOT a merge — it only assigns a topic_stable_id, it never folds two of today's clusters into one.

§4.5 Stage 4 (orchestrator-numbered) — Domain routing

Entry point. _classify_with_llm_fallback and surrounding loop in pipeline_c_processing.py. The LLM-fallback gate is evaluated at pipeline_c_processing.py:1526+, sample assembly at :1541 / :1551-1553.

Algorithm.

1. Compute spherical-mean centroid per cluster. 2. Score against each of the seven canonical domain anchor centroids (cosine). The seven canonical keys are defined in pipeline_c_anchors.DOMAIN_KEYS at pipeline_c_anchors.py:55-63: political, financial, social, health, security, religious, educational. The anchor texts live in PHASE_2A_DOMAIN_ANCHORS at pipeline_c_anchors.py:81+. 3. Gate the decision: If top_sim ≥ LLM_FALLBACK_ABS_FLOOR AND (top_sim − second_sim) ≥ LLM_FALLBACK_DELTA → route by anchor centroid alone (domain_routing_method = 'centroid'). Otherwise dispatch the gpt-4.1-mini fallback with up to LLM_FALLBACK_SAMPLE_SIZE representative posts in original language. A valid response sets domain_routing_method = 'llm_fallback'. An invalid response leaves the row labelled unclassified (LLM-failure-only sentinel; ADR 0007).

Tunables.

Knob Value Definition site
PIPELINE_C_ANCHOR_SIM_THRESHOLD 0.55 pipeline_c_processing.py:67 (Phase-1 only; superseded by the delta + floor gate but persisted on the run header for historical diagnostics)
PIPELINE_C_LLM_FALLBACK_DELTA 0.05 pipeline_c_processing.py:319-320
PIPELINE_C_LLM_FALLBACK_ABS_FLOOR 0.45 pipeline_c_processing.py:322-323
PIPELINE_C_LLM_FALLBACK_SAMPLE_SIZE 10 pipeline_c_processing.py:328-329

Outputs. Per-cluster: unified_topics.domain_primary, domain_similarity, domain_routing_method (indexed TEXT). Per-run rollup: unified_reports.llm_fallback_topic_count, llm_fallback_total_latency_ms.

§4.6 Stage 5 — Representative sampling

Entry point. pipeline_c_processing._representative_sample_urls at pipeline_c_processing.py:850, which delegates to pipeline_shared.get_representative_sample → processing.get_representative_sample.

Stratification. Diversity across platform / dashboard section, weighted by engagement and authority. Implementation is the shared helper — same behaviour as Pipeline B, audited segment-agnostic (ADR 0006 § "Helper reuse audit").

Sample size. SEGMENTED_REPRESENTATIVE_SAMPLE_SIZE = 40 at pipeline_c_processing.py:847. Held in lock-step with Pipeline B's sample size so Phase 5 parallel-run comparisons are apples-to-apples.

Output. unified_topics.representative_post_urls (JSONB list of up to 40 URLs).

§4.7 Stage 5b — Keyword-Jaccard merge

Entry point. pipeline_c_merge.merge_clusters_by_keyword_jaccard, invoked by the orchestrator at pipeline_c_processing.py:1346-1406 (threshold read at pipeline_c_processing.py:1357).

Algorithm. Deterministic single-pass union-find:

1. Sort clusters by (-cluster_size, cluster_label asc) so the largest wins; ties broken by lowest label. 2. For each pair, compute Jaccard over the top-K keyword sets (K=15 via extract_cluster_keyword_sets). 3. If J ≥ threshold, fold the smaller into the larger; union the keyword sets so the merged cluster competes against subsequent comparisons with its larger keyword footprint.

Threshold. 0.55 default (pipeline_c_merge.DEFAULT_THRESHOLD at pipeline_c_merge.py:65). Env: PIPELINE_C_JACCARD_MERGE_THRESHOLD (pipeline_c_merge.py:66, parser at :69-93). Setting to 0.0 disables; malformed values fall back to default with a warning.

Side effects. Mutates labels and df["__cluster_label"] in-process. Only persisted artefact is unified_reports.merged_cluster_count (counter), written by update_unified_report at run-finalisation.

Audit (cited but not re-run here). The Stage-5b activity audit in docs/pipeline-c-flow.md § "Stage 5b" shows zero folds across the 14-day window ending 2026-05-13 — confounded by the sub-floor coverage period documented in docs/pipeline-c-hdbscan-floor-eval.md. Re-audit gate: 7 consecutive days clearing PIPELINE_C_READINESS_COVERAGE_FLOOR OR the HDBSCAN floor flipping to a healthier shape.

§4.8 Stage 5c — Per-post cluster-assignment persist

Entry point. db.bulk_insert_unified_post_cluster_assignments, invoked at pipeline_c_processing.py:1428.

Purpose. Persists the (report_id, cluster_label, post_url) map so the admin Analyzed Clusters drill-through can show which posts ended up in which cluster even if a later stage crashes mid-loop.

Knobs. None — runs unconditionally, isolated in its own try/except so a persist failure cannot crash the run.

Side effects. Writes to unified_post_cluster_assignments (schema at db.py:7278-7286, see §5.4).

§4.9 Stage 6 — Per-cluster enrichment

Entry point. pipeline_c_processing._cluster_enrichment invoked at pipeline_c_processing.py:1628, which dispatches the segmented helpers processing.extract_topic_data, processing.extract_platform_analytics, processing._aggregate_actors_typed, processing.extract_source_analytics, processing.extract_sentiment_trend, processing.calculate_topic_velocity, plus the per-platform unique post counter and source-influence tier helper.

Side effects. Populates unified_topics.cluster_size, unique_posts_by_platform, actor_counts, source_influence_tiers, representative_post_urls, and the legacy-shape enrichment JSON blob. No LLM call. Written via insert_unified_topic (pipeline_c_processing.py:1642).

Helper reuse. All segmented helpers were audited as segment-agnostic — they read only per-row columns produced by prepare_dataframe and keep no segment-keyed state. The full audit list is in docs/unified-pipeline.md § "Helper reuse audit".

§4.10 Stage 7 — Cross-day continuity & per-language LLM dispatch

This stage has two halves.

§4.10.1 Continuity (stable-id inheritance). db.find_nearest_unified_centroid invoked at pipeline_c_processing.py:1580 finds the nearest historic centroid via the unified_topic_centroids HNSW cosine index (§5.3). When cosine_distance ≤ PIPELINE_C_CONTINUITY_DIST_THRESHOLD (default 0.30, pipeline_c_processing.py:76), the cluster inherits the prior topic_stable_id; otherwise a fresh utopic-{12-hex} id is minted. The centroid is then upserted via db.upsert_unified_topic_centroid at pipeline_c_processing.py:1701 (advances last_observed_date, preserves first_observed_date).

§4.10.2 Per-language LLM dispatch. pipeline_c_dispatch.run_topic_analysis_for_run runs one LLM call per (topic, language) against gpt-5.2 using the domain prompt selected by pipeline_c_prompts.select_prompt. The dispatcher uses a ThreadPoolExecutor with max_workers=6 (pipeline_c_dispatch.py:55). Per-language try/except — an English failure does not block the Arabic call.

The eight prompts. pipeline_c_prompts.PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS (pipeline_c_prompts.py:339+) carries one bilingual entry per key in DOMAIN_KEYS_WITH_FALLBACK (pipeline_c_prompts.py:56):

DOMAIN_KEYS_WITH_FALLBACK = tuple(_anchors.DOMAIN_KEYS) + (UNCLASSIFIED_DOMAIN,) # = ("political", "financial", "social", "health", # "security", "religious", "educational", "unclassified")

That's eight prompts: the seven canonical domains plus the unclassified fallback. The task brief mentioned "nine starter-draft prompts" — code shows eight. (The discrepancy may reflect a historical draft count; current production is eight, enforced by the assertion at pipeline_c_prompts.py:514-516.)

Output. Per-topic LLM columns written by db.update_unified_topic_viewpoints and direct SQL at pipeline_c_dispatch.py:543 / :849: title_{ar,en}, brief_{ar,en}, narrative_{ar,en}, the per-analysis text columns (actors_analysis_*, sentiment_analysis_*, geographic_analysis_*, temporal_analysis_*, platform_analysis_*, source_analysis_*, sentiment_summary_*), and the per-language key_voices_{ar,en} JSONB lists.

§4.11 Stage 8 — Reportable cap (+ Stage 8b quota)

Entry point. pipeline_c_reportable.select_reportable_topics (read site at pipeline_c_reportable.py:308 consumes unified_topics). Composite ranker over engagement, cohesion, and size; soft top-N cap; per-cluster reportable_score, reportable_rank, reportable_engagement_raw/norm, reportable_cohesion_raw/norm, reportable_size_norm written back to unified_topics.

Stage 8b — soft per-domain quota (Task #446, ADR 0016). pipeline_c_quota.apply_domain_quota. Deterministic single pass that promotes the best dropped-tail row from any missing domain provided it clears an adaptive composite-score floor, displacing the lowest-scoring kept row whose domain is over-represented.

Knob Default Notes
PIPELINE_C_QUOTA_ENABLED off (opt-in per Task #448) Single kill switch; accepts true / 1 / yes / on
PIPELINE_C_QUOTA_FLOOR_PERCENTILE 50.0 Clamped to [0, 100]

Persisted on every run regardless of whether the quota was enabled: unified_reports.quota_floor (DOUBLE PRECISION), quota_swap_count (INTEGER), quota_swap_log (JSONB, verbose per-swap with scores / labels / floor). The webhook ships the compact form only — see §6.2.

§4.12 Stage 8 (daily synthesis) — per-language exec summary

Entry point. pipeline_c_synthesis.run_daily_synthesis_for_run in pipeline_c_synthesis.py. One LLM call per language against gpt-5.2, writing unified_reports.executive_summary_{ar,en} via update_unified_report_synthesis (pipeline_c_synthesis.py:269).

Degraded-flag contract (pipeline_c_synthesis.py:8-19, constants at :35-36): per-language runs if AT LEAST one brief is available; flagged degraded when EITHER language produced briefs in fewer than SYNTHESIS_DEGRADED_TOPIC_FLOOR = 4 topics AND fewer than SYNTHESIS_DEGRADED_TOPIC_PCT = 50.0 % of the run's topic count (both clauses required). Flag is observability, not a block.

§4.13 Stage 9 — Viewpoints derivation

Entry point. pipeline_c_viewpoints.derive_viewpoints_from_section_analytics in pipeline_c_viewpoints.py.

Algorithm. Deterministic, no LLM. Bilingual templates rendered over per-section analytics (engagement, dominant emotion, top voices) in the canonical section order anchored on pipeline_c_prompts.SECTION_DISPLAY_ORDER (re-imported by the viewpoints module — docs/unified-pipeline.md § "Task #336 addendum" documents the single source of truth for this taxonomy).

Persistence. Written to unified_topics.viewpoints_{ar,en} (JSONB lists) via db.update_unified_topic_viewpoints. Each entry carries the dashboard section key and a bilingual prose description.

Postmortem cross-reference. The Task #437 placeholder-bullet regression (where viewpoints rendered counter names literally instead of perspectives) is fully documented in docs/pipeline-c-flow.md § "Postmortem — viewpoints data-path placeholder bullets".

§4.14 Stage 10 — Factuality grounding (deterministic)

Entry point. pipeline_c_factuality module (pipeline_c_factuality.py + pipeline_c_factuality_check.py). Post-hoc, no LLM call (Task #359). Writes unified_topics.factuality_pass (BOOL) and factuality_diagnostics (JSONB).

The webhook ships a per-run aggregate factuality_summary block {pass, fail, na} computed at webhook.py:1028-1041 — NOT filtered by is_reportable (operators want visibility for clusters held back by the cap too).

---

§5 — Persistence model

Every table Pipeline C writes to or reads from, with schema location, write site, read site.

§5.1 unified_reports (run headers)

Schema. db.py:6778-6971. Columns enumerated:

- Identity / timing: report_id (TEXT PK), report_date (DATE), status (TEXT), trigger (TEXT), started_at, finished_at (TIMESTAMPTZ). - Ingestion: posts_csv_key, posts_csv_folder, rows_raw, rows_after_filter, ingestion_snapshot_id, post_snapshot_late_arrival_count. - Embedding: embedding_model_id, embedding_model_version, embedding_requested, embedded_synchronously, already_present, skipped_due_to_deadline, embedding_failed, embedding_coverage_pct. - Clustering: cluster_count, noise_post_count, avg_cluster_size, merged_cluster_count (Task #383). - Thresholds (mirror of env-var values at run time): anchor_similarity_threshold, continuity_distance_threshold. - Rollups: total_topic_count, reportable_topic_count, unclassified_count, new_topic_count, carried_forward_topic_count, llm_fallback_topic_count, llm_filled_count, llm_failed_count. - Latency / diagnostics: llm_fallback_total_latency_ms, mean_topic_llm_latency_ms, mean_synthesis_latency_ms, cluster_failure_count, run_flags (JSONB). - Synthesis: executive_summary_ar, executive_summary_en. - Reportable cap / quota: reportable_weights (JSONB), reportable_cap, quota_swap_log (JSONB, verbose), quota_floor, quota_swap_count.

Write sites.

- Initial insert: pipeline_c_processing.py:1067 via db.insert_unified_report. - Final update: pipeline_c_processing.py:2158 via db.update_unified_report. - Synthesis update: pipeline_c_synthesis.py:269 via db.update_unified_report_synthesis.

Read sites. db.get_unified_report at db.py:8313 (admin / poll endpoint); db.get_recent_unified_reports at db.py:8388 (admin dashboard listing).

§5.2 unified_topics (per-cluster rows)

Schema. db.py:7020-7230. Columns:

- Identity: id (BIGSERIAL PK), report_id (TEXT FK), report_date (DATE), cluster_label (INT), topic_stable_id (TEXT). - Cluster shape: cluster_size (INT), domain_primary (TEXT), domain_similarity (FLOAT), domain_routing_method (TEXT, indexed: 'centroid' / 'llm_fallback' / NULL for pre-Phase-2a rows), is_carried_forward (BOOL), is_reportable (BOOL). - Analytics (JSONB): unique_posts_by_platform, actor_counts, source_influence_tiers, representative_post_urls, enrichment. - LLM content (TEXT, per-language): title_{ar,en}, brief_{ar,en}, narrative_{ar,en}, actors_analysis_{ar,en}, sentiment_analysis_{ar,en}, geographic_analysis_{ar,en}, temporal_analysis_{ar,en}, platform_analysis_{ar,en}, source_analysis_{ar,en}, sentiment_summary_{ar,en}. - Bilingual lists (JSONB): key_voices_{ar,en}, viewpoints_{ar,en} (per-language — Task #334 / #335). - Factuality: factuality_diagnostics (JSONB), factuality_pass (BOOL). - Reportable scoring: reportable_score, reportable_rank, reportable_engagement_raw, reportable_engagement_norm, reportable_cohesion_raw, reportable_cohesion_norm, reportable_size_norm.

Write sites. Bulk insert at pipeline_c_processing.py:1642 (db.insert_unified_topic); LLM enrichment updates at pipeline_c_dispatch.py:543 and :849 (via db.update_unified_topic_viewpoints and direct SQL).

Read sites. db.get_unified_topics at db.py:9996 (main reader for webhooks and admin UI); orchestrator internal passes at pipeline_c_readiness.py:635, pipeline_c_reportable.py:308, pipeline_c_synthesis.py:154.

§5.3 unified_topic_centroids (continuity index)

Schema. db.py:4575-4600:

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS unified_topic_centroids ( topic_stable_id TEXT, report_id TEXT, report_date DATE, centroid vector(1536) NOT NULL, post_count INT, domain_primary TEXT, first_observed_date DATE, last_observed_date DATE, model_id TEXT, model_version TEXT );

Plus:

- Unique index uq_unified_topic_centroids_topic at db.py:4590-4592. - Date index idx_unified_topic_centroids_report_date at db.py:4593-4594. - HNSW cosine index idx_unified_topic_centroids_ann_hnsw at db.py:4626 (USING hnsw (centroid vector_cosine_ops)).

Column-contract guard _verify_unified_topic_centroids_contract at db.py:4513 asserts the required column set (_REQUIRED_UNIFIED_TOPIC_CENTROIDS_COLUMNS at db.py:4505) after init_unified_topic_centroids_db returns.

Write site. pipeline_c_processing.py:1701 via db.upsert_unified_topic_centroid.

Read site. pipeline_c_processing.py:1580 via db.find_nearest_unified_centroid (cosine NN through the HNSW index).

§5.4 unified_post_cluster_assignments (drill-through)

Schema. db.py:7278-7286. report_id (TEXT FK), report_date (DATE), cluster_label (INT), post_url (TEXT), post_id (BIGINT). PK on (report_id, post_url).

Write site. pipeline_c_processing.py:1428 via db.bulk_insert_unified_post_cluster_assignments.

Read site. db.get_unified_post_cluster_assignments at db.py:7361 — admin drill-through (Analyzed Clusters tab and GET /api/admin/pipeline-c/topics/{topic_id}/posts).

§5.5 Viewpoints

There is no separate unified_viewpoints table. Per-language viewpoint arrays are persisted as JSONB columns directly on unified_topics: viewpoints_ar and viewpoints_en (db.py:7131-7133). The task brief's reference to unified_viewpoints reflects the conceptual entity, not a physical table. Written by db.update_unified_topic_viewpoints; read via db.get_unified_topics.

§5.6 post_embeddings (shared vector store)

Schema. db.py:4723-4735. post_url TEXT, model_id TEXT, model_version TEXT, embedding vector(1536), dim INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Unique on (post_url, model_id, model_version).

Write site. embedding_worker.py:515 via db.upsert_post_embeddings_batch.

Read sites. pipeline_c_processing.py:1220 and :1283 via db.fetch_post_embeddings.

This table is shared with Pipeline B. Flipping EMBEDDING_MODEL_ID / EMBEDDING_MODEL_VERSION invalidates both the anchor cache (Stage 4) and the historic centroid HNSW index (Stage 7 continuity) — coordinate via the embedding rotation playbook (ADR 0003 § "Status amendment").

§5.7 webhook_deliveries (delivery audit)

Schema. db.py:4167-4180. id SERIAL PK, report_id TEXT, attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, success BOOLEAN, http_status INT, error_msg TEXT, payload_bytes INT, origin TEXT (free-text marker — Task #452, e.g. "manual_promote").

Write site. webhook.py:507 (db.record_webhook_delivery / db.insert_webhook_delivery) inside webhook._record_delivery at webhook.py:489-512.

Read site. Admin webhook history endpoint at api.py:15341.

§5.8 admin_audit_log (operator overrides)

Schema. db.py:6298-6305. id, event (TEXT), actor (TEXT), target_email (TEXT), details (JSONB), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ).

Pipeline C usage. Not written by pipeline_c_processing.py itself. Written by api.py at the admin layer for manual triggers (api.py:15233), promote actions tagged pipeline_c_promote_topic (api.py:15991+; Task #459 correctness contract), and kill-switch state changes (around api.py:16001+).

---

§6 — Webhook contract

§6.1 Consumer side /api/receive-report

Hosted on the external client app, not in this repository. There is no route definition for /api/receive-report in api.py or elsewhere in this repo. Configuration:

- Target base URL: CLIENT_APP_URL env var (read in webhook.py:523). - Auth: WEBHOOK_API_KEY shipped as the x-api-key HTTP header (webhook.py:524). - Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8.

Auth, idempotency, retry, TLS — server-side facts only:

- Health check: webhook._check_health at webhook.py:477-486 hits CLIENT_APP_URL/api/webhook/health. Skipped per-attempt only in bulk callers (skip_health_check=True). - Idempotency: not implemented at the transport layer (no X-Idempotency-Key). Idempotency relies on the deterministic id field — same value on retry, same value on manual redeliver. The client app's behaviour at the consumer side is unverified — needs data-app team confirmation (consumer code is out of this repo). - Retry: MAX_RETRIES = 3, RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS = 10 (webhook.py module constants). HTTP 413 (Payload Too Large) is treated as a permanent failure and is not retried (Task #405 history captured in docs/webhook-payload-budget.md). - TLS: webhook._ssl_verify at webhook.py:70-139 disables verification specifically for *.replit.app hosts (Replit egress proxy MITM workaround). Other hosts use system trust. - Delivery logging: every final outcome (success or failure) is recorded in webhook_deliveries via _record_delivery (webhook.py:489-512) → see §5.7.

§6.2 Pipeline C payload shape (quoted from assembly site)

Assembled by webhook.build_unified_report_dict at webhook.py:907-1181. The full top-level envelope:

return { "id": unified_report.get("report_id"), "pipeline_run_id": pipeline_run_id, # = report_id (Task #333) "coverage_metadata": coverage_metadata, # {embedding_model_id, embedding_model_version} "report_date": report_date, "category": UNIFIED_CATEGORY_KEY, "category_name": UNIFIED_CATEGORY_NAME_EN, "category_name_ar": UNIFIED_CATEGORY_NAME_AR, "topics": topics_ar, "topics_ar": topics_ar, "topics_en": topics_en, "executive_summary_ar": exec_ar, "executive_summary_en": exec_en, "executive_summary": exec_ar or exec_en, "total_posts": total_posts, "unique_posts_by_platform": unique_by_plat, "status": "approved", "approved_at": unified_report.get("finished_at") or "", "bilingual": True, "no_data": len(topics_ar) == 0, "pipeline": "unified", "pipeline_run_status": unified_report.get("status"), "total_topic_count": int(unified_report.get("total_topic_count") or 0) or total_with_title, "reportable_topic_count": int(unified_report.get("reportable_topic_count") or 0) or reportable_with_title, "preview_includes_unreportable": bool(include_unreportable), "factuality_summary": factuality_summary, # {pass, fail, na} "quota_floor": quota_floor, "quota_swap_count": quota_swap_count, "quota_swap_log": quota_swap_log_compact, # [{promoted_domain, displaced_domain}, ...] "forced_low_coverage": forced_low_coverage, "coverage_at_trigger": coverage_at_trigger, }

Per-topic shape comes from _unified_topic_to_segmented_pair at webhook.py:677+ → fed through _merge_topic at webhook.py:160+ inside build_webhook_payload (webhook.py:393-474) when the top-level dict round-trips through the shared envelope builder. Key per-topic fields:

- Identity / title: original_topic, title, title_en. - Narrative bilingual pair: brief / brief_en, narrative / narrative_en, plus the per-analysis text columns. - key_voices (AR) + key_voices_en — per-language, no fallback between them (Task #334, webhook.py:185-191). - viewpoints (AR) + viewpoints_en — per-language, no fallback (Task #335, webhook.py:192-203). - domain_routing_method and engaged_sections — Pipeline C convergence-round additive fields (Task #333, webhook.py:225-226). - actors, actors_typed, actors_structured, hashtags, postCount, posts, source_urls, source_analytics, mentions, sentiment, sentiment_summary, keywords, locations, emotions. - visualization removed from the wire payload (Task #405) — the UI-internal duplicate that drove 413 rejections; see docs/webhook-payload-budget.md.

Quota log on the wire is the compact form (promoted_domain + displaced_domain only — webhook.py:1084-1091); the verbose form with per-swap scores lives only in unified_reports.quota_swap_log server-side. See docs/admin-portals.md § "Webhook payload — compact vs verbose quota_swap_log (Task #463)" for the rationale.

§6.3 Pipeline B vs C reconciliation — the four breakage fields

The task brief calls out four documented shape differences. Here are both sides side by side, with code anchors.

(a) Date casing.

Pipeline B (segmented) Pipeline C (unified)
Top-level envelope field reportDate (webhook.py:431, inside build_webhook_payload) report_date (webhook.py:1128, inside build_unified_report_dict)
Persisted column reports.report_date (DATE) unified_reports.report_date (DATE)

Pipeline B's envelope uses camelCase reportDate; Pipeline C's envelope uses snake_case report_date. Both pipelines persist the column as report_date; the camelCase only appears on the segmented wire payload. Reconciliation status: documented difference, no fix shipped. Downstream client must accept both keys for the same logical date (unverified — needs data-app team confirmation that they do).

(b) Post-count field naming.

Pipeline B Pipeline C
Top-level total total_posts (webhook.py:439) — sourced from report.get("total_posts", 0) total_posts (webhook.py:1138) — sourced from sum(row.cluster_size for row in shipped topics) (webhook.py:984)
Per-topic count postCount (webhook.py:177) — sourced from topic_ar.stats.total_posts Same per-topic key name (postCount via _merge_topic) but populated from cluster_size on the unified topic row
Top-level per-platform unique_posts_by_platform (webhook.py:440) — _unique_posts_by_platform_from_topics(topics_ar) if not pre-set unique_posts_by_platform (webhook.py:1139) — accumulated per-cluster from unified_topics.unique_posts_by_platform (webhook.py:985-997)

Field names line up; the semantics differ by construction: Pipeline B's total_posts is a CSV-row count from the segmented report; Pipeline C's is the sum of shipped clusters' sizes. A cluster-noise post in Pipeline C is excluded from total_posts by definition. This is a known semantic skew and is part of why the Phase 5 parallel-run rubric compares per-topic-shape, not headline totals (docs/unified-pipeline-phase-5-rubric.md).

(c) Hashtag shape.

Pipeline B Pipeline C
Per-topic field hashtags (webhook.py:176) — topic_ar.get("hashtags", {}).get("trending", {}) — i.e. a flat dict {tag: count} extracted from the nested {trending: {tag: count}} shape Same key, same extraction — populated upstream by the cluster enrichment helper from enrichment.hashtags.trending (assembled in _unified_topic_to_segmented_pair at webhook.py:800: "hashtags": enrichment.get("hashtags", {}))

Pipeline B's per-topic upstream stores the nested {trending: {...}} shape; Pipeline C's per-topic upstream (the segmented enrichment JSON the cluster-enrichment helper writes) stores the flat dict directly. Both arrive at the wire as {tag: count} because the _merge_topic projector at webhook.py:176 unwraps the nested case for Pipeline B and the flat case passes through. Reconciliation status: same wire shape, different upstream shapes; the wire compact projection at webhook.py:176 is the canonical adapter.

(d) Executive-summary field names.

Pipeline B Pipeline C
Top-level keys overallNarrative + overallNarrativeEn (webhook.py:437-438) — sourced from executive_summary_ar / executive_summary_en (with a legacy fallback to executive_summary) executive_summary_ar + executive_summary_en + executive_summary (webhook.py build_unified_report_dict envelope) — direct column copy, plus a legacy alias executive_summary = AR-or-EN-first-non-empty

Pipeline B's envelope ships under camelCase overallNarrative keys; Pipeline C's envelope ships under snake_case executive_summary_{ar,en} keys. Both pipelines persist the underlying text under the same snake_case column names. Reconciliation status: documented difference, no fix shipped. Downstream client must accept both naming conventions (unverified — needs data-app team confirmation).

§6.4 Plan B additive fields — current status

The four pending additive envelope fields from "Plan B" (the convergence round). All wired in production as of Task #333 / Task #450:

Field Wire status Source
pipeline_run_id Wired (webhook.py:1126, also :454 for segmented passthrough) Alias of unified_report.report_id
coverage_metadata Wired (webhook.py:1127, also :456 for segmented passthrough) {embedding_model_id, embedding_model_version}
domain_routing_method Wired at per-topic level (webhook.py:225, :828) Per-topic unified_topics.domain_routing_method
engaged_sections Wired at per-topic level (webhook.py:226, :829) Ordered dashboard-section keys actually engaged (webhook.py:722-744)

Plus the Task #450 provenance envelope fields all wired at webhook.py:464-472: quota_floor, quota_swap_count, quota_swap_log (compact), forced_low_coverage, coverage_at_trigger.

§6.5 Plan C — per-language key_voices split

Fully deployed. key_voices (AR) and key_voices_en are persisted on unified_topics as separate JSONB columns (db.py:7131-7133 area), populated by the per-language LLM dispatcher (pipeline_c_dispatch.py:543 / :849), and shipped on the wire at webhook.py:190-191 (_merge_topic) and webhook.py:836-837 / :872 / :892 (_unified_topic_to_segmented_pair). No fallback between the two languages — an AR-only LLM success ships key_voices_en: [] and vice versa.

The Task #382 key_voices JSON shape contract + recovery normaliser (replit.md operational-hardening roll-up) governs the per-language list's element shape.

§6.6 Auth / idempotency / retry / delivery logging

Summarised at §6.1 above; full code references:

- Auth header: webhook.py:524 (x-api-key). - Idempotency: deterministic id field, no transport-layer idempotency key. Client-side behaviour unverified. - Retry: MAX_RETRIES = 3, RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS = 10; 413 is permanent. - Delivery logging: every final outcome to webhook_deliveries via _record_delivery at webhook.py:489-512; admin history at api.py:15341.

§6.7 Resolution — viewpoints assembled-vs-delivered

Resolved: viewpoints is delivered. Both per-language arrays are persisted (unified_topics.viewpoints_ar / viewpoints_en, written by db.update_unified_topic_viewpoints), pulled by _unified_topic_to_segmented_pair at webhook.py:838-839, attached to the per-topic AR / EN pair at webhook.py:873 (AR-side viewpoints) and :893 (EN-side viewpoints), and shipped to the wire by _merge_topic at webhook.py:202-203 under the viewpoints (AR) / viewpoints_en keys.

There is no drop between the assembly site and the wire. Empty arrays (a topic whose engaged_sections was empty, or a pre-Task-#335 segmented report) ship as [] — additive contract, never crashes a client that ignores unknown keys (webhook.py:192-201).

---

§7 — Admin diagnostic surface

All route handlers live in api.py. JS function names cited below are the surfacing components defined inside the FastAPI HTML fragments served by the dashboard.

Surface Endpoint (file:line) UI / JS surfacing
Per-run log-tail viewer get_pipeline_c_run_log_tail at api.py:15825 renderPipelineCRunLogTail(reportId) (Pipeline C Report tab)
Raw LLM round-trip viewer get_pipeline_c_run_llm_calls at api.py:15801 renderPipelineCRunLLMCalls(reportId)
Webhook delivery history tile get_pipeline_c_webhook_history at api.py:15341 renderWebhookHistoryTile(reportId)
Prompts content-hash tile get_pipeline_c_prompts_content_hash at api.py:15603 renderPromptsContentHashTile(); content hash from pipeline_c_prompts.prompts_content_hash()
Prompts markdown export get_pipeline_c_prompts_markdown at api.py:15615 "Export Prompts (MD)" button; backed by pipeline_c_prompts.render_markdown()
Unified-reports admin view get_unified_report_html at api.py:20625 Pipeline C Report tab + Analyzed Clusters tab (docs/admin-portals.md §§ "Analyzed Clusters tab (Task #502)" / "Pipeline C 'Unified Report' tab")
Kill-switches tile pipeline_control_status at api.py:16001 renderPipelineKillSwitches() — three independent toggles for pipeline_c / segmented / raw_posts
Pre-publish readiness checklist get_pipeline_c_readiness_summary at api.py:16292 renderReadinessChecklist() — 10-item greenlight gate
Recovery sweep tile sweep_pipeline_c_missing_days at api.py:16070 (POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/sweep-missing at api.py:16069) renderRecoverySweepTile()
Phase 5 parallel-run measurements get_phase5_measurements at api.py:15662 renderPhase5MeasurementDashboard()
Analyzed Clusters list GET /api/admin/pipeline-c/analyzed-clusters (handlers in api.py; cross-ref docs/admin-portals.md §"Analyzed Clusters tab") Sidebar button; lazy-loaded loadAnalyzedClustersTab()
Per-cluster detail / post drill GET /api/admin/pipeline-c/topics/{topic_id}/detail and /posts Analyzed Clusters drill-through
Promote carried cluster POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/topics/{topic_id}/promote Pipeline C Report tab "Promote & analyze" button; Task #459 correctness contract (docs/admin-portals.md)

Per-run log capture mechanism. pipeline_c_log_capture.py — thread-local buffer flushed to the pipeline_c_logs table on run completion / failure ("unverified — needs runtime inspection" for the exact table schema; module exists at 105 lines).

Raw LLM round-trip storage. Captured by the Stage 7 dispatcher (pipeline_c_dispatch.py) into the unified_llm_calls table keyed by report_id ("unverified — needs runtime inspection" for the exact schema; surfaced by renderPipelineCRunLLMCalls).

Analyst-revision CLI. pipeline_c_prompt_revision script ("unverified — needs runtime inspection" for the exact path; referenced by the admin-surface inventory but not present as a top-level .py module — likely lives under scripts/).

---

§8 — Known limitations and honest gaps

1. Stage 5b Jaccard merge has been effectively dormant. Across the 14 days ending 2026-05-13, merged_cluster_count > 0 on zero runs (docs/pipeline-c-flow.md § "Stage 5b" audit). The merge step continues to pay for itself as a safety net, but it has not been a load-bearing quality lever during the sub-floor coverage period.

2. The HDBSCAN cluster_selection_epsilon ≈ 0.10 target A/B is deferred until a single ≥99 %-coverage day is available. Code default and prod env vars are pinned at 0.0 (pipeline_c_processing.py:140-142).

3. Embedding-gate residual-only contract assumes async-worker throughput keeps up. When background coverage stays below PIPELINE_C_EMBEDDING_GATE_MIN_BACKGROUND_COVERAGE (95 %), the synchronous gate refuses to run and the orchestrator may produce zero reportable topics, in which case _decide_finalisation_status downgrades the run to failed. The watch tile is Embed/min vs Ingest/min in the admin dashboard.

4. raw_posts ingestion is a separate code path with its own kill-switch. Pipeline C does not depend on it for its corpus (Stage 1 reads S3 directly), but the daily-summary client endpoint does — see replit.md "raw_posts Ingestion" capability bullet.

5. Webhook idempotency is id-based only. No transport-layer idempotency key. Client-app behaviour on duplicate id is unverified from this repo.

6. Per-topic field naming differences (camelCase vs snake_case) between Pipeline B and Pipeline C envelopes are unresolved (§6.3). Reconciliation deferred.

7. pipeline_c_logs and unified_llm_calls table schemas are not enumerated here (§7 notes). Both surfaces work in production but the precise DDL was not verified for this doc.

8. pipeline_c_prompt_revision analyst-revision CLI path was not located in the repo top level. Likely under scripts/; unverified.

9. Eight prompts vs nine. The task brief mentioned "nine starter-draft prompts"; the current code base ships eight (seven canonical domains + unclassified fallback) enforced by the assertion at pipeline_c_prompts.py:514-516. If a ninth was ever drafted, it did not land in PHASE_2B_DOMAIN_PROMPTS.

10. Pipeline B vs C total_posts semantic skew. Pipeline B reports CSV-row count; Pipeline C reports the sum of shipped cluster sizes. Documented (§6.3) but not reconciled.

---

§9 — Open items and pending plans

- Plan A / B / C convergence work. Plan B's four additive envelope fields and Plan C's per-language key_voices split are all wired (§6.4 / §6.5). Plan A's remaining scope is unverified from code alone and is not listed as outstanding in replit.md's capability map — treat as closed pending data-app team confirmation. - Phase 5 parallel-run measurement window. Active. Tile lives at api.py:15662 (renderPhase5MeasurementDashboard); rubric in docs/unified-pipeline-phase-5-rubric.md. The measurement is ongoing because the editorial team continues to compare Pipeline B and Pipeline C outputs day by day before any retirement decision. - Analyst-revision prompt pass. A CLI surface (pipeline_c_prompt_revision) is referenced from the admin tile inventory (§7) but is not located in the repo top level — likely resides under scripts/. Audit deferred. - HDBSCAN epsilon ≈ 0.10 A/B and the ~35/10 healthy-day rerun. Both wait on PIPELINE_C_READINESS_COVERAGE_FLOOR clearing for ≥3 consecutive days (docs/pipeline-c-hdbscan-floor-eval.md). - PIPELINE_C_QUOTA_ENABLED retirement. Whether to retire the env toggle once the soft per-domain quota proves stable is an open question recorded in ADR 0016. - Naming reconciliation (date casing, executive-summary key names) between B and C envelopes. Deferred (§6.3).

---

§10 — Changelog

2026-05-21 — Initial canonical issue (Task #511).

- New file. No prior docs/pipeline-c-architecture.md existed in the repo before this issue; the audit confirmed the file was absent. - Code-grounded audit of every Pipeline C invocation path, all pipeline stages (1–10 including 5b / 5c / 8b), the persistence model, the webhook contract (with side-by-side reconciliation of the four documented Pipeline B vs C breakage fields), and the admin diagnostic surface. - §6.7 resolves the open viewpoints question with a "yes, it is delivered" answer grounded in code citations. - §4.4 explicitly answers the three safeguard questions: (a) absent, (b) present as Stage 5b at threshold 0.55 / K=15, (c) absent. - Items unverifiable from code alone are tagged inline rather than guessed: pipeline_c_logs and unified_llm_calls table schemas (§7); the pipeline_c_prompt_revision CLI path (§7 / §9); the external /api/receive-report consumer's idempotency handling (§6.1). - One discrepancy with the task brief noted in §8.9: the brief referenced "nine starter-draft prompts"; current code ships eight (assertion at pipeline_c_prompts.py:514-516).

Source files consulted (read or grep-cited):

- pipeline_c_processing.py (orchestrator, gate, clustering, domain routing, finalisation status, env knob reads) - pipeline_c_reader.py (Stage 1 S3 reader) - pipeline_c_scheduler.py (cron, sweep, watchdog) - pipeline_c_merge.py (Stage 5b Jaccard merge + threshold) - pipeline_c_dispatch.py (Stage 7 per-language LLM dispatch) - pipeline_c_synthesis.py (Stage 8 daily synthesis + degraded contract) - pipeline_c_viewpoints.py (Stage 9 deterministic viewpoints) - pipeline_c_factuality.py / pipeline_c_factuality_check.py (Stage 10 deterministic grounding check) - pipeline_c_anchors.py (seven canonical domain keys + anchor texts) - pipeline_c_prompts.py (eight per-domain prompts, content hash, selection contract) - pipeline_c_quota.py (Stage 8b soft per-domain quota) - pipeline_c_reportable.py (Stage 8 reportable cap) - pipeline_c_log_capture.py (per-run log tail) - pipeline_shared.py (embedding gate, shared prep/filter helpers) - embedding_worker.py (post_embeddings writer) - db.py (schemas + write/read helpers for every Pipeline C table) - webhook.py (segmented + unified envelope builders, delivery logging, retry, TLS) - api.py (admin endpoints, kill-switches, readiness, sweep, promote) - docs/unified-pipeline.md, docs/pipeline-c-flow.md, docs/admin-portals.md, docs/adr/0005–0016 (orientation only; every claim re-verified against code) - replit.md (System Architecture capability map for cross-check against current code)

20 / 28 · Pipeline C Architecture Reference Source: pipeline-c-architecture.md · Task #511 · 2026-05-21 regeneration · every claim file:line-cited
21 / Data & Quality

Embedding Topology — One Corpus, Filter, Then Cluster.

A Pipeline C decision record (ADR-style) for the ingestion / embedding stage and the clustering-topology question it touches. Embed the complete day's corpus as a single file, then filter by platform and locale, then run HDBSCAN on the filtered slices — chosen over splitting the source before embedding. The two paths produce identical vectors and identical clusters on the same slices, so the choice was made on operational and architectural grounds, not embedding quality. Companion to §19 Pipeline C Architecture and §18 Pipeline C Audit. Source: pipeline-c-embedding-corpus-decision.md.

Status: Decided · Scope: Pipeline C (unified corpus) — ingestion / embedding stage and the · clustering-topology question it touches. · Suggested placement: next free ADR number (ADRs 0005–0010 exist) or · docs/pipeline-c-embedding-strategy.md. · Constants referenced are canonical as of this record — see Appendix A.

§0 — TL;DR

DECISION

Embed the complete day's corpus as a single file (one canonical vector per post, persisted to pgvector), then apply platform (FB / T) and locale (PS / INT / IL) filters, then run HDBSCAN clustering on the filtered slices.

This was chosen over splitting the source into per-(platform × locale) files before embedding. The two paths produce identical embedding values and identical clusters when fed the same slices — so the choice was made on operational and architectural grounds, not on embedding quality.

---

§1 — Context — the question we resolved

Two candidate ingestion shapes were on the table:

  • Scenario 1 — Embed unified, filter, then cluster. One complete posts.csv → embed the whole corpus → filter by platform/locale → cluster each slice.
  • Scenario 2 — Split first, then embed. Source pre-split into posts_PS_FB.csv, posts_PS_T.csv, posts_IL_FB.csv, … → embed each split file separately → cluster each.

The load-bearing technical question underneath both was:

*Is the embedding of a post dependent on the other posts in its file, or independent per post? Would a post's vector change between a large corpus file and a split file?*

---

§2 — Core mechanical finding — embedding is per-post independent

VERDICT — Embedding independence

The embedding of a post is a pure function of that post's own text (plus the model weights and requested dimensionality). It takes no input from the other rows in the file. File composition has zero causal influence on the number. Splitting vs. unifying does not change a post's vector.

Why this holds, mechanically:

  • text-embedding-3-small encodes each input string on its own — one post in, one 1536-dim vector out.
  • There is no corpus-level statistic in the encoding: no IDF, no document-frequency weighting, no batch-level normalization that mixes one post into another.
  • A transformer embedding model uses within-sequence attention only. Post A's tokens never attend to Post B's tokens, even when A and B ride in the same batch of a single API call. Batching is a throughput convenience server-side, not a semantic operation.
  • The pipeline's L2 normalization is per-vector (each vector divided by its own norm), so the normalization step reintroduces no corpus dependence either.

2.1 The only source of non-identity (and why it is irrelevant here)

VERDICT — Non-determinism is call-level, not corpus-level

Bit-for-bit equality across two separate API calls is not contractually guaranteed. The same string sent twice can differ at roughly the 1e-6–1e-7 level per component (frequently identical). The cause is backend floating-point behavior — GPU reduction order, server-side batching/padding, mixed precision — and it is call-to-call variance, not file-composition variance. You would see the same jitter embedding the same lone post twice in two runs of the same scenario.

Practical consequence for us: any difference observed between "separate file" and "single corpus file" is attributable to it being a second API call, not to the corpus around the post. And because we embed once and persist to pgvector (model_id / model_version stamped), the canonical vector is reused downstream — the jitter never re-enters. Even where re-embedding a duplicate would occur, the delta is orders of magnitude below any clustering threshold and cannot move a cluster boundary.

---

§3 — Scenario comparison

Dimension Scenario 1 — embed unified, filter, cluster Scenario 2 — split, then embed
Embedding value per post Identical (per-post independent) Identical (per-post independent)
Clusters on the same slice Identical (HDBSCAN deterministic on fixed points/params) Identical
Embedding quality No difference No difference
Embeds per post Exactly once (canonical) Once per file the post lands in — duplicates embed N times
Token cost Baseline Same, unless a post appears in multiple split files → wasted spend
Vector consistency / provenance One canonical vector, one run, one provenance Risk of N near-identical vectors for one post across files
pgvector mapping Clean: one table, WHERE platform=… AND locale=… Risk of siloed / redundant storage
Corpus-wide pre-clustering ops (e.g. §5.1 template detector) Available — runs on full corpus before the split Foreclosed at ingestion
Cross-lens work (unclassified audit) Available Foreclosed
Clustering-granularity flexibility Choose at clustering time (incl. not splitting) Hard-coded at ingestion
Auditability Higher (single embed run, single model-identity stamp) Lower (multiple runs, drift surface)

---

§4 — Why we chose Scenario 1

Because embedding quality is identical, the decision rests entirely on the right-hand consequences above:

  1. Embed-once is cost-safe and consistent. One canonical vector per post; no duplicate-embedding spend; no two-slightly-different-vectors-for-one-post problem.
  2. It maps cleanly onto pgvector and onto the existing model_id/model_version provenance stamping.
  3. It preserves optionality. Splitting before embedding bakes the segmentation into ingestion and forecloses anything that needs the whole corpus. Embedding once keeps the door open to: - the §5.1 template detector running corpus-wide before the platform/locale filter, and - the unclassified cross-lens diagnostic.
  4. It is more auditable — one embed run, one model-identity stamp — which fits the audit-first / verify-model-identity discipline.
VERDICT — The embed-where question carries no quality cost

Picking Scenario 1 over Scenario 2 wins on cost-safety, dedup, provenance, pgvector fit, and optionality, and loses nothing on embedding or clustering quality.

---

§5 — The decision splits into two independent questions — keep them separate

This is the most important framing to preserve in the docsite, because it is easy to conflate.

# Question What we decided Quality consequence
Q1 Where do we embed? Embed the complete corpus once, then filter None — vectors identical either way
Q2 What do we cluster — segmented slices or the unified corpus? Filter by platform and locale, then cluster each slice Real — see §6

Q1 is settled with no trade-offs. Q2 is the actual quality lever and is

orthogonal to Q1. The chosen path answers Q2 with segmented clustering (per platform × locale). That is a legitimate choice, but it is Pipeline B's segmentation philosophy applied on new axes, not Pipeline C's native unified-corpus clustering — and it brings the quality implications in §6.

---

§6 — Quality implications of the chosen clustering topology

Filtering by platform and locale before HDBSCAN produces smaller point sets per slice, which interacts with the absolute clustering floors (min_cluster_size=35, min_samples=10). Watch for:

  • Sub-floor micro-story dropout. A topic that clears the density floor in the unified corpus can fall below it once sliced into a thin PS/FB-only subset, and vanish as noise.
  • Structural splitting of cross-platform running stories. A story that spans FB and T is split at the platform boundary before clustering ever sees it, rather than being fused and then analyzed.
  • Reportability shifts. A cluster that would be reportable in the unified corpus may not reach reportable shape inside a single thin slice.
VERDICT — Embedding cost: zero; clustering topology: a real lever

The chosen path imposes no embedding-quality penalty. Any quality movement comes from the decision to segment before clustering, not from embedding once. This lever should be validated on real data before it is treated as settled (see §8).

---

§7 — Things to always consider / watch list

  • Embedding is deterministic for every practical purpose. Treat a post's vector as a fixed function of its text. Do not attribute any clustering change to "which file it was in."
  • Sub-noise jitter is call-level, never corpus-level. If two vectors for the "same" post differ, the cause is a second API call, not the corpus.
  • One canonical vector per post. Guard against the same post being embedded in more than one file/run; dedup before/at embedding, not after.
  • Absolute floors bite harder on thin slices. Any change to slice granularity (adding an axis, narrowing locale) shrinks point sets and interacts with min_cluster_size / min_samples. Re-check floors when granularity changes.
  • Cross-platform / cross-locale stories. Segmented clustering cannot fuse across a boundary it split on. Decide deliberately which boundaries are analytically meaningful to split on.
  • Preserve corpus-wide pre-clustering hooks. Run the §5.1 template detector (and any future corpus-global filter) on the full corpus before the platform/locale filter, while the unified vector set still exists.
  • Model identity is canonical. text-embedding-3-small, not BGE-M3. Verify model identity before any cost or architecture decision; stamp model_id/model_version on persisted vectors.
  • No UMAP in production. HDBSCAN runs directly on L2-normalized 1536-dim vectors; UMAP exists only in the offline evaluation harness.

---

§8 — Open / follow-up items

  • Validate Q2 (segmentation lever) on evidence, not intuition. Recommended read-only diagnostic: embed one day once, then cluster the same day three ways — fully unified / locale-only split / locale × platform split — and compare on cluster count, noise %, and known failure modes (platform-split running stories, sub-floor micro-stories). Decide the topology on the result.
  • Confirm template-detector placement. If §5.1 ships, fix its position as corpus-wide, before the platform/locale filter.
  • No change required to the embedding stage itself beyond the standing embed-once + persist-to-pgvector behavior already in place.

---

Appendix A — Canonical constants referenced

  • Embedding model: text-embedding-3-small (OpenAI), 1536-dim. Not BGE-M3.
  • Vector handling: L2-normalized per-vector; HDBSCAN direct (no UMAP in production).
  • HDBSCAN (healthy-day targets): min_cluster_size=35, min_samples=10, cluster_selection_epsilon=0.0. Production env-var pins may differ.
  • LLM: gpt-4.1-mini.
  • Storage: pgvector / PostgreSQL; vectors stamped with model_id / model_version.
  • Domains: seven canonical domains + unclassified sentinel (cross-lens, first-class).
  • Terminology: Pipeline B = segmented legacy; Pipeline C = unified corpus.

---

*This record is a faithful summary of the working session that produced the decision. The embedding-independence finding is a property of the model and the endpoint, not a measurement taken during the session; the ~1e-6–1e-7 jitter figure describes known endpoint behavior, not a value benchmarked here.*

21 / 28 · Embedding Topology Decision Source: pipeline-c-embedding-corpus-decision.md · Status: Decided · ADR-style record
22 / Data & Quality

Client App Spec — Pipeline C Reports Page.

The canonical wire-contract reference. Lists every field, every endpoint, every header, and the rendering rules a client-app engineer (or LLM acting as one) needs to build the Pipeline C daily reports page. No field is summarised away. Companion to §19 Pipeline C Architecture (server-side) and §16 Data Inventory (field-level reality). Source: client-pipeline-c-spec.md.

Audience. A client-app engineer (or an LLM acting as one) who has to build a page that lists and renders the Pipeline C daily reports this project ships. This spec is self-contained: it lists every wire field, every endpoint, every header, and the rendering rules. No field is summarised away. If something is missing from here, treat it as out of scope — do not invent it. Source of truth this spec was lifted from (in this repo): - webhook.py: - build_unified_report_dict (~lines 880–1048) — Pipeline C report dict, BEFORE wire serialisation. - _unified_topic_to_segmented_pair (~lines 674–879) — Pipeline C per-topic dict, BEFORE wire serialisation. - build_webhook_payload (~lines 393–458) — the actual envelope put on the wire. Picks a curated subset of keys from the dict above. - _merge_topic (~lines 142–227) — the actual per-topic shape put on the wire. Merges the AR + EN segmented-pair dicts into a single dict with bilingual field / field_en pairs and drops any key not explicitly listed. - send_report_webhook (~lines 491–650) — transport, retries, webhook_deliveries logging. - docs/pipeline-c-client-schema-parity.md, docs/pipeline-c-flow.md, docs/unified-pipeline.md, docs/webhook-payload-budget.md. - api.py — read-side endpoints. Critical pre-flight. The wire shape is not the same as the internal build_unified_report_dict output. The internal dict carries topics_ar / topics_en, pipeline, pipeline_run_status, total_topic_count, reportable_topic_count, preview_includes_unreportable, factuality_summary, plus several per-topic Pipeline-C-only identity fields (domain_primary, momentum, is_carried_forward, topic_stable_id, source_influence_tiers, actors_analysis, sentiment_analysis, total_reactions / total_shares / total_comments / total_views). None of those keys are forwarded by build_webhook_payload or _merge_topic to the client today. Only the keys listed in §2 and §3 actually arrive on the wire. This spec documents the wire truth; the dropped internal fields are listed in §3.4 so a future task can ship them additively without confusion. Forward-only contract. The wire payload is additive. New keys may appear in any release; the page MUST tolerate unknown keys without erroring, and MUST treat any missing additive key as null / "" / [] / {} (per its documented type) without erroring.

---

1. Transport

The only canonical channel for delivering Pipeline C reports to the client app is the webhook push described in §1.1. There is no public pull endpoint that returns the Pipeline C envelope — the read-side /api/reports/* endpoints operate on the segmented (Pipeline B) reports table, while Pipeline C runs live in a separate unified_reports table whose wire shape is built on the fly by webhook.build_unified_report_dict → build_webhook_payload and dispatched through the webhook (and the admin redeliver endpoint).

This means the client MUST persist every received envelope, because there is no way to refetch the same envelope shape later.

1.1 Webhook push (the only canonical channel)
POST {CLIENT_APP_URL}/api/receive-report Headers: x-api-key: <WEBHOOK_API_KEY> # shared secret, same value on both sides Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8 Body: <envelope JSON, see §2>

Exact URL assembly: f"{CLIENT_APP_URL.rstrip('/')}/api/receive-report" (webhook.py line ~548). The path /api/webhook/reports is not used by this project.

- Fired automatically on a Pipeline C run reaching succeeded or degraded status (send_unified_report_webhook → send_report_webhook). Pipeline B (segmented) reports use the same URL and headers on admin approval; Pipeline C reports are distinguished from Pipeline B by category == "unified-pipeline-c" (see §2). - Also fired on manual redelivery via the admin endpoint POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/redeliver (admin session required, not x-api-key). This hits the exact same path with the exact same payload, and reuses the same retry / delivery bookkeeping. - Idempotency. The same report may be re-delivered any number of times (operator-triggered backfill, retry after a transient failure, gap-fill via POST /api/reports/backfill-webhook). The client MUST dedupe on (id, status) — re-receiving the same id with the same status is expected and MUST be a no-op upsert (overwrite the stored copy in place, do not insert a duplicate row). - Sender retry behaviour. See webhook.send_report_webhook (webhook.py ~lines 491–650): - 1st attempt is synchronous; on 2xx the sender records success and stops. - Any non-2xx response (or network/connection error) triggers background retries up to MAX_RETRIES total attempts with RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS between them — including 4xx. The only exception is HTTP 413 (see next bullet). - HTTP 413 Request Entity Too Large is treated as permanent: the sender does not retry (retrying the same bytes is guaranteed to fail). The 413 is recorded with the exact payload_bytes in the admin webhook-history view. Operators replay after a fix via POST /api/reports/backfill-webhook. The client should keep its reverse-proxy body-size limit ≥ 1 MB to stay clear of the sender's ~950 KB soft budget. - TLS / payload-size quirks. - Soft payload budget on the sender is WEBHOOK_PAYLOAD_BUDGET_BYTES (default 972 800 bytes = 950 KB); the trim caps per-topic posts (formerly example_posts) to WEBHOOK_EXAMPLE_POSTS_PER_TOPIC (default 5) and strips the duplicate content field. The per-topic visualization object is dropped from the wire entirely. - The sender skips TLS certificate verification when CLIENT_APP_URL resolves to a *.replit.app host (intermediate cert-chain quirk on Replit deployments). Production clients on a custom domain get full TLS verification.

1.2 Read-side endpoints (Pipeline B only — do NOT use for Pipeline C envelope retrieval)

The /api/reports/* family of endpoints exists for the segmented (Pipeline B) pipeline. They are listed here only so a client implementer does not waste time trying to retrieve Pipeline C data through them. Their response shape comes from api.format_report_response (api.py line ~11760) and is not identical to the webhook envelope — it carries a different per-topic shape, uses report_date instead of reportDate, and includes legacy keys (visualization, admin_edited, detailed_analysis) that the webhook builder strips.

Method Path Auth What it returns Pipeline C support?
GET /api/reports/approved x-api-key or admin Bearer (require_admin_or_api_key) Approved Pipeline B reports, optionally filtered by date=YYYY-MM-DD or days=N No — Pipeline C runs are not stored in the reports table
GET /api/reports/date/{date} public for approved_only=true (default); admin session required to set approved_only=false Approved Pipeline B reports for a single date No
GET /api/reports/date/{date}/latest public Latest approved Pipeline B report per category for a single date No
GET /api/reports/{report_id} public for approved_only=true (default); admin session required for drafts One Pipeline B report by id No
GET /api/client/reports x-api-key required Lightweight discovery list: {reports: [{id, reportDate, category, categoryName, category_name_ar, approved_at}, …], total} over Pipeline B approved reports No for envelope; useful only as a Pipeline B index
GET /api/client/reports/daily-summary x-api-key required {date, unique_posts_by_platform: {platform: int, …}, total_unique_posts: int} sourced from raw_posts for a single date N/A — not a report envelope, but useful as a non-inflated per-platform daily headline number; safe to call regardless of pipeline

Notes:

- x-api-key value is whatever the server has in its WEBHOOK_API_KEY env var; same secret as the push direction. - The only operational way to ask the server to re-emit a specific Pipeline C envelope is the admin redeliver endpoint POST /api/admin/pipeline-c/runs/{report_id}/redeliver (admin session) or a date-range replay via POST /api/reports/backfill-webhook. Both push to the same webhook URL — the client receives them through the normal §1.1 path.

---

2. Envelope schema (top-level wire fields)

Source of truth: webhook.build_webhook_payload (webhook.py ~lines 393–458). The keys below are the exact set the wire emits. Anything not in this table is NOT on the wire today, even if it appears inside build_unified_report_dict's internal dict.

Field Type Source (in build_webhook_payload) Example What the page should do with it
id string report.id (= unified_reports.report_id) "unified-2026-05-12-deadbeef" Primary key. Idempotency key on receive. Show as small mono caption on the report detail view.
reportDate string YYYY-MM-DD report.report_date[:10] "2026-05-12" Primary sort key. Note: the webhook envelope uses reportDate (camelCase); the read-side Pipeline B response uses report_date. Pipeline C only ships reportDate.
category string report.category (= constant UNIFIED_CATEGORY_KEY) "unified-pipeline-c" Filter on this to scope the page to Pipeline C. Pipeline B reports use different category keys (e.g. "PS_Media").
categoryName string report.category_name (= UNIFIED_CATEGORY_NAME_EN) "Unified Daily Trends (Pipeline C)" EN page title fallback.
category_name_ar string report.category_name_ar (= UNIFIED_CATEGORY_NAME_AR) "الاتجاهات اليومية الموحدة" AR page title fallback.
title string report.category_name_ar (with EN fallback) "الاتجاهات اليومية الموحدة" AR page title — preferred over category_name_ar.
title_en string report.category_name (with AR fallback) "Unified Daily Trends (Pipeline C)" EN page title — preferred over categoryName.
overallNarrative string report.executive_summary_ar (fallback: executive_summary) "…" (Arabic prose) AR day-level executive summary. Render at the top of the AR view. May be "".
overallNarrativeEn string report.executive_summary_en "…" (English prose) EN day-level executive summary. May be "".
total_posts int report.total_posts 24667 Headline stat tile ("Posts analysed").
